source.messages:
Incident Bot: :rotating_light: Paging alert: perf-canary regression storm detected (dedupe disabled due to unique labels)

- Signal: `perf_canary_p95_latency_ms` + `perf_canary_tokens_per_sec`
- Affected: 27 alert instances across models/regions/SKU in last 5m
- Example: `model=llama-3-70b-instruct` `region=us-east` `sku=h100-80g` p95 +18% vs baseline (n=62)
- Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/SRE/Runbook%3A+Perf+Regression+Canary+Alert
- Dashboard: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/perf-canary/perf-canary?var-region=us-east

Thread created for incident coordination. Please acknowledge.

Rafael Mendes: ack. seeing my pager light up like a christmas tree. is this real or canary gone wild?

Sean Gallagher: ack. looking at incidents channel, multiple pages within 2 mins. treating as alerting failure until proven otherwise.

Noah Patel: ack. I’m on. pulling canary logs + baseline store status.

Grafana: Alert state change: 11 firing -> 19 firing (rule: perf-canary-p95-regression-page)

Allison Grant: ack. can someone confirm whether this is *paging* rules only or also warnings? (did we just flip paging in more dims?)

Rafael Mendes: looks like paging rules. warnings also firing but those go to #eng-runtime normally.

Sofia Ivanova: ack. checking scheduler metrics on one of the “regressed” models. initial glance: queueing time flat-ish, but query shows lots of new label combos.

Logan Wright: ack. I’m suspicious of cardinality change. we had a schema review earlier this week.

Nadia Rahman: ack. baseline job ran ~30m ago (I see artifact upload). might’ve rolled baseline version?

Omar El-Sayed: ack. checking runtime tag emission; we added kernel tags recently. could be exploding labelset.

Vanessa Ortiz: I can check if any fleet change happened last hour (sku reshuffle / driver). but the simultaneity across regions smells config/telemetry.

Rafael Mendes: immediate goal: stop paging noise. can we disable paging rules or canary eval while we triage?

Sean Gallagher: +1. per runbook we can:
1) set incident-bot dedupe key override
2) disable paging rules in observability-pack
3) lower canary QPS to near-zero if needed

Noah Patel: I can flip canary `capacity_budget_pct` down to 0.05% via control plane if needed, but pages are already happening due to alerts on existing metrics.

Allison Grant: let’s first mute paging rule globally for 60m. Rafael can you do silence?

Rafael Mendes: doing silence now in Alertmanager for `perf-canary-*page*` rules. ETA 2 min.

Grafana: Alert silenced by Rafael Mendes (duration: 1h, matcher: alertname=~"perf-canary-.*page.*")

Rafael Mendes: silence in place. still need root cause + prevent recurrence.

Noah Patel: pulled one alert payload from incident-bot. it includes `runtime_build_id` and `kernel_id` labels now.

```
labels:
  model: llama-3-70b-instruct
  model_version: 3.1.2
  region: us-east
  tier: hosted
  sku: h100-80g
  runtime_build_id: runtime-2026.02.02-rc3
  kernel_id: flashattn_v3_sm90_seq128k_batchdyn
  kernel_family: flashattn_v3
  prompt_bucket: p90
  canary_profile: default
```

That’s… a lot.

Logan Wright: yeah that’s the issue. if alert grouping includes kernel_id + prompt_bucket, you’ll get 100s of unique series, each with small n, baseline mismatch, and dedupe breaks.

Sofia Ivanova: also prompt_bucket being a label is super risky. any drift in distribution -> new time series -> baseline “missing” -> evaluator might be falling back to something dumb.

Nadia Rahman: confirming: baseline store key is `model + model_version + sku (+region optional)`.
If canary emits metrics with extra labels but baseline lookup doesn’t strip them, you get “baseline not found” -> and evaluator could treat baseline=0 or default baseline.

Noah Patel: digging in canary evaluator code: baseline fetch uses the full labelset for keying unless `baseline_key_labels` config is set. I think it’s currently unset in prod. :facepalm:

Allison Grant: ok so this is config regression. why did it surface now?

Omar El-Sayed: runtime PR added `kernel_id` tag to telemetry last week; canary started emitting it yesterday in us-east; then today we enabled paging rules with “top models” and it blew up.

Caleb Johnson: also: ingestion delay in telemetry right now (prom remote-write lag ~3-4m). that can affect “min samples” logic if it’s based on wall clock.

Vanessa Ortiz: fleet: no major change. driver update is scheduled tomorrow. nothing that would instantly hit all regions.

Rafael Mendes: action items right now:
1) fix the alert grouping / label set
2) fix canary baseline lookup to ignore high-card labels
3) re-enable paging when stable

Noah Patel: I can hotfix canary config to set `baseline_key_labels=[model,model_version,region,sku,tier]` and also set metrics emission allowlist.
But that’s a deploy.

Connor O'Brien: if you want, I can coordinate with releases to fast-track canary config change (control plane) vs full deploy.
What is adjustable without redeploy?

Noah Patel: baseline_key_labels is config in control plane (no code change). metric labels are code-side; but we can disable extra labels by setting `emit_kernel_labels=false` in canary config (also control plane).

Logan Wright: for long-term: telemetry should still carry kernel_id, but alerts shouldn’t group on it. dashboards can show it as a breakdown via exemplars or logs.

Allison Grant: yes. policy: paging alerts must not include high-cardinality labels in the alert identity. at most model/version/region/tier.

Sean Gallagher: +1. and `prompt_bucket` should never be in paging series. it’s a dimension for analysis, not alerting.

Noah Patel: making config changes now:
- `emit_kernel_labels=false`
- `emit_prompt_bucket_label=false`
- `baseline_key_labels=model,model_version,region,sku,tier`
- increase `min_samples_paging` from 50 -> 200 (temporary)

Will apply to us-east first.

Connor O'Brien: ok. once applied, we should watch warning rules (not silenced) to see if they settle.

Grafana: Alert state change: 19 firing -> 6 firing (rule: perf-canary-p95-regression-warn)

Sofia Ivanova: I’m seeing the number of series drop in Grafana. good sign. queueing/decode lat look normal-ish now.

Nadia Rahman: baseline fetch logs from canary before:
`baseline_lookup_miss rate=0.78`
Now after config change:
`baseline_lookup_miss rate=0.03`

That was the storm.

Rafael Mendes: can we keep paging silenced until end of day and then re-enable after we confirm stability for a couple hours?

Sean Gallagher: yes. also: we need a writeup. this is essentially an incident caused by alerting/config, not perf regression.

Allison Grant: agreed. Sev? I’d call it Sev3 internal (oncall disruption) with follow-ups.

Rafael Mendes: logging as internal incident, Sev3. primary impact: oncall noise + risk of missing real pages.

Logan Wright: we should also add a guardrail: metrics-cardinality audit in CI for canary changes.

Caleb Johnson: and add an alert on cardinality itself. like “number of active series for perf_canary_* > threshold”.

Omar El-Sayed: +1. plus explicit “do not include in baseline key” list for labels: `kernel_id`, `prompt_bucket`, `request_id` (lol), etc.

Noah Patel: I’ll open a follow-up ticket to make baseline lookup *always* use explicit key labels with safe defaults. right now it’s too footgun-y.

Vanessa Ortiz: any chance this impacted customer traffic? canary overhead maybe spiked due to fanout?

Noah Patel: canary QPS stayed under cap; overhead ~0.3% per region. no evidence of inference impact. this was alerting noise, not load.

Sean Gallagher: ok. next steps in-thread:
- keep paging silenced 1h
- confirm series count stable
- write postmortem + update alert policy doc

Allison Grant: I’ll update the alerting policy doc: “paging series identity must be <= (model, model_version, region, tier)” and require min n.

Rafael Mendes: I’ll add a note to oncall training + runbook: if you see many pages with unique label combos, check label explosion + silence paging rule.

Noah Patel: update: rolled config to eu-west + us-west as well. baseline miss rate now low across regions.

Grafana: Alert state change: 6 firing -> 1 firing (rule: perf-canary-p95-regression-warn)

Sofia Ivanova: remaining warning is llama-3-8b in eu-west with p95 +9%. likely real-ish but warning-level only. can follow up later.

Nadia Rahman: baseline drift is another issue: baseline artifacts updated earlier today with different sample window (traffic mix changed). we should talk about rolling vs fixed baseline in ADR.

Logan Wright: yep. but this incident was more “baseline key mismatch” than drift. still, drift makes it worse and increases fire rate.

Sean Gallagher: can someone link the baseline drift thread? I remember we discussed anti-flap + smoothing.

Nadia Rahman: thread: https://slack.redwood.ai/archives/eng-runtime/p1768604412-baseline-drift

Allison Grant: we should capture both in postmortem:
- primary: label/cardinality + baseline-key mismatch
- contributing: baseline update + ingestion lag
- mitigation: silence paging, reduce labels, set baseline_key_labels
- prevention: guardrails + ADR + templates

Rafael Mendes: I’ll create postmortem doc and assign owners. Noah can you be DRI for tech fixes? Allison for policy? Logan/Nadia for telemetry/baseline sections.

Noah Patel: yep.

Logan Wright: yes.

Nadia Rahman: yes.

Allison Grant: yes.

Incident Bot: Status update requested. Please provide current status and next update ETA.

Rafael Mendes: Status: paging silenced, storm stopped after canary config change to remove high-card labels + correct baseline key. Monitoring warnings for 2h. Next update in 60m; plan to re-enable paging tomorrow after rule/template changes.

Connor O'Brien: note for releases: we should block any further paging enablement until the alert rule grouping is updated in observability-pack. I’ll coordinate.

Sean Gallagher: ok closing the loop: once postmortem is drafted, we’ll un-silence paging in a controlled window with one model/region.

Rafael Mendes: +1. leaving thread open until postmortem link is live.

Allison Grant: quick PR idea: in alert rule, add `sum by (model, model_version, region, tier)` *before* evaluating regression. even if extra labels exist, they won’t affect alert identity.

Caleb Johnson: that would help a lot. and maybe enforce via recording rule.

Noah Patel: agreed. ok I’m off to implement guardrail + open ENG tickets.

Rafael Mendes: ok. I’ll post postmortem link here once created.

Rafael Mendes: Postmortem doc stub: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/ONCALL/Postmortem%3A+Perf+Canary+False+Positive+Alert+Storm (please add details + action items)

Incident Bot: :white_check_mark: Incident marked mitigated by Rafael Mendes (Sev3). Reminder: add follow-ups in Linear and link here when filed.

`baseline_lookup_miss rate=0.03`

That was the storm.

Rafael Mendes: can we keep paging silenced until end of day and then re-enable after we confirm stability for a couple hours?

Sean Gallagher: yes. also: we need a writeup. this is essentially an incident caused by alerting/config, not perf regression.

Allison Grant: agreed. Sev? I’d call it Sev3 internal (oncall disruption) with follow-ups.

Rafael Mendes: logging as internal incident, Sev3. primary impact: oncall noise + risk of missing real pages.

Logan Wright: we should also add a guardrail: metrics-cardinality audit in CI for canary changes.

Caleb Johnson: and add an alert on cardinality itself. like “number of active series for perf_canary_* > threshold”.

Omar El-Sayed: +1. plus explicit “do not include in baseline key” list for labels: `kernel_id`, `prompt_bucket`, `request_id` (lol), etc.

Noah Patel: I’ll open a follow-up ticket to make baseline lookup *always* use explicit key labels with safe defaults. right now it’s too footgun-y.

Vanessa Ortiz: any chance this impacted customer traffic? canary overhead maybe spiked due to fanout?

Noah Patel: canary QPS stayed under cap; overhead ~0.3% per region. no evidence of inference impact. this was alerting noise, not load.

Sean Gallagher: ok. next steps in-thread:
- keep paging silenced 1h
- confirm series count stable
- write postmortem + update alert policy doc

Allison Grant: I’ll update the alerting policy doc: “paging series identity must be <= (model, model_version, region, tier)” and require min n.

Rafael Mendes: I’ll add a note to oncall training + runbook: if you see many pages with unique label combos, check label explosion + silence paging rule.

Noah Patel: update: rolled config to eu-west + us-west as well. baseline miss rate now low across regions.

Grafana: Alert state change: 6 firing -> 1 firing (rule: perf-canary-p95-regression-warn)

Sofia Ivanova: remaining warning is llama-3-8b in eu-west with p95 +9%. likely real-ish but warning-level only. can follow up later.

Nadia Rahman: baseline drift is another issue: baseline artifacts updated earlier today with different sample window (traffic mix changed). we should talk about rolling vs fixed baseline in ADR.

Logan Wright: yep. but this incident was more “baseline key mismatch” than drift. still, drift makes it worse and increases fire rate.

Sean Gallagher: can someone link the baseline drift thread? I remember we discussed anti-flap + smoothing.

Nadia Rahman: thread: https://slack.redwood.ai/archives/eng-runtime/p1768604412-baseline-drift

Allison Grant: we should capture both in postmortem:
- primary: label/cardinality + baseline-key mismatch
- contributing: baseline update + ingestion lag
- mitigation: silence paging, reduce labels, set baseline_key_labels
- prevention: guardrails + ADR + templates

Rafael Mendes: I’ll create postmortem doc and assign owners. Noah can you be DRI for tech fixes? Allison for policy? Logan/Nadia for telemetry/baseline sections.

Noah Patel: yep.

Logan Wright: yes.

Nadia Rahman: yes.

Allison Grant: yes.

Incident Bot: Status update requested. Please provide current status and next update ETA.

Rafael Mendes: Status: paging silenced, storm stopped after canary config change to remove high-card labels + correct baseline key. Monitoring warnings for 2h. Next update in 60m; plan to re-enable paging tomorrow after rule/template changes.

Connor O'Brien: note for releases: we should block any further paging enablement until the alert rule grouping is updated in observability-pack. I’ll coordinate.

Sean Gallagher: ok closing the loop: once postmortem is drafted, we’ll un-silence paging in a controlled window with one model/region.

Rafael Mendes: +1. leaving thread open until postmortem link is live.

Allison Grant: quick PR idea: in alert rule, add `sum by (model, model_version, region, tier)` *before* evaluating regression. even if extra labels exist, they won’t affect alert identity.

Caleb Johnson: that would help a lot. and maybe enforce via recording rule.

Noah Patel: agreed. ok I’m off to implement guardrail + open ENG tickets.

Rafael Mendes: ok. I’ll post postmortem link here once created.

Rafael Mendes: Postmortem doc stub: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/ONCALL/Postmortem%3A+Perf+Canary+False+Positive+Alert+Storm (please add details + action items)

Incident Bot: :white_check_mark: Incident marked mitigated by Rafael Mendes (Sev3). Reminder: add follow-ups in Linear and link here when filed.

source.thread:
IncidentBot: :rotating_light: Alert triggered: Hosted canary / runtime-1.21 / kernel_autoselect_v2
Signal: p99 latency +42% (baseline 1.9s -> 2.7s) for chat.generate
Scope (initial): us-east-1, canary pool "hosted-canary-rt121", GPU=L40S
Error rate: stable
Start: 12:03 PT (auto)
Runbook: https://confluence.redwood/eng-serving-runtime/runbooks/kernel-selection-v2-override-and-rollback-runbook

Sean Gallagher: oncall here. Seeing the spike on the canary dashboard too. It’s narrow but pretty sharp. Anyone around from runtime kernels/selector?

Omar El-Sayed: here. Looking at selection telemetry panels now (sampling 1%). It looks like selection distribution shifted specifically on L40S: prefill kernel choice is skewing to `prefill_fa2_packed_qkv` (which we normally avoid on this SKU for long context).

Aisha Bello: confirming from SLO view: only p99 is affected; p95 is up ~8%. No corresponding increase in 5xx. Tail looks like long-context requests.

Allison Grant: +1. Alert threshold tripped on p99 only. No error-budget burn yet but we should mitigate before the canary window continues.

Connor O'Brien: release train perspective: canary is supposed to run for another 3 hours. If we need to flip gate we can, but let’s keep it scoped if possible.

Yuki Sato: I’m in. That kernel shouldn’t be getting picked on L40S unless we’re in the short-prefill bucket. Do we know what seq bucket the selector thinks these are?

Omar El-Sayed: looking at the event fields: `seq_bucket=prefill_short` on a bunch of these, but request traces show prompt_tokens ~14k+ on the slow ones. Smells like misclassification / bad token count estimate.

Marcus Reed: I can cross-check with perf-canary scenario logs. In canary, we enabled scheduler hint passthrough last week—could be feeding the selector stale distribution hints?

Hiro Tanaka: L40S is sensitive to that packed QKV path at long context; it can blow up tail if it hits the wrong memory pattern. Agree this is likely wrong bucket or wrong SKU mapping.

Sean Gallagher: proposing immediate mitigation: disable v2 only for L40S in us-east-1 canary pool. That should snap latency back while we debug.

Benji Okafor: I can apply a deny rule in the gate config: `kernel_autoselect_v2=false` when gpu_sku=L40S AND env=hosted-canary AND region=use1. Hot reload should be <2 min.

Ethan Park: control plane gate supports that. Benji, please do it via the standard policy repo so we get an audit trail. Link the incident.

DeployBot: Change requested by Benji Okafor
Repo: control-plane-policy
Change: disable kernel_autoselect_v2 for gpu_sku=L40S region=us-east-1 env=hosted-canary-rt121
Status: pending

Sahana Iyer: while that rolls, I’m pulling selector debug dumps from a couple slow traces. If we see `prefill_short` + 14k prompts, it’s definitely feature extraction/hinting.

Yuki Sato: Omar/Marcus: can you confirm whether the selector is using request-level token counts or scheduler-provided `workload_hint` for bucket? In v2 we added “mixed workload stability” using observed distribution hints (PR-28502).

Marcus Reed: in hosted canary, I believe we enabled `selector.use_scheduler_hints=true` for that pool (for stability testing). If the hint says “mostly short prefill” it could bias bucket decisions.

Omar El-Sayed: yeah, field `hint_source=scheduler` shows up in the telemetry for most of these. That’s probably it.

Sean Gallagher: if that’s the case, mitigation (disable v2) is fine. We’ll need a follow-up: either lower weight of scheduler hints or cap hint influence when request prompt_tokens exceed threshold.

DeployBot: Change applied successfully
Status: live

Aisha Bello: watching p99 now. Already dropping—p99 back to ~2.0s within the last 3 min window. Looks like mitigation worked.

Allison Grant: alert should auto-resolve once the 10-min window clears. Please keep an eye on cardinality/telemetry; but this looks clean.

Sean Gallagher: great. Let’s keep canary running for non-L40S SKUs. Benji/Ethan, thanks. Omar/Yuki/Hiro/Sahana: can you own the RCA?

Yuki Sato: yep. Hypothesis: scheduler hint incorrectly biasing seq bucket selection for long-context requests; selector uses hint too aggressively. Action: add guardrail: if `prompt_tokens >= long_context_threshold`, force `prefill_long` regardless of hint. Also check if token count is coming in late/estimated.

Sahana Iyer: I have one debug dump showing:
- request prompt_tokens reported to selector: 1024 (estimated)
- actual prompt_tokens at execution: 14832
So we might be using pre-tokenization estimate (or truncated) in the selector feature extraction.

Hiro Tanaka: that matches older bug class: token count isn’t final until after tokenizer pass / prefill setup. If selector runs earlier, it sees a default/placeholder. We should rely on max of (scheduler hint + conservative upper bound) or delay selection until real token count is known.

Omar El-Sayed: we can also add a safety rule: if `prompt_bytes` is high (or input length in chars), treat as long context. Not perfect, but better than misclassifying to short.

Connor O'Brien: for release notes / comms: this is canary-only and mitigated by gating. Please document that L40S in us-east-1 canary had v2 disabled pending fix. We’ll want to mention “scheduler hint integration guardrail” as a patch.

Sean Gallagher: incident summary for the thread:
- Impact: brief p99 latency spike in hosted canary pool on L40S only
- Mitigation: disabled kernel_autoselect_v2 for L40S us-east-1 canary via gate (hot reload)
- Status: recovered
Next: RCA + patch, then re-enable and re-run canary.

IncidentBot: :white_check_mark: Alert resolved: p99 latency returned to baseline for chat.generate (hosted canary / us-east-1 / L40S)
Duration: 18 minutes
Owner: Sean Gallagher

Allison Grant: please file a follow-up ticket and link here; also add an explicit rollback trigger note to the canary checklist (“if selector sees estimated tokens, force conservative bucket”).

Omar El-Sayed: will do. I’ll also add a temporary dashboard annotation so future viewers know why L40S selection distribution changes abruptly at ~12:12 PT.

Benji Okafor: gate change is scoped + reversible. I’ll leave it in place until runtime confirms fix.

Ethan Park: +1. Make sure we capture the exact policy condition; we’ve had SKU normalization issues ("L40S" vs "NVIDIA-L40S" vs instance family) in the past.

Sean Gallagher: closing out. Thanks all.

source.messages:
Jordan Blake: FYI I ran a recurrence clustering pass for May

ug (30
-day windows). Prioritized groups by SLA hits and customer escalations. Short summary + suggested owners below. Full notebook: https://redwood-internal/notes/recurrence-synthesis-2026-05-aug

Tara Venkatar: nice, can you paste the clusters + basic counts so we can scope lift?

Jordan Blake: sure 

```
cluster_key | incidents | sla_violations | cust_escalations
KV_HOT_TENANT_THRASH | 21 | 8 | 3
FALLBACK_ROLLOVER_OSC | 15 | 6 | 2
GRPC_CONN_RESET_OLD_IMAGE | 12 | 4 | 1
AUTOSCALER_SUDDEN_SCALE_DOWN | 9 | 5 | 1
EGRESS_THROTTLE_THIRD_PARTY | 6 | 3 | 2
OTHER | 10 | 2 | 0
```

Query: `-- clustering by signature + time-window + customer-impact score` (see notebook). I also computed a recurrence half-life metric per cluster to know which will decay vs persist. KV_HOT_TENANT_THRASH has the longest tail.

Ethan Zhao: i'll take KV_HOT_TENANT_THRASH. plan: (1) per-tenant eviction caps + soft isolation, (2) adaptive admission for hot prefixes, (3) eviction latency SLOs + dashboard. Risk: Dedicated p95 might shift; will add feature-flag and 1-click revert. ETA: 3 weeks (2026-09-08).

Rosa Alvarez: i'll own FALLBACK_ROLLOVER_OSC. hypothesis: simultaneous pool degradation + aggressive rollback policy causes flip-flop. Action: add rollout cooldowns, per-pool fallback hysteresis, and a console gate that pauses rollouts above a fallback threshold. Also adding an eval suite for semantic drift during canary. ETA: 2 weeks (2026-08-25).

Tara Venkatar: for GRPC_CONN_RESET_OLD_IMAGE i can coordinate reprovisioning and a keepalive bump. will tag eng-infra and schedule a node-image retirement plan. ETA: 10 days.

Jordan Blake: I'll create epic INC-4722 and seed tickets. incidents-bot, please create INC-4722 and tag recurrence-analysis + priority-high.

incidents-bot: Created epic INC-4722: "Recurrence synthesis: May-Aug followups" and added labels: recurrence-analysis, priority-high. https://jira.local/browse/INC-4722

Ethan Zhao: action items to add to INC-4722:
- Ethan Zhao 
V: per-tenant eviction caps + admission control PR + load tests + rollback docs 

 due 2026-09-08
- Rosa Alvarez 

 fallback cooldowns + canary gate + eval prompts 

 due 2026-08-25
- Tara Venkatar 

 grpc keepalive bump + node-image retirement plan 

 due 2026-08-30
- Platform SRE (assign: @ops-oncall) 

 autoscaler safety nets + alert tuning 

 due 2026-09-01
- Integrations team 

 investigate third-party egress throttles + backoff guidance 

 due 2026-08-22

Rosa Alvarez: risk flags: fallback gating must avoid blocking urgent security rollouts. will add a bypass path with manual approval and audit log.

Ethan Zhao: for load tests i'll run 2x observed peak QPS and compare p50/p95/p99 and cache-hit ratio to baseline. will upload results to the epic.

Tara Venkatar: propose measurement plan for the sweep: baseline metrics (SLA violations/week, incidents/week, median MTTR), run weekly pulse check and report a decay curve. if recurrence count doesn't drop 50% in 6 weeks escalate to ops review.

Jordan Blake: good measurable goals. I'll add a dashboard tile for each cluster and a weekly automated summary email to incident-owners.

incidents-bot: Reminder scheduled: "Recurrence sync: INC-4722" on 2026-08-19 16:00 UTC.

Rosa Alvarez: let's meet Wed 2026-08-19 09:00 PT (16:00 UTC) 30m to lock owners and check initial blockers. Ping in this thread if anything needs to be moved earlier. :eyes:

source.text:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: *PERF CANARY PAGE* (Sev2)
Model: `llama-3-70b-instruct` (top model)
Region/Tier: `us-east / hosted`
Signal: sustained regression vs baseline
• p95 e2e latency: *+29.4%* (baseline 1.42s -> observed 1.84s)
• tokens/sec: *-17.8%* (baseline 5,950 -> observed 4,890)
Confidence: high (min samples met)
Window: last 12m (dampening passed)
Dash: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/perf-canary/model?var-model=llama-3-70b-instruct&var-region=us-east
Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/eng-sre/Runbook+-+Perf+Regression+Canary+Alert
Dedupe key: `perfcanary|llama-3-70b-instruct|us-east|p95`

Rafael Mendes: I’m on. Acking page. Can someone from runtime join? @Noah Patel @Sofia Ivanova

Sean Gallagher: Here. Let’s keep this thread tight + follow runbook. First question: is this canary-only or reflected in customer SLOs?

Aisha Bello: Checking customer-facing latency panels now. Quick look: p95 for hosted route for this model in us-east is up ~20-25% since ~15 mins ago. Not just canary.

Noah Patel: Joined. Looking at perf-canary details + runtime build tags. Do we have any deploys in last hour?

Vanessa Ortiz: Fleet side: there was a partial GPU host rotation in us-east hosted pool starting ~30 min ago (rack maintenance). But shouldn’t cause this magnitude unless we dropped to a slower SKU. I’m pulling SKU mix.

Allison Grant: Reminder: for top model sustained regression, Sev2 page is expected. Next: isolate *where* time is going (queueing vs runtime execution vs prefill/decode). Use the breakdown panels.

Sofia Ivanova: I’m in the dashboard. Breakdown:
- Queueing time p95 is basically flat.
- Runtime execution p95 is up.
- Decode p95 is up more than prefill.
Batch size distribution looks slightly smaller than baseline but not enough to explain -18% tps.

Omar El-Sayed: I can check runtime telemetry tags. Do we have `runtime_build_id` and `kernel_id` on the canary series for this window?

Noah Patel: Yep, looking at canary’s emitted labels. Observed series shows `runtime_build_id=rw-runtime-2026.01.22-rc3` and `kernel_id=attn_flash_v3_7`.
Baseline was `rw-runtime-2026.01.18` and `kernel_id=attn_flash_v3_6`.
So likely tied to kernel selection or runtime release.

Hiro Tanaka: Joining. `attn_flash_v3_7` was rolled to canary first yesterday but shouldn’t be in prod default yet. Did kernel auto-select flip on for this model?

Connor O'Brien: Release side: we started a phased rollout of `2026.01.22-rc3` to us-east hosted ~40 mins ago (10% -> 30%). No rollback triggered yet.

Sean Gallagher: So this page is probably the guardrail working. If customer SLO is impacted, we should consider immediate rollback/pause rollout.

Marissa Cole: +1 pause rollout. For comms: internal note to #eng-runtime and #eng-releases, no external comm unless we have confirmed customer impact beyond brief bump.

Rafael Mendes: Action items:
1) confirm scope (only us-east hosted? dedicated? other regions?)
2) decide rollback/pause
3) collect quick attribution hints for follow-up

Aisha Bello: Scope check: eu-west looks normal. us-west looks normal. Dedicated pools for this model look normal (but fewer canary samples). The spike is us-east hosted.

Vanessa Ortiz: SKU mix: we did not drop to slower GPUs. Still mostly H100s, some A100 spillover but that was true yesterday too. Nothing dramatic.

Sofia Ivanova: Scheduler signals stable. Queue flat. This points away from capacity/queueing and toward kernel/runtime.

Hiro Tanaka: If it’s `attn_flash_v3_7`, we can flip feature flag to force v3_6 for `llama-3-70b-instruct` in us-east while we debug. That’s faster than full runtime rollback.

Noah Patel: We also need to stop the runtime rollout to avoid spreading. Connor can you pause `2026.01.22-rc3` promotion in us-east?

Connor O'Brien: Can pause now. Doing it.

deploy-bot: Release pipeline update: *Paused* runtime rollout `rw-runtime-2026.01.22-rc3` in `us-east/hosted` at 30%.

Rafael Mendes: Great. Hiro can you force kernel v3_6? Omar/Noah confirm the flag name / change path.

Omar El-Sayed: Kernel override is via control-plane config `kernel_policy_override`. For emergency we can set `force_kernel_id=attn_flash_v3_6` scoped to (model, region, tier). Needs approval from runtime oncall (Noah) + audited change.

Hiro Tanaka: Do it. I’ll sign off. Suspect: v3_7 regression on decode for this model shape.

Noah Patel: Approved. Omar can you apply override scoped: model `llama-3-70b-instruct`, region `us-east`, tier `hosted`. TTL 6h.

Omar El-Sayed: On it.

Allison Grant: While that’s happening, someone capture the canary prompt bucket(s) that are triggering the regression (long decode vs short?). That’ll help RCA.

Sofia Ivanova: From canary breakdown by prompt bucket: biggest delta is `prompt_len=2k-4k` with `target_new_tokens=512`. Decode step time up ~35% vs baseline.

Omar El-Sayed: Override pushed. Waiting for propagation (~2-3 mins) + confirm telemetry flips back to v3_6.

Vanessa Ortiz: I’ll also check if any GPU driver update coincided. But timing aligns with runtime rc3.

Aisha Bello: Watching both canary and customer p95 panels. Still elevated but maybe starting to trend down.

Connor O'Brien: With rollout paused, only existing 30% is on rc3. If kernel override doesn’t fix, we should roll back those hosts to `2026.01.18`.

Sean Gallagher: Agree. Let’s set a 10 min checkpoint: if p95 doesn’t recover materially, rollback runtime.

Rafael Mendes: +1. Checkpoint at T+10.

Omar El-Sayed: Confirmed new canary samples: `kernel_id` label now shows `attn_flash_v3_6`. Good.

Noah Patel: Nice. Expect recovery soon if that’s the culprit.

Aisha Bello: Canary p95 e2e just dropped from 1.84s -> 1.55s over last few mins. Tokens/sec trending back up. Customer p95 also improving (still above baseline but moving).

Hiro Tanaka: That strongly implicates v3_7. I’ll open a kernel bug + revert the rollout plan for v3_7 until we root-cause.

Sofia Ivanova: Queue/batch metrics unchanged during recovery, so it’s not scheduler.

Allison Grant: Please paste a quick before/after snapshot for the incident log (baseline, peak, recovery). Keeps postmortem easy.

Rafael Mendes: Noted.

Aisha Bello: Snapshot:
- Baseline p95 e2e ~1.42s
- Peak ~1.86s (12m sustained)
- Now ~1.50-1.55s and falling
Tokens/sec:
- Baseline ~5.95k
- Low ~4.85k
- Now ~5.6k and rising

Connor O'Brien: Do we keep rc3 paused until we confirm no other regressions? I’d prefer keep paused + require canary green for 30m before resuming.

Marissa Cole: Product-wise: keep paused. Also please post in #eng-releases that kernel override is in place + rc3 paused pending investigation.

Noah Patel: I’ll send note after we stabilize.

Sean Gallagher: Any customer tickets yet?

Rafael Mendes: None in queue as of now (checking support channel). This seems caught early.

Omar El-Sayed: FYI: override TTL set 6 hours. We should either remove after kernel fix or keep until next runtime build that disables v3_7.

Hiro Tanaka: I’ll push a change to keep v3_7 disabled for this model until patched.

Rafael Mendes: T+10 checkpoint: looks like we’re recovering. Do we still need runtime rollback? I’m leaning no, since pause + kernel override fixed. But keep Sev2 open until p95 is within ~5-10% baseline.

Aisha Bello: Current p95 is ~1.46s (within ~3% of baseline) on canary. Customer p95 panel also near baseline.

Sean Gallagher: OK to resolve once it’s stable for another 10 mins. But keep rc3 paused.

Noah Patel: Agreed. Next steps:
- Keep kernel override
- Pause rollout stays
- Open follow-ups: kernel v3_7 regression on long decode bucket

Allison Grant: Also ensure we attach this incident to the perf-canary tracking ticket and add a short retro note.

Rafael Mendes: Will do.

Noah Patel: Posting comms now.

Noah Patel: (internal comm) Shared in #eng-runtime + #eng-releases: us-east hosted `llama-3-70b-instruct` p95 regression tied to `attn_flash_v3_7`; rollout paused; forced `attn_flash_v3_6` w/ TTL 6h; metrics recovered.

Hiro Tanaka: I’ll start a quick bisect on v3_7. Might be an alignment issue with that bucket. Will update.

Vanessa Ortiz: Fleet looks stable. No further action from infra.

Rafael Mendes: Marking incident as mitigated. Leaving thread open for 10 more mins to ensure no rebound.

Aisha Bello: 10-min stability check: p95 stable ~1.44-1.47s. tokens/sec back to baseline range. No new spikes.

Sean Gallagher: Good. Please close with summary + follow-ups.

Rafael Mendes: :white_check_mark: *Resolved* (mitigated)
Impact: us-east hosted `llama-3-70b-instruct` p95 latency +~30% and tokens/sec -~18% for ~15-20 mins.
Detection: perf-canary paging alert (Sev2) + corroborated in customer panels.
Mitigation: paused runtime rollout `rw-runtime-2026.01.22-rc3` in us-east; forced kernel override from `attn_flash_v3_7` -> `attn_flash_v3_6` scoped to model/region/tier (TTL 6h). Metrics returned to baseline.
Suspected root cause: kernel regression in `attn_flash_v3_7` affecting long-decode bucket (2k-4k prompt, 512 new tokens).
Follow-ups:
- Hiro to file kernel bug + disable/patch v3_7 for this model.
- Connor to keep rc3 paused until canary green 30m and kernel plan updated.
- Noah/Omar to confirm override removal plan and add incident note to perf-canary retro.

incident-bot: Incident updated: status=Resolved, severity=Sev2, owner=Rafael Mendes. Dedupe key closed: `perfcanary|llama-3-70b-instruct|us-east|p95`

source.content:
IncidentBot: :rotating_light: ALERT: `cost-model-drift` SLI breached in prod
- Signal: predicted vs observed cost drift (hourly)
- Current: 9.8% (threshold 5.0%)
- Scope: region=us-east-1, deployment=hosted
- Dashboard: https://grafana.redwood.internal/d/cost-model-drift
- Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.internal/eng-sre/runbooks/cost-model-api-incident-runbook

Rafael Mendes: ack. taking a look. anyone seeing other symptoms? (cost-model API latency/errors?)

Thomas Baird: cost-model API looks normal. p95 ~120ms, error rate flat. this is just the drift job firing.

Rafael Mendes: ok. pulling the drift job logs now.

Rafael Mendes: seeing `observed_cost_window` undercounting for the last 20-30m. matches a telemetry lag pattern more than actual cost divergence.

Logan Wright: yeah we had a brief backlog in token-metering ingestion. kafka consumer group in us-east fell behind (we saw ~17m lag peak). recovered now.

Nadia Rahman: +1. metering pipeline caught up but the drift job probably evaluated during the lag window. observed side was missing late-arriving usage.

Rafael Mendes: that would explain it. any data loss or just delay?

Logan Wright: delay only. no dropped partitions. offsets are moving again. we should be back to <60s lag.

Mei Lin: confirmed in the warehouse too. late arrivals are landing; hourly aggregates will “heal” but the drift check is too eager.

Allison Grant: do we have alert gating on telemetry lag? (i.e. don’t page drift if metering lag > X)

Thomas Baird: not today. drift monitor job uses a fixed 5m watermark. if lag > 5m, it will look like we’re under-attributing observed costs.

Ethan Park: makes sense. from the service side nothing changed. last deploy was yesterday.

Rafael Mendes: mitigation: I’m going to silence `cost-model-drift` paging for 2h and leave it as a dashboard-only alert until we add gating. :thumbsup:

IncidentBot: Rafael Mendes updated incident status: MITIGATING (silencing alert)

Rafael Mendes: silenced. can someone sanity check drift after lag clears? want to make sure it drops back below threshold once late arrivals are included.

Mei Lin: looking now. the last completed window is still “bad” but the next rollup should correct. will update in ~10m.

Logan Wright: I’ll also post the telemetry lag graph here: https://grafana.redwood.internal/d/token-metering-lag (peak 1020s at ~11:06)

Nadia Rahman: we should probably treat observed_cost as “final” only after >=30m lateness allowance. 5m was optimistic.

Thomas Baird: agreed. also: drift job currently compares per-hour by request tags; when ingestion lags, high-cardinality tags amplify the apparent drift. we could aggregate to a coarser key while lagging.

Rafael Mendes: action items:
1) add telemetry-lag gating + dynamic watermark to drift job (don’t alert if metering lag > N) (Logan/Mei?)
2) update drift SLI doc to explicitly define lateness window + “provisional” vs “final” (Allison + Rafael)
3) update runbook w/ “telemetry lag => drift false positive” + steps to confirm (Rafael)

Ethan Park: + one more: can we emit `telemetry_lag_seconds` as a metric alongside drift job output so dashboard can show “drift vs lag” on same panel? would make it obvious.

Allison Grant: +1. SRE friendly.

Logan Wright: I can take (1) + add the metric. might need a small change in the drift job to query lag from metering pipeline and pass through.

Mei Lin: I’ll help w/ the watermark / lateness side. we can also backtest on last 30 days to pick N (15m? 30m?) without masking real drift.

Rafael Mendes: perfect. once we confirm drift recovers, we can close.

Mei Lin: update: newest window just recomputed. drift now 1.9% (back under threshold). looks like pure lag.

Rafael Mendes: great. closing incident as false positive due to telemetry lag. leaving paging silenced until gating lands.

IncidentBot: Rafael Mendes updated incident status: RESOLVED

Rafael Mendes: follow-ups I’ll file:
- ENG: “Drift monitor: gate on telemetry lag + increase lateness window”
- DOC: “Runbook update: drift false positives during lag”

Logan Wright: cool. will coordinate with Nadia on what lag signal is canonical (kafka vs warehouse ingest vs token-metering service).

Nadia Rahman: thx. prefer kafka consumer lag as primary + a secondary “late arrivals rate” metric if we can.

Rafael Mendes: sounds good. please drop links to tickets here when created.

source.messages:
incident-bot: [WARN] perf-canary regression detected (warning tier)
• Model: `llama-3.1-70b-instruct` (model_version: `2026-01-15.1`)
• Region/Tier: `us-east` / `hosted`
• Window: last 20m (min_samples met)
• Observed vs baseline:
  - e2e p95 latency: 1.42s vs 1.24s (+14.5%)
  - runtime-only p95: 1.08s vs 1.04s (+3.8%)
  - tokens/sec: 138 tok/s vs 149 tok/s (-7.4%)
  - queueing p95: 190ms vs 85ms (+123%)
• Confidence: medium (variance elevated)
Dashboards:
- Per-model overview: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/perf-canary/perf-canary?var-model=llama-3.1-70b-instruct&var-region=us-east
- Breakdown: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/perf-canary-breakdown/perf-canary-breakdown?var-model=llama-3.1-70b-instruct&var-region=us-east
Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/SRE/Runbook+-+Perf+Regression+Canary+Alert
Dedupe key: `perf-canary|warn|llama-3.1-70b-instruct|us-east|e2e-p95`

Rafael Mendes: looking. this is warn tier, not paging yet. i’ll do quick triage per runbook.

Aisha Bello: pulled the dashboard. e2e up but runtime-only basically flat. queueing spike sticks out.

Noah Patel: +1. if runtime-only isn’t moving, likely scheduler pressure / fleet / autoscaling lag.

Sofia Ivanova: checking batch size dist + queue metrics. seeing avg batch size trending smaller (more fragmentation) and queue p95 up.

Omar El-Sayed: any chance telemetry ingestion lag is skewing the window? we had delays yesterday.

Logan Wright: ingestion looks OK rn (p95 pipeline latency ~12s). not a delayed-metrics artifact.

Vanessa Ortiz: did we change anything in us-east GPU pool recently? i remember a small maintenance rotation today.

Rafael Mendes: yep, can confirm if there was a fleet event. i’m also checking other models for correlated queueing.

Aisha Bello: other top models (mixtral-8x22b, qwen2.5-72b) show mild queueing increase but below thresholds. only llama-3.1-70b is breaching warn.

Vanessa Ortiz: found it: us-east hosted pool had ~8% nodes drained/rotated ~30m ago (driver patch). should have rebalanced, but could explain transient queue.

Sofia Ivanova: that lines up with the queue spike starting ~25-30m back. runtime-only stays flat, so kernels likely not culprit.

Noah Patel: any runtime release rolling? if not, i’d lean infra pressure. also note tokens/sec dip is small and likely from queue + smaller batches.

Rafael Mendes: checking releases… no runtime deploy in us-east in the last 2h. last runtime build tag on panel is stable (`runtime_build=2026.01.19+rc2`).

Omar El-Sayed: can we confirm kernel tag stable too? (the new tag should be on the canary series)

Aisha Bello: kernel_selection_id looks constant over the window (same value as baseline). no sudden switch.

Sean Gallagher: thanks. sounds like warn-level signal that’s actionable but not page-worthy if transient + explained. what’s the plan?

Rafael Mendes: propose: keep as WARN only, monitor for 30-45m. if queueing p95 stays >2x baseline and e2e p95 >15% for another 2 windows, we escalate to paging per policy.

Noah Patel: agree. also would be helpful to annotate dashboard w/ fleet event so future us doesn’t chase runtime ghosts.

Vanessa Ortiz: i can add an ops note + check autoscaler status. i’m seeing pending nodes coming up now; should settle.

Logan Wright: fyi: this is exactly why we broke out queue vs runtime. good signal.

Sofia Ivanova: :thumbsup: i’ll keep an eye on batch size distribution. if it doesn’t recover after capacity returns, we may have a scheduler heuristic issue.

Aisha Bello: i’ll drop a short summary in-thread:
- likely infra drain/rotation causing temporary capacity dip -> queueing
- runtime-only + kernel tag stable
- other models mildly impacted

Rafael Mendes: decision: no paging. keep in incidents channel + monitor. if it clears, we can close with cause “fleet maintenance transient”.

incident-bot: (auto) Reminder: If mitigations applied, update incident summary + link relevant dashboards. Use `/incident close` when resolved.

Vanessa Ortiz: autoscaler catching up. queue p95 is already down from ~190ms to ~120ms last datapoint.

Aisha Bello: e2e p95 down to 1.31s in the last 5m slice. trending toward baseline.

Noah Patel: nice. i’ll stop here unless it rebounds.

Rafael Mendes: cool. i’ll wait another window then close the warn thread.

Rafael Mendes: update @+40m: queue p95 back to ~90ms, e2e p95 1.23s (within noise). marking as resolved. root cause: transient capacity reduction from us-east hosted pool drain/rotation.

incident-bot: (auto) Status changed: RESOLVED (warning)
Summary: perf-canary warn on `llama-3.1-70b-instruct` us-east due to transient queueing spike during GPU pool rotation; recovered without action.
Artifacts: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/perf-canary/perf-canary?var-model=llama-3.1-70b-instruct&var-region=us-east

source.content:
Incident Bot: :rotating_light: Controlled failover live thread started. Scenario: Hosted API us-east -> eu-west. Owners: @Rafael Mendes (SRE lead), @Miles Thornton (arch), @Mina Haddad/@Ravi Menon (routing), @Ethan Park (control plane), @Victor Alvarez (network), @Allison Grant/@Sergio Costa (obs). Comms: @Devon Price. Please post timestamps + actions.

Talia Benson: Kicking off. This is the production controlled test window (per go/no-go). Blast radius: `hosted/chat` + `hosted/generate` on staging-verified model set (tier=dev + a small allowlist). Feature flags scoped to `tenant_group=gameday-1`.

Rafael Mendes: 00:00 — On bridge. Confirming checklist complete. Everyone here?

Miles Thornton: here. Watching region-health + controller audit stream.

Mina Haddad: here. Routing console open, ready to flip policy if controller recommends.

Ravi Menon: here. I’ll monitor policy-engine eval logs + determinism checks.

Ethan Park: here. Control plane healthy. Can approve automation hook if needed.

Victor Alvarez: here. Looking at reachability probes + provider status.

Allison Grant: here. Dashboards pinned:
- Failover signals: https://grafana.redwood-inference.net/d/failover-signals
- Hosted API golden: https://grafana.redwood-inference.net/d/hosted-golden
- Policy flips/audit: https://grafana.redwood-inference.net/d/failover-audit

Sergio Costa: Alert rules muted for paging in this window, but recording annotations is ON.

Nikhil Sharma: here. Watching customer impact metrics + support queue.

Lauren Bishop: here (FYI Dedicated not in this one, but I’m watching anyway in case routing bleeds).

Peter Holtz: here. API gateway circuit breaker configs verified (retry budget capped, `Retry-After` enabled).

Rafael Mendes: 00:02 — Baseline check. Current primary: us-east. Secondary: eu-west.
Baseline metrics (last 10m):
- 5xx: 0.07% overall
- p95 latency us-east: 410ms
- p99 latency us-east: 780ms
- eu-west idle headroom: ~55%
Can someone confirm region health status is green?

Victor Alvarez: Reachability probes: us-east=GREEN, eu-west=GREEN. Cross-region RTT from probes: us-east->eu-west ~92ms median.

Miles Thornton: RegionHealthService summary (TTL=10s):
- us-east: `healthy` (no burn, no reachability issues)
- eu-west: `healthy`
No missing signals.

Jordan Lee: Policy engine version in prod: `policy-engine@2.19.4` (deterministic eval patch included). Logs look clean.

Rafael Mendes: 00:04 — Step 1: enable controller in DRY-RUN for `tenant_group=gameday-1`.
@Ethan Park can you confirm flag path?

Ethan Park: Yep. Use `failover-controller.enable=true` + `failover-controller.mode=dry_run` scoped by tenant group. Approval hook remains required for execution.

Deploy Bot: Applied config change: `failover-controller.mode=dry_run` `failover-controller.enable=true` scope=`tenant_group=gameday-1` (commit 8f21c3a). Rollout: 100% of controller pods.

Miles Thornton: 00:06 — Seeing dry-run recommendations stream. Currently: `no_action`.

Allison Grant: Adding annotation “dry-run enabled” on dashboards.

Grafana Bot: Annotation created by Allison Grant: "gameday-1: failover controller dry-run enabled".

Rafael Mendes: 00:08 — Step 2: induce impairment signal. We’re simulating us-east impairment by toggling synthetic probe failure + injecting 5xx burn (limited scope). @Victor Alvarez confirm probe override is ready.

Victor Alvarez: Ready. I can flip perf-canary target for `us-east` probes to fail from 3 probe POPs (SFO/FRA/IAD). This is scoped to `tenant_group=gameday-1` ingestion, not global.

Ravi Menon: Reminder: ADR-042: reachability is authoritative but needs corroboration (2 sources OR sustained 90s + burn). Hold-down=120s.

Rafael Mendes: 00:10 — Proceed with probe override.

Victor Alvarez: 00:10 — Flipped. Expect reachability score to start degrading within ~30s (probe interval 10s, aggregator TTL 10s).

Miles Thornton: 00:11 — RegionHealthService: us-east reachability down to 0.62 and falling. Error burn still nominal.

Sergio Costa: Panels show reachability dropping. No 5xx yet. Controller still `no_action` (as expected).

Peter Holtz: Gateway is stable. No retry storm.

Rafael Mendes: 00:12 — Step 3: add scoped 5xx injection for `hosted/chat` route for `tenant_group=gameday-1` in us-east only. @Ethan Park ok to proceed?

Ethan Park: Ok. Change is reversible quickly. Please cap at 3% for 3 minutes.

Deploy Bot: Traffic fault injection enabled: `us-east` route=`hosted/chat` scope=`tenant_group=gameday-1` rate=`3%` duration=`180s`.

Allison Grant: 00:13 — Metrics snapshot (tenant_group=gameday-1 only):
- us-east 5xx: 2.8% (expected)
- eu-west 5xx: 0.1%
- us-east p95: 520ms
- us-east p99: 1.2s

Miles Thornton: Controller dry-run now recommending `failover` for scope `hosted/chat` -> eu-west. Confidence: HIGH. Evidence: reachability fail + 5xx burn corroboration. Hold-down satisfied? says `hold_down_remaining=38s`.

Rafael Mendes: Good. Let’s wait for hold-down to fully clear then execute.

Ravi Menon: Seeing deterministic eval output identical across 3 policy-engine replicas. :thumbsup:

Nikhil Sharma: Support queue quiet. No new tickets. I’ll watch external 5xx (global) to ensure we’re not leaking beyond allowlist.

Rafael Mendes: 00:15 — Prepare for execution. Reminder: approval hook required. @Mina Haddad you’ll execute via controller CLI or manual policy flip?

Mina Haddad: We should do controller “execute recommended action” to exercise the path + audit logs. I’ll run CLI with explicit scope + reason.

Miles Thornton: +1. Also confirms rollback wiring.

Mina Haddad: Running:
`failoverctl recommend --scope tenant_group=gameday-1 --route hosted/chat --from us-east --to eu-west`
then
`failoverctl execute --id rec_7c1a --approve --reason "gameday-1 controlled failover"`

Ethan Park: I’m standing by to ack approval prompt.

Incident Bot: (automation) Failover recommendation emitted: `rec_7c1a` action=`shift_primary` route=`hosted/chat` from=`us-east` to=`eu-west` confidence=`high` evidence=`reachability+5xx_burn`.

Ethan Park: Approval granted for `rec_7c1a` (prod, scoped). Proceed.

Incident Bot: (automation) Executing failover action `rec_7c1a`…

Devon Price: Posting in #eng-sre after we confirm success. Holding off on broader comms.

Sergio Costa: 00:17 — Seeing policy flip event. Traffic weight moving: us-east 100% -> 10%, eu-west 0% -> 90% (ramp over 60s).

Grafana Bot: Annotation created by Sergio Costa: "gameday-1: policy flip started (us-east->eu-west)".

Allison Grant: 00:18 — Metrics snapshot (gameday scope):
- us-east RPS dropping (expected)
- eu-west RPS rising
- overall 5xx: 0.9% and trending down
- eu-west p95: 690ms (higher, but within guardrail)
- eu-west p99: 1.6s (watching)

Peter Holtz: p99 spike likely from clients reconnecting streams / retries. Gateway is returning `Retry-After` on some 429s (small).

Rafael Mendes: Are we seeing any overload in eu-west capacity signals?

Keiko Yamamoto: eu-west autoscaler holding. GPU headroom now ~38% (down from 55%). Still safe. No queue depth alerts.

Victor Alvarez: Network looks ok. No packet loss. Provider status green. Probe RTT stable.

Ravi Menon: Controller logs show “anti-flap timer armed 20m” + “max flips/hr not exceeded”. Good.

Miles Thornton: 00:20 — Health aggregator now marks us-east as `degraded` (reachability fail, burn). eu-west `healthy`. Controller state `active_failover`.

Nikhil Sharma: Confirming from customer-facing metrics (global, not scoped): overall Hosted API 5xx unchanged. Good. No support pings.

Rafael Mendes: Great. Next: remove the 5xx injection, keep probes failing for another 5 min to test stability + no oscillation.

Deploy Bot: Traffic fault injection disabled: `us-east` route=`hosted/chat` scope=`tenant_group=gameday-1`.

Allison Grant: 00:22 — After removing injection:
- overall 5xx (gameday): 0.18%
- eu-west p95: 640ms
- eu-west p99: 1.3s
Still a bit elevated vs baseline but trending down.

Miles Thornton: Audit event emitted includes who/why + evidence IDs. Looks complete.

Devon Price: Incident-bot summary looks good. Please paste into this thread once it posts.

Incident Bot: (summary) Failover executed: route=`hosted/chat` scope=`tenant_group=gameday-1` us-east->eu-west. Trigger: reachability + 5xx burn. Time-to-decision=~2m10s. Time-to-shift-90%=~1m05s. Links: dashboards + audit rec_7c1a.

Rafael Mendes: 00:25 — Now test rollback readiness: we will restore reachability probes to healthy, but do NOT rollback immediately; confirm hold-down prevents early reversion.

Victor Alvarez: 00:25 — Restoring probes. Expect reachability score to recover within ~30-60s.

Sergio Costa: us-east reachability climbing. Controller still holding failover state (as expected).

Ravi Menon: Hold-down for rollback per ADR-043 is 15m stable + corroboration. So it should not flap back.

Miles Thornton: Controller showing `rollback_eligible=false` reason=`hold_down_active`.

Allison Grant: 00:28 — Gameday metrics:
- 5xx: 0.12%
- eu-west p95: 610ms
- eu-west p99: 1.1s
- streaming disconnects: small bump at flip time, now normal.

Peter Holtz: Not seeing any retry amplification. Good sign for circuit breaker tuning.

Lauren Bishop: Confirming Dedicated traffic unchanged. No drain workflows triggered.

Rafael Mendes: 00:31 — Any unexpected alerts/noise?

Sergio Costa: Only one warning alert (“eu-west p99 > 1.5s for 2m”) during the ramp. No pages (muted). We should tune annotation + alert threshold in the spec.

Allison Grant: Agree. Also label cardinality looks under control; `failover_reason` values are bounded.

Rafael Mendes: 00:34 — Decision: keep traffic pinned to eu-west for 10 more minutes, then rollback manually to test rollback path, assuming stable.

Miles Thornton: +1. Let’s capture “steady-state on secondary” metrics for report.

Nikhil Sharma: FYI support got 1 question in #support (not ticket) about a blip; we can reply “planned test, scoped, no expected impact” if needed.

Devon Price: I’ll coordinate response guidance with Support. No status page update required (per plan).

Rafael Mendes: 00:41 — Pre-rollback check:
- us-east health: back to GREEN
- eu-west health: GREEN
- error rates: stable
Proceed to rollback execution to original primary for the scoped group.

Mina Haddad: Executing rollback via controller (not manual). Command:
`failoverctl rollback --scope tenant_group=gameday-1 --route hosted/chat --to us-east --reason "gameday-1 rollback"`

Ethan Park: Approval granted for rollback (scoped).

Incident Bot: (automation) Executing rollback action `rb_2a9f` route=`hosted/chat` eu-west->us-east…

Sergio Costa: 00:43 — Seeing weights ramp back (60s). Annotating.

Grafana Bot: Annotation created by Sergio Costa: "gameday-1: rollback started (eu-west->us-east)".

Allison Grant: 00:44 — During rollback:
- transient 5xx peak: 0.6% (gameday)
- us-east p95: 470ms
- us-east p99: 980ms
Looks fine.

Peter Holtz: Streaming reconnections look smoother than last rehearsal. (Nice.)

Miles Thornton: Controller state now `normal`. Anti-flap timer active.

Rafael Mendes: 00:47 — Wrap. Capturing final timestamps for results doc:
- Dry-run enabled: ~00:06
- Probe impairment: ~00:10
- 5xx injection start: ~00:13
- Controller rec_7c1a emitted: ~00:16
- Failover ramp start: ~00:17
- 90% shifted: ~00:18
- Injection removed: ~00:21
- Probes restored: ~00:25
- Rollback start: ~00:43
- Rollback complete: ~00:45
Anything else to note?

Victor Alvarez: One note: reachability recovery lagged by ~20s due to probe windowing. Might be fine, but worth documenting.

Ravi Menon: Policy determinism was good. Would like better “evidence” formatting in CLI output (hard to scan).

Allison Grant: I’ll pull charts + export snapshots for results. Also want an annotation for “injection removed” (we missed that).

Sergio Costa: I can add it retroactively in the dashboard timeline.

Devon Price: I’ll paste the incident-bot summary + link into the game day results doc once Allison exports.

Rafael Mendes: Closing live thread. Post any stragglers here, then we’ll move to retro thread.

Incident Bot: :rotating_light: Potential incident detected: metrics ingestion cost anomaly
- Service: runtime-metrics
- Signal: `timeseries_active` + `ingest_bytes/sec` spiking
- Cluster: hosted-us-east-1 (canary)
- Started: ~10m ago
- Suspected change: tiered batching defaults canary enablement

Allison Grant: seeing it too. active series jumped from ~1.8M -> 7.4M in ~12 min. This lines up exactly with enabling `tier` label in runtime histograms.

Logan Wright: pulling numbers. Ingest for `runtime_latency_ms_bucket{...}` alone is 4x. Also seeing high-cardinality on `reason_code` tag in a couple of metrics.

Aisha Bello: dashboards are basically timing out. Biggest offenders look like:
- `redwood.runtime.request_latency_ms` (hist)
- `redwood.runtime.queue_wait_ms` (hist)
- `redwood.runtime.batch_tokens` (summary)
All now have `tier`, `model`, `region`, `route`, `tenant` tags. That cross-product is huge.

Caleb Johnson: can confirm from Mimir side: series explode is mostly from `route` + `tenant` with `tier`. `tenant` was already borderline but adding `tier` doubled everything.

Julian Moore: I thought we were gating high-cardinality labels behind the cardinality controls from PR-28492. Did we miss something in the canary config?

Rafael Mendes: canary config for tiered batching got flipped on in hosted us-east about 30m ago (flag `tiered_batching_defaults=canary_1pct`). We didn’t change metrics config intentionally.

Allison Grant: the cardinality control code is there, but it only drops tags for a subset of metrics. Looks like histograms are still emitting full tag set. Also: `effective_profile` is being emitted as a tag in tracing-derived metrics. That’s per-route right?

Logan Wright: yes, `effective_profile` includes resolved knobs hash. That’s basically unbounded and will explode. That should never be a label.

Connor O'Brien: do we need to roll back the canary right now to stop the bleed? Or can we hotfix just metrics quickly?

Sean Gallagher: from SRE POV: we should stop the cost spike first. Our ingest budget alerts are going to page again in ~15 if it keeps climbing.

Aisha Bello: +1 rollback/kill switch. Can we disable tier labels without disabling tiered batching behavior? (so we keep perf canary data but reduce series)

Julian Moore: there’s a runtime flag `metrics.tier_labels_enabled` in the new build, but it’s not in prod yet. For now it’s baked into the new runtime metrics. Quickest is to roll back canary to pre-1.22 build or disable tiered batching flag entirely.

Rafael Mendes: I can flip canary flag off (kill switch) within 2-3 mins. That would revert to old defaults + old metric set? Actually metric labels are emitted by runtime build, not the feature flag.

Caleb Johnson: right. If the canary hosts are running 1.22 build already, turning off tiered batching won’t stop tier labels being emitted. Need to rollback runtime build or deploy a hotfix build that removes labels / limits.

Allison Grant: ok decision point:
A) Roll back canary runtime build in hosted-us-east (preferred immediate containment)
B) Prepare hotfix build that drops high-card labels / removes `effective_profile` label and caps `tenant`/`route` for tiered metrics
We should do both if feasible.

Logan Wright: agreed. I’ll open a hotfix request: remove `effective_profile` from metrics labels; keep it only in traces/log fields. Also cap labels to `{tier, model_family, region}` for hist metrics.

Nadia Rahman: FYI metering pipeline also consumes some of these metrics (cost attribution panels). If we nuke `tenant` label entirely, make sure it doesn’t break cost views. But for runtime latency hist, tenant-level isn’t required.

Caleb Johnson: we can keep tenant on low-volume counters and drop from high-volume histograms. That’s consistent w/ our standards.

Sean Gallagher: +1. For incident scope, prioritize platform stability and telemetry spend. We can still diagnose tier selection via logs/traces.

Connor O'Brien: I’ll sync with release eng. If we roll back the canary hosts to 1.21, we lose tiered batching data but stop the metric flood. We can re-run canary after hotfix.

Rafael Mendes: executing containment: pausing canary expansion and draining canary pool.
- action: stop scheduling traffic to canary instances
- action: rollback canary runtime deployment to previous stable

Incident Bot: Action recorded by Rafael Mendes: paused rollout + initiating rollback for hosted-us-east canary pool.

Aisha Bello: watching ingest graphs. If rollback works, we should see active series start to fall within ~10-20 mins as series expire.

Julian Moore: I’m looking at code. The offender is in `emit_histogram_with_common_tags()` which includes `route_id` + `tenant_id` always. Tier labels added on top = boom. We need to apply the cardinality allowlist there.

Allison Grant: can you patch that today? We should align with PR-28544 (I remember a draft about limiting tier label cardinality).

Logan Wright: yep, PR-28544 is exactly this. It changes:
- drop `effective_profile` label entirely
- hash bucket for route only when debug sampling
- hard cap: only emit tier label on a subset of metrics + strip tenant/route on hist
I can get it reviewed quickly.

Mateo Alvarez: joining. what’s impact beyond cost? any risk to serving latency / stability?

Sean Gallagher: no serving impact observed. This is telemetry ingest/cost and dashboard usability. But it can cascade into pipeline backpressure if we keep flooding.

Mateo Alvarez: ok. make sure we stop the bleed, then hotfix. I’m ok to delay canary data collection if needed.

Caleb Johnson: rollback seems to be taking effect. Active series down from 7.4M -> 6.2M in last 5 mins. Still falling.

Aisha Bello: confirmed. ingest bytes/sec down ~35%. still elevated, but trending.

Rafael Mendes: rollback status: 70% of canary nodes back on 1.21. Remaining 30% draining.

Allison Grant: next steps:
1) Land hotfix (PR-28544) to prevent recurrence
2) Add a canary gate: if active series > threshold, auto-abort rollout (release tooling)
3) Update metrics taxonomy doc to explicitly forbid `effective_profile`/knob-hash as label

Logan Wright: +1. PR up in a few mins. Need reviewers: @Allison Grant @Caleb Johnson.

Julian Moore: I’ll add a unit test for label allowlist on hist emissions so we don’t regress.

Connor O'Brien: comms: I’ll post in #eng-releases that canary is rolled back due to metrics cardinality and that tiered batching canary is paused until hotfix build is cut.

Incident Bot: Reminder: add incident notes + timeline in the incident doc and link PR / rollback commit.

Allison Grant: quick note for timeline:
- T0: canary runtime 1.22 enabled in hosted-us-east
- T0+~20m: telemetry ingest alarm fired
- containment: rollback canary build
- fix: hotfix to cap labels

Nadia Rahman: please tag me on PR if it touches metering labels. If only runtime latency/queue metrics, should be fine.

Caleb Johnson: reviewing PR as soon as it’s up. Also: we should reduce histogram bucket count? It’s not the core issue but helps.

Logan Wright: PR-28544 posted. Summary:
- remove `effective_profile` from metrics labels
- apply allowlist per metric type; histograms keep only `{tier, model_family, region}`
- keep tenant/route on counters only; add sampling for route debug

Allison Grant: reviewing now.

Aisha Bello: active series now 2.3M (almost back to baseline). dashboards responding again.

Rafael Mendes: rollback complete. canary pool fully reverted. no customer-facing impact reported.

Sean Gallagher: let’s keep incident open until hotfix is merged + build cut. also add alert for series growth rate so we catch this before 4x.

Allison Grant: agreed. assigning action items:
- Logan: hotfix PR merge + coordinate build
- Rafael/Connor: prepare canary re-run after hotfix
- Aisha/Sean: add alert thresholds + runbook note
- Julian: add regression tests

Incident Bot: Incident status updated: Mitigated (rollback). Pending follow-ups: hotfix + new canary guardrails.

source.body:
Devon Price: seeing staging failover rehearsal blocked — failover-controller is crashlooping in stg-us-east. anyone around to triage?

incident-bot: :rotating_light: Incident opened: STG-INC-2147
incident-bot: Summary: failover-controller CrashLoopBackOff in staging during rehearsal
incident-bot: Severity: SEV3 (staging)
incident-bot: Owner: Devon Price

Miles Thornton: on it. which cluster/namespace?

Devon Price: cluster `stg-use1-k8s-03`, ns `routing-system`. started ~5 min ago.

Rafael Mendes: I’m here. This blocks today’s rehearsal, let’s treat as time-sensitive. Miles can you grab logs + last deploy?

Devon Price: last deploy shows chart change from this morning (feature flags for simulate endpoint) but could be unrelated. `kubectl -n routing-system get pods | grep failover-controller`:
```
failover-controller-7c9c88bd8f-4j8lq   0/1   CrashLoopBackOff   6   7m
```

Miles Thornton: pulling logs now.

Miles Thornton: logs snippet (last lines):
```
2026-01-15T18:07:31.402Z  INFO controller starting  version=1.14.3-stg  dry_run=false
2026-01-15T18:07:31.905Z  INFO loading signals  source=region-health
2026-01-15T18:07:31.911Z  WARN missing signal stream  signal=perf_canary_reachability  region=us-east
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
  at controller/eval.go:217
  at controller/tick.go:88
```
Looks like NPE when a signal is missing.

Jordan Lee: yep that’s in the evaluation path. If `SignalSet["perf_canary_reachability"]` is nil we deref without guard. We assumed perf-canary always present.

Ethan Park: perf-canary was restarted in stg earlier, might explain missing stream. controller should degrade, not die.

Benji Okafor: +1, guard it and treat as “unknown” -> should fail closed (no automatic flip) but keep running + emit audit event.

Allison Grant: do we have an alert on controller crashloops in staging? surprised we found via rehearsal.

Sergio Costa: we have a generic K8s crashloop warning but it’s not paging and has no service context. can add a targeted alert rule for this controller.

Victor Alvarez: FYI perf-canary in `stg-use1` had a brief DNS issue earlier, probes were timing out. That might be why it stopped publishing.

Miles Thornton: impact: rehearsal can’t proceed because controller never reaches steady state. We can hotfix quickly.

Rafael Mendes: Jordan can you patch? Prefer minimal diff: null check + metric/audit log.

Jordan Lee: ack. I can push a hotfix PR against `release/staging` today. Proposed behavior:
- if required signal missing -> record `signal_missing{signal=...}`
- treat missing as `UNKNOWN` and do NOT trigger failover
- continue loop

Benji Okafor: make sure we don’t mark region unhealthy just due to missing one input. also add jitter so we don’t spam logs.

Devon Price: can someone paste the exact file/line? helps for PR.

Miles Thornton: stack trace points to `controller/eval.go:217` where we call `reachability.LatencyP95()` without nil guard.

Jordan Lee: got it.

redwood-deploy-bot: Detected deployment event in `stg-use1-k8s-03/routing-system`: helm release `failover-controller` updated 8h ago by `release-bot`.

Ethan Park: while Jordan patches, can we unblock rehearsal with a config toggle? maybe `require_perf_canary=false`?

Miles Thornton: I checked values, no toggle currently. Only `signals.required=[...]` and it includes perf-canary. even if we remove it, the eval code still expects the struct.

Rafael Mendes: ok patch it is. Jordan, ETA?

Jordan Lee: 30–45 min for PR + build.

Allison Grant: please add a log line that includes `region`, `route`, `signal` so we can correlate in dashboards. but keep labels low-cardinality.

Jordan Lee: will do. using structured log fields only; metrics will be `signal_missing_total{signal=<enum>, env=staging}`.

Miles Thornton: while waiting, I’ll restart perf-canary too, but we shouldn’t depend on that.

Victor Alvarez: I can verify perf-canary publishing again once it’s restarted.

Devon Price: ok I’m marking incident as “mitigation in progress” and linking to hotfix PR once it exists.

Jordan Lee: PR up: `redwood/pr-19840-fix-failover-controller-npe-on-missing-signal` (hotfix). Adds nil guard + unit test `TestEval_MissingSignalDoesNotPanic`.

Benji Okafor: reviewing.

Benji Okafor: left one comment: make sure missing signal sets `confidence=low` in recommendation output so dry-run UI doesn’t look overly confident.

Jordan Lee: pushed follow-up commit. also adds an audit event: `failover.signal_missing` with fields `{signal, region, controller_version}`.

Ethan Park: LGTM from control-plane side.

Miles Thornton: +1, minimal and safe.

Rafael Mendes: merge + deploy to staging.

redwood-deploy-bot: Build succeeded for `pr-19840` (artifact: `failover-controller:1.14.4-stg-hotfix1`).

redwood-deploy-bot: Deploy started: `stg-use1-k8s-03` release `failover-controller` -> `1.14.4-stg-hotfix1`.

Devon Price: watching rollout.

Devon Price: rollout done. pod is up:
```
failover-controller-6f6b9d8f6b-qd7m2   1/1   Running   0   32s
```

Miles Thornton: confirm logs look sane now:
```
INFO controller tick ok  regions=6
WARN signal missing signal=perf_canary_reachability region=us-east action=no_op
INFO emitted audit event type=failover.signal_missing
```
No panic.

Victor Alvarez: perf-canary is also back, publishing again. (DNS issue cleared). missing warnings should stop shortly.

Allison Grant: I’m seeing `signal_missing_total` increment a few times then flatten. good.

Rafael Mendes: rehearsal unblocked? can we proceed with the dry-run exercise?

Devon Price: yes. rehearsal team is green now.

Rafael Mendes: cool. Let’s capture follow-ups before we close.

Miles Thornton: follow-ups I see:
1) Add controller-level alert for CrashLoopBackOff (staging + prod) with service context
2) Add explicit “missing signal” section to runbook (what it means, which deps to check)
3) Evaluate whether perf-canary signals should be REQUIRED or CORROBORATING per ADR-042 (avoid hard dependency)

Sergio Costa: I’ll take (1) and wire alert annotations to dashboards.

Devon Price: I’ll take (2) runbook note and link to this incident thread.

Benji Okafor: I’ll take (3) and open a small doc/issue: required vs corroboration; propose defaulting missing to UNKNOWN and requiring corroboration for action.

Allison Grant: also add an annotation when we emit `signal_missing` audit events, so they show up in the failover dashboard timeline (helps during game day).

Ethan Park: +1. we can add that in incident-bot or observability-pack later.

Devon Price: closing STG-INC-2147 as resolved. Root cause: controller assumed perf-canary signal present; missing stream caused NPE. Fix: nil guard + safe behavior + unit test + audit event.

incident-bot: Incident STG-INC-2147 marked Resolved by Devon Price
incident-bot: Duration: 1h 0m
incident-bot: Follow-ups: alerts, runbook update, signal dependency review
