Allison Grant: Post-mitigation, `gateway_auth_failure_rate` now 0.7% (near baseline). `invalid_signature` still elevated but being 401’d fast; introspect timeouts basically gone.

Rafael Mendes: Good. Fiona, any remaining reasons to keep SEC incident open?

Fiona McCarthy: We’ll keep SEC-2419 open for a bit to finish IP reputation blocks and confirm no compromised creds. But as of now: *no indicators of compromise*, looks like probing/abuse.

Rafael Mendes: Acknowledged. For this incident: mark mitigated once we hold baseline for 15m. We’ll do a postmortem note with the dual-track decision and what signals triggered SecOps involvement.

incident-bot: Reminder: Add contributing systems and SLA outcome before resolve.

Rafael Mendes: Contributing systems: Gateway Auth, Auth Introspection Service, Rate Limiter/WAF.
SLA: mitigated within Sev1 TTM target (tracking).

Ethan Park: Confirmed stable for 15m. I’ll keep the autoscale min elevated until traffic normalizes.

Rafael Mendes: Resolving. Action items:
- Add clearer decision tree entry: "auth failures + high IP diversity => pull SecOps + consider dual-track"
- Add dashboard panel: invalid_signature rate vs introspect timeouts
- WAF rules + gateway rate limit runbook update

incident-bot: Incident status updated: *Resolved* by @Rafael Mendes. Please link postmortem for Sev1 within 24h.
text:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: *New Incident* Sev1 — "Auth failures spike" (Hosted)
• Trigger: `api.gateway.auth_failure_rate > 6% (5m)`
• Symptoms: elevated 401/403 + token introspection timeouts
• Region(s): us-east-1, eu-west-1
• Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.internal/oncall/runbooks/auth-and-rbac
• Create Zoom: https://zoom.us/j/xxx

Rafael Mendes: IC here. Seeing auth failures across multiple tenants. Mostly 401s + a chunk of 500s from auth service. @Ethan Park can you grab control plane/auth service status?

Dylan Brooks: I’m on comms/ops. Also seeing a bunch of “invalid_signature” in gateway logs. Could be a bad key roll or… not great.

Ethan Park: Looking. auth-introspect p95 jumped 80ms -> 2.3s and error rate 4.8%. Also seeing increased requests per second on `/oauth/introspect`.

Allison Grant: Metrics sanity: this isn’t just dashboard noise. `gateway_auth_denied_total` and `auth_introspect_errors_total` both up, plus `unique_source_ips` spikes in logs pipeline.

Rafael Mendes: If unique IPs spiking + invalid sig, we should pull in SecOps. @Fiona McCarthy can you join and advise? We need to decide if this is just reliability auth outage vs security incident.

Fiona McCarthy: Joining. Quick triage questions:
1) Are denies clustered to a single customer/token issuer or broad?
2) Any WAF alerts / rate-limit triggers?
3) Evidence of credential stuffing (many usernames) or token replay?
If broad + weird signatures + high IP diversity, we should *dual-track* until we rule out malicious.

Mina Haddad: I checked router: no config changes in last hour. No new routing policies. Traffic spike looks external, not internal retries.

Ethan Park: Broad impact. Not one tenant. Error breakdown:
- ~60% `invalid_signature` at gateway
- ~25% `introspect_timeout`
- ~15% `rbac_denied` (normal-ish)
Also: KMS looks ok. No key rotation event in control plane today.

Fiona McCarthy: `invalid_signature` across many tenants suggests either (a) clients sending bad tokens en masse, (b) we changed verification keys/algorithms (doesn’t sound like it), or (c) automated probing.

Dev Patel: Support ping: 6 new chats in last 10m saying “all requests failing auth”, mix of Hosted + Dedicated. One enterprise is asking if this is an attack.

Rafael Mendes: For now classify as reliability incident: *Auth/RBAC failure* (suspected external traffic anomaly). But we’ll open a parallel security ticket if needed.

Fiona McCarthy: +1 on reliability primary. For *dual-track* guidelines (so we’re consistent):
- If customer impact from auth failures is the main issue, keep this incident channel as primary.
- If *any* of the following are true, open a Security Incident (SEC-*) in parallel and keep SecOps as co-lead:
  • confirmed malicious indicators (WAF confirms botnet, clear credential stuffing patterns)
  • suspected secret/key compromise (signing keys, KMS anomalies, unexpected key IDs)
  • data exposure risk (auth bypass, privilege escalation, token forging that validates)
  • legal/compliance triggers (regulated customers, breach notification thresholds)
- While dual-tracking: keep mitigation discussion here, and investigation/artifacts (IPs, samples) in the SEC thread/channel to reduce oversharing.

Dylan Brooks: That’s helpful. Can you confirm: creating SEC incident doesn’t change our Sev1 SLA timers, right?

Fiona McCarthy: Correct—reliability remediation SLA still runs here. Security runs its own timeline. They can share updates but the reliability IC retains customer comms cadence unless/until security says otherwise.

Allison Grant: I can pull a quick sample of the top offending source IPs + user agents. Where should that go?

Fiona McCarthy: Please DM to me or post in `#sec-incidents` once opened. Don’t paste raw IP lists publicly in this channel.

Rafael Mendes: Acknowledged. Let’s do this:
1) Mitigate auth failures now (rate limit + protect introspect)
2) In parallel, have SecOps analyze whether this is malicious.
Dylan, can you open a SEC ticket + channel? Fiona will lead that track.

Dylan Brooks: On it. Creating SEC-2419 and `#sec-incident-2419-auth-probing`.

incident-bot: Update requested fields:
• incident_type: _unset_
• incident_subtype: _unset_
• owner: _unset_

Rafael Mendes: Setting taxonomy now.
incident_type = Auth/RBAC failure
subtype = Token verification errors (invalid signature / issuer)
owner = Platform Control Plane (Auth)
Secondary: Security Ops (consult)

Ethan Park: Immediate mitigation option: enable stricter gateway rate limits on `/oauth/introspect` by IP + per-tenant, and increase cache TTL for introspection results to reduce load. Risk: might affect legitimate high-churn tokens.

Ravi Menon: For Dedicated: we can apply at edge too but need to avoid breaking customer custom auth proxies. Suggest start Hosted first, then Dedicated with allowlist for known egress IPs.

Dev Patel: Please keep me posted before we roll out anything that could change behavior for Dedicated customers. I can prep a short comms note.

Rafael Mendes: Plan:
- Step 1: Hosted gateway: rate limit introspect + enable circuit breaker fallback (temporary)
- Step 2: Increase introspect service autoscale
- Step 3: Evaluate Dedicated edge policies w/ Support heads-up
Ethan can you execute Step 1/2?

Ethan Park: Starting Step 2 now: bump min replicas 20 -> 60. ETA 5-7m.
Step 1: I’ll push a config change to gateway rate limit: `200 rps/ip` on introspect, burst 400. Also add `429` instead of timeout.

Allison Grant: Watching: `auth_introspect_queue_depth` climbing but should stabilize with scale. I’ll alert if it doesn’t.

Fiona McCarthy: In SEC channel, we’re seeing a bot-like UA pattern and many JWTs with nonsense `kid` values. That points to probing rather than key compromise. No evidence tokens are validating incorrectly (i.e., no auth bypass).

Rafael Mendes: Great. That keeps this as reliability-only, SecOps consult. Any action needed re: WAF?

Fiona McCarthy: Recommend adding WAF rule for that UA + IP reputation block. SecOps will file it; doesn’t need to block this mitigation.

Ethan Park: Scale is in. p95 down to 600ms and falling. Auth failures: 6% -> 2.1%. Still above baseline.

Rafael Mendes: Dylan, comms update for status page internal + Support? Keep it generic: "elevated auth errors" no mention of attack.

Dylan Brooks: Draft:
"We are investigating elevated authentication errors causing increased request failures. Mitigation is in progress and errors are trending down. Next update in 30 minutes."

Dev Patel: Works. For the enterprise customer asking: I’ll say we’re seeing elevated auth failures and have mitigations in progress; we’ll follow up if we confirm anything security-related.

Allison Grant: Post-mitigation, `gateway_auth_failure_rate` now 0.7% (near baseline). `invalid_signature` still elevated but being 401’d fast; introspect timeouts basically gone.

Rafael Mendes: Good. Fiona, any remaining reasons to keep SEC incident open?

Fiona McCarthy: We’ll keep SEC-2419 open for a bit to finish IP reputation blocks and confirm no compromised creds. But as of now: *no indicators of compromise*, looks like probing/abuse.

Rafael Mendes: Acknowledged. For this incident: mark mitigated once we hold baseline for 15m. We’ll do a postmortem note with the dual-track decision and what signals triggered SecOps involvement.

incident-bot: Reminder: Add contributing systems and SLA outcome before resolve.

Rafael Mendes: Contributing systems: Gateway Auth, Auth Introspection Service, Rate Limiter/WAF.
SLA: mitigated within Sev1 TTM target (tracking).

Ethan Park: Confirmed stable for 15m. I’ll keep the autoscale min elevated until traffic normalizes.

Rafael Mendes: Resolving. Action items:
- Add clearer decision tree entry: "auth failures + high IP diversity => pull SecOps + consider dual-track"
- Add dashboard panel: invalid_signature rate vs introspect timeouts
- WAF rules + gateway rate limit runbook update

incident-bot: Incident status updated: *Resolved* by @Rafael Mendes. Please link postmortem for Sev1 within 24h.

source.content:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: *Incident opened* (Sev1)
*Service:* Redwood API (Hosted) + Dedicated spillover
*Region:* us-east-1
*Symptoms:* elevated 5xx, queue depth rising, router fallback rate > 40%
*Incident channel:* #inc-1763194772

Rafael Mendes: grabbing IC. seeing p95 explode + 5xx spike starting ~10 mins ago. anyone already looking at fleet?

Vanessa Ortiz: Fleet here. us-east-1 GPU pool is pinned. `gpu_allocatable` basically zero in 3 clusters. autoscaler trying but provisioning lag.

Harish Reddy: +1. also seeing spot interruption spike. looks like we lost a chunk of A10 nodes in one AZ and backfill is slow.

Mina Haddad: Routing on. router is flipping to fallback model/region more than usual. question: are we failing to gracefully shed (429) and instead throwing 5xx?

Ravi Menon: looking at router metrics. fallback_reason breakdown: ~70% `capacity`, ~20% `health`, remainder `policy`. 5xx seem correlated with `no_backend_available` errors.

Rafael Mendes: ok. we need to pick incident taxonomy now (required). debate is capacity shortage vs routing/fallback misbehavior. initial read: root is capacity event, but routing behavior might be making it worse.

Sergio Costa: dashboard link for everyone: https://grafana.redwoodinference.net/d/ops-router/router-overview?var-region=us-east-1
`router_fallback_rate` jumped at same time as `fleet_gpu_saturation`.

Aisha Bello: also seeing SLO burn: Hosted availability SLO is burning hard in us-east-1. if we can convert failures to 429 + retries we’ll reduce 5xx impact.

Mina Haddad: for clarity: router is doing what it’s told: it tries cross-AZ then cross-region based on policy. but we’re hitting a hard cap on cross-region bandwidth + some customers are pinned to us-east-1 only.

Vanessa Ortiz: capacity is definitely the trigger. we have `provisioning_backlog` 900+ GPUs. cloud quota is fine, but instance fulfillment is slow.

Harish Reddy: quick mitigation options:
1) temporarily raise max price / switch to on-demand for the A10 pool in us-east-1
2) reduce per-request max tokens in Hosted to increase throughput
3) push some Dedicated overflow back to their reserved pools (stop spill)

Ravi Menon: from routing side, we can:
- tighten `fallback_policy` to prefer same-region smaller models instead of cross-region (reduces egress + latency)
- enable `shed_to_429` earlier when queue depth > threshold

Rafael Mendes: let’s do mitigations in parallel. Vanessa/Harish take fleet changes. Mina/Ravi, can you propose the routing knobs with lowest risk?

Mina Haddad: yup. proposing: enable early shed to 429 for Hosted tier only in us-east-1. for Dedicated, keep behavior but cap spillover.

Dev Patel: Support here, we have 3 Dedicated customers escalating already. can we tell them this is “regional capacity degradation” and we’re failing over?

Rafael Mendes: yes, but careful: don’t promise full failover if they’re pinned. we’ll align with comms: *regional capacity constraints leading to elevated errors/latency; mitigation in progress; some traffic rerouted where possible*.

incident-bot: *Reminder:* incident classification required before marking mitigated/resolved.
Set `incident_type`, `incident_subtype`, and confirm `primary_owner`.

Rafael Mendes: classification discussion:
Option A) `Capacity / Fleet Shortage` (regional saturation / provisioning lag)
Option B) `Routing/Fallback Misbehavior` (fallback not graceful, wrong errors)
What do we think as *primary*? We can add contributing systems in postmortem.

Vanessa Ortiz: primary should be capacity/fleet. router is reacting to lack of capacity.

Harish Reddy: agree. also spot interruption event is outside routing control. provisioning backlog is the driver.

Mina Haddad: fine with capacity as primary, but we should note routing contributed to customer impact (5xx vs 429) + pinned policy edge cases.

Ravi Menon: +1. also our `no_backend_available` should probably map to 429 for some routes. that’s routing remediation.

Aisha Bello: for taxonomy, I’d set:
- incident_type: Capacity/Fleet Shortage
- subtype: Regional GPU capacity exhaustion / provisioning lag
- contributing: Routing fallback behavior (error mapping / policy constraints)

Rafael Mendes: ok, locking that.

incident-bot: *Classification draft* (auto-suggested)
`incident_type`: Capacity/Fleet Shortage
`incident_subtype`: Regional capacity saturation (GPU exhaustion)
`primary_owner`: Infra/Fleet
`consulted`: Smart Routing
React with :white_check_mark: to confirm or reply with edits.

Vanessa Ortiz: :white_check_mark:

Harish Reddy: :white_check_mark:

Mina Haddad: :white_check_mark: (please add routing as contributing system in PM)

Ravi Menon: :white_check_mark:

Rafael Mendes: confirmed. owner = Infra/Fleet. routing consulted. we’ll capture contributing factor + follow-ups in PM.

Sergio Costa: update: fleet change rolling out? seeing small dip in queue depth.

Harish Reddy: switched 2 clusters to on-demand, autoscaler catching up. ETA 15-20m for more allocatable.

Mina Haddad: enabled early shed-to-429 for Hosted us-east-1. should reduce 5xx. watching error mix.

Aisha Bello: 5xx rate trending down; 429 up (expected). SLO burn slowing.

Dev Patel: thanks. I’ll update CS notes: capacity constraints, mitigation: added capacity + adjusted shedding; Dedicated pinned customers may still see degradation.

Rafael Mendes: for postmortem: please note classification rationale: primary = regional capacity event; routing behavior changed to reduce impact; action items on routing error mapping + pinned policy review.

incident-bot: *Status update* marked: Mitigation in progress.
SLA timer continues until customer impact is cleared.

Vanessa Ortiz: allocatable GPUs now +120 and climbing. should be stable once we refill AZ-a.

Ravi Menon: capturing logs for PM:
```
router_fallback_reason{region="us-east-1"}:
  capacity=0.71 health=0.21 policy=0.08
router_errors_no_backend_available spiked 18:02-18:47
```

Mina Haddad: also note: a chunk of Dedicated requests were spilling into Hosted shared pool due to a stale config on 2 tenants. I’ll open a follow-up ticket.

Rafael Mendes: good catch; that’s a contributing config issue. still within capacity shortage umbrella but we should document.

incident-bot: *Update:* incident marked Mitigated by @Rafael Mendes.
*Checklist:* add postmortem link/placeholder before close (required for Sev1).

Rafael Mendes: creating placeholder PM now. will link shortly.

incident-bot: Postmortem placeholder created: https://confluence.redwoodinference.net/x/PM-1763194772
Please ensure fields are filled:
- incident_type/subtype (set)
- primary_owner (set)
- contributing systems (pending)
- remediation SLA outcome (pending)

Aisha Bello: I’ll add SLO impact + timeline graphs to PM.

Vanessa Ortiz: Fleet will add capacity timeline + provider notes. likely need a playbook for spot interruption spikes.

Mina Haddad: I’ll add routing changes + recommendation: map `no_backend_available` to 429 when queue depth triggers shedding.

Rafael Mendes: closing loop. thanks all.

incident-bot: Incident closed by @Rafael Mendes.
*Final classification:* Capacity/Fleet Shortage → Regional capacity saturation (GPU exhaustion)
*Primary owner:* Infra/Fleet | *Consulted:* Smart Routing

source.messages:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: **GAME DAY / SIMULATED INCIDENT STARTED**
Scenario: Hot-route overload simulation (Hosted API) — /v1/chat/completions burst in us-east
Objective: Validate hot-route capacity protection preserves Enterprise SLO while shedding Best-effort predictably.
Commander: Rafael Mendes | Scribe: Talia Benson
Links: Dashboard https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/hot-route-protection?var_region=us-east | Runbook https://confluence.redwood.ai/x/overload-protection

Rafael Mendes: kicking off. Traffic injector starts in ~2 min. Everyone pls keep notes in-thread. We’ll treat this like a real Sev2.

Talia Benson: ack, I’m scribing. Success criteria reminder: enterprise p95 stays < target, best-effort sheds w/ 429 overload reason, no 5xx cascade, alerts fire but no paging storm.

Vanessa Ortiz: fleet signal baseline (us-east): gpu_saturation ~62%, scheduler_lag_p95 ~18ms, queue_depth_avg ~0.3.

Logan Wright: telemetry looks clean. No cardinality spikes so far. I’m watching `overload_event_total` + `admission_decision_total{tier}`.

Aisha Bello: FYI burn alerts are in “observe” mode for game day (no PagerDuty), but Slack notifications should still post.

Sofia Ivanova: runtime side: queue depth exporter is stable; last deploy had the lag histograms fixed. If we see lag > 250ms sustained we’re in the danger zone.

Elena Popov: routing config in place:
- enterprise_priority_class=gold
- best_effort_priority_class=bronze
- admission_control enabled via `hot_route_protection_us_east`
Current thresholds: `queue_depth_high=8`, `queue_depth_critical=14` (route group: chat)

deploy-bot: Traffic injector job `gameday-hotroute-chat-burst-us-east` scheduled (start in 60s)

Rafael Mendes: start injection now. Goal is to push best-effort until we hit queue_depth_high.

deploy-bot: Traffic injector job started. Target RPS ramp: 1k -> 8k over 10 min. Mix: 70% best-effort, 30% enterprise allowlisted.

incident-bot: :chart_with_upwards_trend: **Signal** queue_depth_p95(route_group=chat, region=us-east) crossing HIGH watermark (8). Current=9.1
Action: Admission control should begin gating bronze tier.

Peter Holtz: seeing admission decisions flip. `admission_decision_total{tier=bronze,decision=reject,reason=overload_queue_depth}` climbing. Gold still all admitted.

Amara Diallo: gateway 429s are showing expected headers:
`x-redwood-retry-after-ms: 250`
`x-redwood-limit-reason: overload`
`x-redwood-overload-reason: queue_depth_high`
Payload matches docs.

Mina Haddad: latency check: gold p95 is stable (1.42s -> 1.51s), bronze p95 is up a bit but not spiky. 5xx flat.

Logan Wright: confirm 429 reason breakdown: ~96% overload_queue_depth, ~4% quota_window. That’s good; no “unknown”.

Talia Benson: noting: HIGH watermark hit at ~T+3m, bronze gating engaged, gold unaffected.

incident-bot: :warning: **Alert (observe)** `hot_route_shed_rate_high` shed_rate(bronze, chat, us-east)=18% for 2m (threshold=15%)
Runbook step: Validate enterprise SLO + ensure shed reason codes are overload (not quota).

Aisha Bello: alert fired as expected. Not paging. No other noisy alerts yet.

Rafael Mendes: ok, let it ramp another 2 min. If gold p95 creeps > target, we adjust critical threshold lower.

Sofia Ivanova: scheduler_lag_p95 now ~74ms; still ok.

Vanessa Ortiz: gpu_saturation jumped to 86% but not pinned; we’ve got headroom.

Elena Popov: queue_depth_p95 now 13.2 (approaching critical=14). If it crosses, routing should start stricter gating + disable fallbacks for gold per guardrails.

Jordan Lee: policy-eval latency stable ~0.6ms p95. Cache hits 98%. Decision logs sampling 1/1k, no spikes.

incident-bot: :rotating_light: **Signal** queue_depth_p95(route_group=chat, region=us-east) crossing CRITICAL watermark (14). Current=14.6
Action: Admission control in “critical” mode. Bronze reject increases; Silver (if any) begins partial reject. Gold protected.

Mina Haddad: I’m seeing a *small* gold p99 bump (from ~3.9s to 5.2s) right after critical flipped. p95 still fine.

Peter Holtz: looks like some long-prompt requests slipped into gold mix. Critical mode is rejecting bronze aggressively but gold still shares GPU pool. Might need earlier critical to avoid p99 bump.

Rafael Mendes: agree. Let’s adjust `queue_depth_critical` from 14 -> 12 for chat in us-east for game day. Elena can you apply via config override? We’ll watch if it stabilizes p99.

Elena Popov: on it. Applying route group override (us-east only) via admin config.
Command:
`rrctl set routing.admission.chat.us-east.queue_depth_critical=12 --reason "gameday p99 stabilization"`

deploy-bot: Config change applied: `routing.admission.chat.us-east.queue_depth_critical` 14 -> 12 (etag=9f31c). Propagation ETA ~30s.

Amara Diallo: heads up: with earlier critical, bronze 429s will increase. That’s expected; support semantics still overload.

Logan Wright: watching for any metric cardinality changes due to new override tag. Should stay low (route_group + region only).

incident-bot: :information_source: **Config annotation** admission_control threshold updated (chat/us-east): critical=12

Sofia Ivanova: scheduler_lag_p95 dropped back to ~61ms. queue_depth_p95 is hovering ~12.3. That’s healthier.

Mina Haddad: gold p99 trending down (5.2s -> 4.4s). p95 steady 1.55s. Looks like the change helped.

Aisha Bello: burn-rate for gold SLO is flat. Bronze error budget is burning (expected).

Talia Benson: capturing: threshold adjustment at ~T+8m (critical 14->12) reduced gold p99 and stabilized scheduler lag.

Rafael Mendes: keep running for 5 more minutes then start ramp down. We want to confirm recovery behavior: shedding decreases cleanly and no stuck “critical mode”.

deploy-bot: Traffic injector reached max load (8k RPS). Holding for 3 min.

incident-bot: :chart_with_upwards_trend: **Signal** shed_rate(bronze, chat, us-east)=41% (critical mode). Gold shed_rate ~0.2%.

Peter Holtz: gold shed is near zero but not exactly. Can we confirm those are client cancellations not sheds? Reason code matters.

Amara Diallo: checking gateway logs for gold 429s… seeing a handful but `x-redwood-limit-reason=quota` (customer-specific quota), not overload. No overload 429s for gold.

Logan Wright: confirms: overload_event_total{tier=gold} unchanged; gold 429s are quota. Good.

Jordan Lee: policy logs show gold admitted decisions; no “overload_reject” for gold.

Vanessa Ortiz: gpu_saturation pinned at 93–95% during hold but no throttling of gold.

deploy-bot: Traffic injector ramping down (8k -> 1k over 5 min).

incident-bot: :chart_with_downwards_trend: **Recovery** queue_depth_p95(route_group=chat, region=us-east) below HIGH watermark (8). Current=7.4
Action: Admission control should exit critical/high and return to normal.

Elena Popov: routing reports mode=normal now. Shed rate dropping fast.

Mina Haddad: gold p95 back to baseline (1.44s). 5xx still flat.

Aisha Bello: alerts resolved automatically. No lingering “shed rate high” notifications.

Sofia Ivanova: runtime lag back to ~22ms p95. Healthy.

Rafael Mendes: looks good. End game day scenario. Action item: we should consider lowering default critical threshold for chat route group or making it workload-shape aware (long prompts). Also check doc note: earlier critical increases 429s but stabilizes tail latency.

Talia Benson: got it. I’ll file follow-up in Linear and link this thread + the before/after graphs.

incident-bot: :white_check_mark: **GAME DAY / SIMULATED INCIDENT ENDED**
Outcome summary requested:
- Enterprise SLO protected? (Y/N)
- Best-effort behavior predictable? (Y/N)
- Observability sufficient? (Y/N)
- Notable issues / follow-ups

Rafael Mendes: Y / Y / mostly Y. Follow-ups: evaluate default `queue_depth_critical` for chat; add runbook note on p99 sensitivity; double-check quota vs overload messaging for gold so oncall doesn’t misread.

Aisha Bello: +1. Also consider alert threshold tweak: `shed_rate_high` could include a “critical mode expected” suppression to reduce noise during planned tests.

Logan Wright: telemetry looks good. No cardinality regressions. I’ll add a panel for “mode transitions” so we can correlate threshold changes with p99 shifts.

Elena Popov: I’ll propose default critical=12 for chat in us-east/us-west with a guardrail doc. Needs sign-off from SRE + product.

Amara Diallo: docs-wise, gold quota 429 vs overload 429 is clear in headers; maybe add support macro snippet explicitly calling out `x-redwood-limit-reason`.

Talia Benson: wrapping notes. Thanks all.

source.messages:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: Incident opened by @Rafael Mendes (sev=2) — "Shed rates spiking after config change; elevated 429s on protected route groups" runbook: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/x/runbook-overload-protection

Rafael Mendes: heads up, seeing a sudden jump in shed on us-east right after a config push. best-effort shed_rate went from ~8-12% to ~55% in <5m. queue depth doesn’t look that bad.

Aisha Bello: is enterprise impacted or just best-effort? any burn alerts?

Logan Wright: looking at Grafana now. Hot Route Protection overview (us-east-1): https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/hot-route-protection?var-region=us-east-1
- shed_rate_best_effort on `route_group=chat_completions_v1` ~52%
- shed_rate_best_effort on `route_group=embeddings_v1` ~48%
- enterprise 429s non-zero (0.6-0.9%) which is unusual
- 5xx still low

Rafael Mendes: enterprise 429s is the scary part. started ~2-3 mins after last config reload.

incident-bot: Deploy-bot: config apply succeeded — artifact `routing-config@2025-11-13T19:02Z` in us-east-1. Change: https://deploy.redwoodinference.com/changes/routing-config-17629988

Peter Holtz: on-call for routing. checking admission controller logs. seeing rejects with reason `OVERLOAD_BUDGET_EXHAUSTED` not `QUEUE_DEPTH`.

Mina Haddad: budget exhausted suggests policy/budget calc, not actual fleet pressure. did we change per-route budgets or tier defaults?

Benji Okafor: flag rollout earlier today included config migration for route groups + tier defaults. did a follow-up tweak ~20m ago to "align enterprise budget %".

Jordan Lee: pulling policy-engine decision logs. do we have a request id sample from enterprise 429?

Amara Diallo: gateway view: i’m seeing 429 payloads with `x-redwood-rate-limit-reason: overload_budget_exhausted` + `retry-after: 1`. sample:
```
HTTP/2 429
x-redwood-rate-limit-reason: overload_budget_exhausted
x-redwood-priority-class: enterprise
{"error":{"type":"throttled","code":"overload_budget_exhausted","message":"Temporarily unable to serve request under current capacity policy. Please retry."}}
```

Aisha Bello: `priority-class: enterprise` getting that reason is definitely wrong unless we’re truly out of enterprise budget, which shouldn’t happen with these demand levels.

Logan Wright: correlating with capacity signals: GPU util ~81-86%, scheduler lag steady ~9-12ms, queue depth not elevated. doesn’t look like real overload.

Irene Choi: infra confirms: no GPU pool saturation event. autoscaler stable. no noisy-neighbor spike.

Rafael Mendes: ok so likely a config/policy regression causing enterprise budget=too low.

Jordan Lee: found something. decision log snippet (sanitized):
```
route_group=chat_completions_v1
priority=enterprise
budget.total_rps=120
budget.used_rps=118
budget.window=1s
result=REJECT reason=OVERLOAD_BUDGET_EXHAUSTED
```
120 rps enterprise budget for chat in us-east is wildly low. should be in the thousands.

Mina Haddad: yeah that’s basically "always exhausted" at baseline.

Benji Okafor: that sounds like we accidentally applied best-effort default to enterprise tier or clobbered the per-route override.

Peter Holtz: checking the live config blob on routing:
- `route_group: chat_completions_v1`
- `tier_defaults.enterprise.rps: 120`
- `tier_defaults.best_effort.rps: 90`
this is… not right. enterprise default rps got set to a placeholder value.

Sergio Costa: dashboard annotations show config change at 19:02Z and shed spike immediately after. looks causal.

Elena Popov: can confirm routing is respecting that budget. admission is cutting enterprise once it hits ~120 rps. explains why queue depth looks fine.

Rafael Mendes: immediate mitigation: roll back the config artifact. benji/peter can you revert to previous routing-config version?

Benji Okafor: yep, rolling back to `routing-config@2025-11-13T18:41Z` now. ETA 2-3m. (reactions: :thumbsup: from Rafael Mendes)

incident-bot: Change initiated by @Benji Okafor — rollback `routing-config@2025-11-13T19:02Z` -> `routing-config@2025-11-13T18:41Z` (us-east-1) https://deploy.redwoodinference.com/changes/routing-config-rollback-17629996

Amara Diallo: note: gateway caches tier limits for 30s. after rollback we might need to wait a minute for headers/behavior to normalize.

Jordan Lee: root cause candidate: config migration PR that introduced tier defaults for route groups. might have shipped with a test value in enterprise defaults. anyone know which PR touched this?

Benji Okafor: likely from the config migration work. referencing: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/27512 and a small follow-up config-only change (not PR) applied via admin tool.

Peter Holtz: also could be related to the allowlist/canary tooling; the admin tool UI might have the wrong unit label (rps vs rps_per_shard). but right now it’s clearly applying 120 global.

Logan Wright: monitoring after rollback start: enterprise 429s already trending down slightly.

Vanessa Ortiz: fleet side unchanged, so if 429 drops we can be confident it was policy config.

incident-bot: Rollback applied successfully in us-east-1. Config version now `routing-config@2025-11-13T18:41Z`.

Logan Wright: post-rollback (2m):
- enterprise 429s back to ~0.02% (quota-only)
- best-effort shed_rate down to ~11-14%
- latency unchanged (good)

Aisha Bello: nice. can we verify no other regions have the bad config artifact?

Benji Okafor: checking deploy history. looks like only us-east got the 19:02Z config (manual apply). eu-west still on 18:41Z.

Rafael Mendes: great. keep incident open 10-15m to confirm stable.

Owen Phillips: Support ping: one enterprise customer asked about brief 429s. i’ll respond w/ “configuration issue, rolled back, stable now” and ask for request ids if they continue.

Amara Diallo: if anyone sees lingering `overload_budget_exhausted` on enterprise after rollback, share request ids. could be cache or another path.

Jordan Lee: follow-ups:
1) lock down admin tool so tier default changes require review + validation (no placeholder values)
2) add config lint: enterprise tier defaults must be >= min baseline for protected routes
3) add alert: sudden drop in enterprise budget / spike in `OVERLOAD_BUDGET_EXHAUSTED` for enterprise

Peter Holtz: +1. also we should annotate dashboards with “policy budget rejects by tier” and page only when enterprise sees it.

Sergio Costa: i can add a panel + alert rule in the dashboards pack. will link it to this incident.

Rafael Mendes: status: mitigated via rollback. staying open until we have 15m green on enterprise SLO panel.

Logan Wright: 15m view is green. enterprise p99 normal. no continued 429 anomalies.

incident-bot: Incident resolved (sev=2). Owner @Rafael Mendes. Summary: config regression set enterprise route budgets too low, triggering `OVERLOAD_BUDGET_EXHAUSTED` rejects; rollback restored expected behavior. Follow-ups to be filed in Linear.

Sergio Costa: i can add a panel + alert rule in the dashboards pack. will link it to this incident.

Rafael Mendes: status: mitigated via rollback. staying open until we have 15m green on enterprise SLO panel.

Logan Wright: 15m view is green. enterprise p99 normal. no continued 429 anomalies.

incident-bot: Incident resolved (sev=2). Owner @Rafael Mendes. Summary: config regression set enterprise route budgets too low, triggering `OVERLOAD_BUDGET_EXHAUSTED` rejects; rollback restored expected behavior. Follow-ups to be filed in Linear.
text:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: Incident opened by @Rafael Mendes (sev=2) — "Shed rates spiking after config change; elevated 429s on protected route groups" runbook: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/x/runbook-overload-protection

Rafael Mendes: heads up, seeing a sudden jump in shed on us-east right after a config push. best-effort shed_rate went from ~8-12% to ~55% in <5m. queue depth doesn’t look that bad.

Aisha Bello: is enterprise impacted or just best-effort? any burn alerts?

Logan Wright: looking at Grafana now. Hot Route Protection overview (us-east-1): https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/hot-route-protection?var-region=us-east-1
- shed_rate_best_effort on `route_group=chat_completions_v1` ~52%
- shed_rate_best_effort on `route_group=embeddings_v1` ~48%
- enterprise 429s non-zero (0.6-0.9%) which is unusual
- 5xx still low

Rafael Mendes: enterprise 429s is the scary part. started ~2-3 mins after last config reload.

incident-bot: Deploy-bot: config apply succeeded — artifact `routing-config@2025-11-13T19:02Z` in us-east-1. Change: https://deploy.redwoodinference.com/changes/routing-config-17629988

Peter Holtz: on-call for routing. checking admission controller logs. seeing rejects with reason `OVERLOAD_BUDGET_EXHAUSTED` not `QUEUE_DEPTH`.

Mina Haddad: budget exhausted suggests policy/budget calc, not actual fleet pressure. did we change per-route budgets or tier defaults?

Benji Okafor: flag rollout earlier today included config migration for route groups + tier defaults. did a follow-up tweak ~20m ago to "align enterprise budget %".

Jordan Lee: pulling policy-engine decision logs. do we have a request id sample from enterprise 429?

Amara Diallo: gateway view: i’m seeing 429 payloads with `x-redwood-rate-limit-reason: overload_budget_exhausted` + `retry-after: 1`. sample:
```
HTTP/2 429
x-redwood-rate-limit-reason: overload_budget_exhausted
x-redwood-priority-class: enterprise
{"error":{"type":"throttled","code":"overload_budget_exhausted","message":"Temporarily unable to serve request under current capacity policy. Please retry."}}
```

Aisha Bello: `priority-class: enterprise` getting that reason is definitely wrong unless we’re truly out of enterprise budget, which shouldn’t happen with these demand levels.

Logan Wright: correlating with capacity signals: GPU util ~81-86%, scheduler lag steady ~9-12ms, queue depth not elevated. doesn’t look like real overload.

Irene Choi: infra confirms: no GPU pool saturation event. autoscaler stable. no noisy-neighbor spike.

Rafael Mendes: ok so likely a config/policy regression causing enterprise budget=too low.

Jordan Lee: found something. decision log snippet (sanitized):
```
route_group=chat_completions_v1
priority=enterprise
budget.total_rps=120
budget.used_rps=118
budget.window=1s
result=REJECT reason=OVERLOAD_BUDGET_EXHAUSTED
```
120 rps enterprise budget for chat in us-east is wildly low. should be in the thousands.

Mina Haddad: yeah that’s basically "always exhausted" at baseline.

Benji Okafor: that sounds like we accidentally applied best-effort default to enterprise tier or clobbered the per-route override.

Peter Holtz: checking the live config blob on routing:
- `route_group: chat_completions_v1`
- `tier_defaults.enterprise.rps: 120`
- `tier_defaults.best_effort.rps: 90`
this is… not right. enterprise default rps got set to a placeholder value.

Sergio Costa: dashboard annotations show config change at 19:02Z and shed spike immediately after. looks causal.

Elena Popov: can confirm routing is respecting that budget. admission is cutting enterprise once it hits ~120 rps. explains why queue depth looks fine.

Rafael Mendes: immediate mitigation: roll back the config artifact. benji/peter can you revert to previous routing-config version?

Benji Okafor: yep, rolling back to `routing-config@2025-11-13T18:41Z` now. ETA 2-3m. (reactions: :thumbsup: from Rafael Mendes)

incident-bot: Change initiated by @Benji Okafor — rollback `routing-config@2025-11-13T19:02Z` -> `routing-config@2025-11-13T18:41Z` (us-east-1) https://deploy.redwoodinference.com/changes/routing-config-rollback-17629996

Amara Diallo: note: gateway caches tier limits for 30s. after rollback we might need to wait a minute for headers/behavior to normalize.

Jordan Lee: root cause candidate: config migration PR that introduced tier defaults for route groups. might have shipped with a test value in enterprise defaults. anyone know which PR touched this?

Benji Okafor: likely from the config migration work. referencing: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/27512 and a small follow-up config-only change (not PR) applied via admin tool.

Peter Holtz: also could be related to the allowlist/canary tooling; the admin tool UI might have the wrong unit label (rps vs rps_per_shard). but right now it’s clearly applying 120 global.

Logan Wright: monitoring after rollback start: enterprise 429s already trending down slightly.

Vanessa Ortiz: fleet side unchanged, so if 429 drops we can be confident it was policy config.

incident-bot: Rollback applied successfully in us-east-1. Config version now `routing-config@2025-11-13T18:41Z`.

Logan Wright: post-rollback (2m):
- enterprise 429s back to ~0.02% (quota-only)
- best-effort shed_rate down to ~11-14%
- latency unchanged (good)

Aisha Bello: nice. can we verify no other regions have the bad config artifact?

Benji Okafor: checking deploy history. looks like only us-east got the 19:02Z config (manual apply). eu-west still on 18:41Z.

Rafael Mendes: great. keep incident open 10-15m to confirm stable.

Owen Phillips: Support ping: one enterprise customer asked about brief 429s. i’ll respond w/ “configuration issue, rolled back, stable now” and ask for request ids if they continue.

Amara Diallo: if anyone sees lingering `overload_budget_exhausted` on enterprise after rollback, share request ids. could be cache or another path.

Jordan Lee: follow-ups:
1) lock down admin tool so tier default changes require review + validation (no placeholder values)
2) add config lint: enterprise tier defaults must be >= min baseline for protected routes
3) add alert: sudden drop in enterprise budget / spike in `OVERLOAD_BUDGET_EXHAUSTED` for enterprise

Peter Holtz: +1. also we should annotate dashboards with “policy budget rejects by tier” and page only when enterprise sees it.

Sergio Costa: i can add a panel + alert rule in the dashboards pack. will link it to this incident.

Rafael Mendes: status: mitigated via rollback. staying open until we have 15m green on enterprise SLO panel.

Logan Wright: 15m view is green. enterprise p99 normal. no continued 429 anomalies.

incident-bot: Incident resolved (sev=2). Owner @Rafael Mendes. Summary: config regression set enterprise route budgets too low, triggering `OVERLOAD_BUDGET_EXHAUSTED` rejects; rollback restored expected behavior. Follow-ups to be filed in Linear.

source.text:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: New incident created: INC-3187
Severity: Sev2
Impacted tier: Hosted (some Dedicated noisy)
Signal: streaming_disconnect_rate > 2.5% (baseline ~0.3%) in us-east + eu-west
Primary symptoms: elevated client disconnects during streaming; spike in gateway 499/502
Runbook suggestions: Streaming disconnects, Gateway errors

Kira Thompson: I'm IC for now. Seeing customer reports + our synthetic streaming checks failing. Before we get too deep: how are we classifying this in the new taxonomy? I'm torn between "Client/network" vs "Gateway".

Rafael Mendes: For v1 taxonomy, we *should* be able to pick a primary even if it's ambiguous. Let's start with: do we see disconnects initiated by client (499) vs server resets (502/504)?

Allison Grant: Quick numbers from dashboards (last 20m):
- `edge_http_status{route="/v1/chat/completions",stream="true"}`: 499 up 4-5x, 502 up ~2x
- `edge_upstream_reset_reason`: spikes in `connection_termination` and `upstream_timeout`
- Runtime `stream_abort` counter looks flat
Grafana: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/edge-streaming/overview?from=now-1h

Mina Haddad: Routing looks clean. No change in fallback rate + no capacity events. Router logs show normal completion rates. This doesn't smell like smart routing.

Kira Thompson: So 499 could be client bailing. But the upstream_timeout signal points at us.

Victor Alvarez: Any region-level network blips? I'm checking `northstar` for edge<->gateway link errors.

Aisha Bello: Taxonomy decision tree we agreed on in workshop: if we have server-side evidence (timeouts/resets) and it's not obviously customer-side, classify as Streaming > Disconnect spike (subtype by where).
If we later prove it's customer SDK or a single customer NAT issue, we can reclassify.

Devon Price: Reminder: incident-bot modal now wants `incident_type` + `subtype` before we mark mitigated. You can set now and change later. `/incident update` → fields.

Kira Thompson: ok. Proposed type: "Streaming disconnects". Subtype options again? (I don't have the list handy)

Rafael Mendes: From the v1 draft: Streaming > Disconnects includes:
- Client-initiated disconnects (client cancels / network drop)
- Gateway/edge resets/timeouts
- Upstream runtime stream aborts
Given Allison’s upstream_timeout + reset reasons, I'd pick "Gateway/edge resets/timeouts".

Sofia Ivanova: Runtime side: I pulled a sample of request_ids from incident-bot context. Runtime is happily generating; no OOM spikes, no scheduler stalls, no increase in `stream_write_error`. Latency p95 on runtime is steady.
If clients are getting cut off, it’s likely before/after runtime, not runtime itself.

Victor Alvarez: Network view: no backbone incident. But I do see a rise in `tcp_retransmits` on the edge->gateway path in us-east only. Not huge, but correlated. Could still be timeout tuning though.

Ethan Park: Did we ship anything on edge/gateway recently? I see a config rollout to envoy in us-east about ~45m ago (connection idle timeout adjustments for non-streaming routes).
Change log: CP-CHANGE-11982.

Allison Grant: That aligns with the time the disconnect_rate alert fired.
Also: 502s are disproportionately on longer streams (>60s), not short ones.

Kira Thompson: ok, that’s pretty strong. Who owns mitigation? Platform control plane vs Infra? (I don’t want to page the wrong folks.)

Rafael Mendes: Primary should be whoever can change/rollback the gateway/edge config quickly. That sounds like Platform Control Plane / Edge team. Infra/Network as secondary/consult.

Dev Patel: Support is already getting pings from a couple Hosted customers saying "stream stops mid-response". If we classify as gateway issue, can we tell them "we’re seeing elevated disconnects at the gateway" without over-claiming root cause?

Rafael Mendes: Yes, keep it symptom-based. "We are investigating elevated streaming disconnects at our edge/gateway layer; mitigation in progress".

Mina Haddad: +1 to Platform owning. Routing has nothing actionable here.

Victor Alvarez: :+1: I can stay engaged to confirm if we need to tune retries/timeouts or if there’s a region-specific packet loss issue, but rollback is fastest.

Kira Thompson: Ok I’m setting taxonomy now.

Kira Thompson: /incident update INC-3187 incident_type="Streaming: disconnect spike" subtype="Gateway/edge resets/timeouts" owner="Platform Control Plane" secondary="Infra/Network" 

incident-bot: Updated INC-3187
incident_type: Streaming: disconnect spike
subtype: Gateway/edge resets/timeouts
Primary owner: Platform Control Plane
Secondary: Infra/Network
SLA timer: started (Sev2 Hosted)

Ethan Park: I’ll ping oncall for control plane/edge. Proposed mitigation: rollback CP-CHANGE-11982 in us-east first, then eu-west if needed. Canary monitor: `streaming_disconnect_rate` + `edge_upstream_timeout_total`.

Allison Grant: Watching metrics. If this is the idle timeout change, expect disconnects to drop within 5-10 min of rollback.

Sofia Ivanova: If rollback doesn't move the needle, we should look at client SDK behavior (recent SDK release?) as contributing factor, but agree gateway first.

Devon Price: FYI if you end up thinking it’s primarily client SDK regression, taxonomy says keep Streaming as primary and add contributing_system="SDK" in postmortem fields.

Victor Alvarez: Also note: 499 can still be caused by our side closing the upstream and client surfacing it as cancel. Don’t over-index on 499 alone.

Kira Thompson: Edge oncall acknowledged. Rolling back now.

incident-bot: deploy-bot: Rollback initiated: envoy-config us-east (CP-CHANGE-11982) by @edge-oncall

Allison Grant: 7m post-rollback: disconnect_rate down from 2.8% → 0.9% and falling. 502s also dropping.

Kira Thompson: Marking mitigated once it stabilizes <0.5%.

Aisha Bello: Nice. For the taxonomy question: this is a good example for the decision tree doc—"client vs gateway" ambiguity but we used upstream_timeout/reset reasons + deploy correlation.

Dev Patel: I’ll update the customer threads: "Mitigation applied (gateway config rollback); monitoring".

Ethan Park: Root cause likely the envoy idle timeout change applying to streaming responses (oops). Need a follow-up to add an explicit streaming override + tests.

Kira Thompson: Metrics look back to baseline. Declaring mitigated.

incident-bot: INC-3187 status updated: Mitigated
Next steps: confirm steady-state 30m; decide if Sev2 → resolved; create follow-up ticket for guardrails

Rafael Mendes: Before closure: please keep classification as-is, and in postmortem add contributing factor: config rollout / validation gap. This is exactly why taxonomy wants "where" not "why".

Kira Thompson: :thumbsup: Will do. Keeping type Streaming/Gateway. Will link postmortem placeholder before closing.

source.text:
incident-bot: 🚨 New incident created by Rafael Mendes
Severity: Sev2
Service: Hosted API
Symptom: p95 latency increased (chat.completions)
Detected by: latency-slo-burn (prod-us-east)
War room: #inc-1762721188
Dashboard: https://console.redwood.ai/ops/dashboards/latency?region=us-east

Rafael Mendes: seeing p95 jump from ~1.2s -> 3.6s in us-east over last 15m. error rate flat. anyone else seeing this / is this real user impact vs telemetry?

Sergio Costa: looking at ops dashboard now. p95 and p99 both up. p50 barely moved. looks like tail latency issue.

Allison Grant: quick check: do we trust the p95 panel? that panel is from `gateway_request_latency_ms` histogram (was changed last week). there was a bucket config change.

Rafael Mendes: good call. can someone sanity check with traces or client-side metrics?

Sergio Costa: tracing sample: https://console.redwood.ai/ops/traces?query=route:chat.completions%20region:us-east
I’m seeing some requests with long “runtime_queue_wait” spans (~1.8s) on a subset.

Sofia Ivanova: if queue_wait is up, that’s more like runtime scheduler or batching pressure than telemetry. do we have a corresponding increase in runtime saturation or kv cache evictions?

Noah Patel: pulling runtime metrics now. `runtime_scheduler_stall_seconds` looks noisy but not clearly spiking. `kv_cache_evictions_total` up ~20% but that’s within normal variance.

Allison Grant: another angle: compare with `client_observed_latency_ms` (SDK beacon) and `edge_to_gateway_latency_ms`. if those are flat, could be instrumentation bug.

Rafael Mendes: not setting yet. still deciding whether this is runtime perf regression vs telemetry false positive.

source.text:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: *INC-48219* created by @Rafael Mendes
Severity: *Sev1*
Tier: *Hosted*
Region: *us-east-1*
Customer impact: elevated 5xx + timeouts on /v1/chat/completions

Quick links:
• Grafana (API overview): https://grafana.redwoodinference.internal/d/ri-api-overview?var=region=us-east-1
• Grafana (Router): https://grafana.redwoodinference.internal/d/ri-router?var=region=us-east-1
• Grafana (Fleet capacity): https://grafana.redwoodinference.internal/d/ri-fleet-capacity?var=region=us-east-1
• Runbook (Overload/5xx): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGSRE/Runbook+-+Overload+and+5xx
• Runbook (Region capacity/failover): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGINFRA/Runbook+-+Region+Failover+and+Capacity

*Incident taxonomy (required):* incident_type + subtype
• Decision tree: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGSRE/Incident+taxonomy+v1+definitions

Signals detected (last 10m):
• `request_error_rate{code=5xx}` up (baseline 0.2% -> 6.8%)
• `router_fallback_rate` up (baseline 1% -> 22%)
• `gpu_utilization` > 95% in 3 pools

Suggested classification (auto): `capacity.fleet_shortage` / `regional_saturation` (confidence: 0.74)
To apply: `/incident set-type capacity.fleet_shortage regional_saturation`

Rafael Mendes: IC here. seeing 5xx spike + queueing. can someone sanity check if this is capacity vs router doing something dumb?

Aisha Bello: Looking at API overview now. p95 latency also climbing hard (1.2s -> 6-8s) and then errors. smell is overload.

Vanessa Ortiz: Fleet dashboard shows us-east A100 pool at 99% util + pending queue. provision backlog increasing. leaning capacity.

Mina Haddad: Router dashboard: fallback_reason mostly `capacity` not `health`. not seeing weird policy flips. agree capacity primary.

incident-bot: Update: 5xx sustained > 5% for 12 minutes.
Top affected routes:
• /v1/chat/completions (82% of errors)
• /v1/embeddings (11% of errors)

Current router fallback reasons (last 5m):
• capacity: 78%
• timeout: 16%
• policy: 4%
• health: 2%

Dylan Brooks: +1. also note the fallback is happening but we’re still hitting timeouts after fallback. might be that both pools are saturated.

Harish Reddy: capacity note: there was a scheduled maintenance on 1 rack in us-east, we’re running tighter than usual. can accelerate bringing us-east spare pool online but ~20-30 min.

Rafael Mendes: ok applying taxonomy as suggested. 

Rafael Mendes: `/incident set-type capacity.fleet_shortage regional_saturation`

incident-bot: :label: Classification set
incident_type: *capacity.fleet_shortage*
subtype: *regional_saturation*
Primary owner (resolved): *@Infra/Fleet* (oncall: @Vanessa Ortiz)
Secondary/consulted: *@Routing* (consult), *@SRE* (IC)

Recommended next steps (runbook excerpts):
1) Enable graceful degradation (lower max_tokens, tighten concurrency) if safe
2) Trigger regional burst capacity (fleet)
3) Temporarily relax routing constraints to allow cross-region failover

Runbook links:
• Fleet capacity event: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGINFRA/Fleet+Capacity+Event
• Router failover controls: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGPLAT/Router+Failover+Controls

Vanessa Ortiz: taking owner. starting burst in us-east + shifting some traffic to us-west. also checking if autoscaler stuck.

Kira Thompson: For quick mitigation, runbook says we can lower default `max_concurrent_requests` on the hottest model pool. do we want to do that or prioritize failover?

Mina Haddad: I’d prioritize failover first. If we clamp concurrency we may just increase queuing elsewhere. Router can widen allowable regions for hosted tier.

Rafael Mendes: Mina can you make the router change? keep it minimal + reversible.

Mina Haddad: yep. doing a temporary policy update: allow `us-west-2` for hosted overflow for top 3 models. will post diff.

Mina Haddad: change is in control plane: `routing_policy/hosted-default` -> add `us-west-2` as overflow region w/ cap 20%. ETA 2-3 min.

incident-bot: Control plane change detected (routing policy updated): `routing_policy/hosted-default` by @Mina Haddad
Audit link: https://console.redwoodinference.internal/auditlog?change_id=chg_9f3c2

Dev Patel: heads up Support is getting pings from a couple startups. what should we tell them? is this a generic capacity event in us-east?

Rafael Mendes: yes. message: elevated errors/latency in us-east hosted, mitigation in progress via failover + capacity. avoid details about racks.

Dev Patel: got it. will use the capacity/overload snippet.

Aisha Bello: metrics update: 5xx down to ~3.1% after policy change started rolling. p95 still high but improving.

Vanessa Ortiz: autoscaler wasn’t stuck, just maxed out quota. bringing spare pool online now. ETA 15-20m for meaningful headroom.

incident-bot: Update: `router_fallback_rate` stabilizing (22% -> 9%).
`request_error_rate{5xx}` trending down (6.8% -> 2.9%).
Suggested next check: verify `timeout` fallback_reason isn’t dominating.

Dylan Brooks: timeout fallback_reason is still ~10-12%. suspect queue time > upstream timeout. if we can increase router request timeout temporarily?

Mina Haddad: we can, but risk tying up workers longer. runbook says only do it if queue is draining. i’d wait 5 min.

Rafael Mendes: agree. holding for now. Vanessa ping when spare pool is serving.

Vanessa Ortiz: spare pool started accepting traffic. util in primary pool down to 92%. queue depth dropping.

Aisha Bello: p95 back to 2.4s and 5xx ~0.8%. looks like we’re recovering.

incident-bot: :white_check_mark: Recovery signal detected
• 5xx < 1% for 5 minutes
• p95 latency within 2x baseline

Reminder: before resolving, confirm:
• incident_type/subtype set (done)
• owner confirmed (Fleet)
• add runbook link + mitigation summary

Rafael Mendes: resolving soon if stable another 10. adding summary:
- primary: us-east capacity saturation
- mitigation: allow overflow to us-west + bring spare pool online
- follow-up: investigate tighter capacity headroom during maint window

Rafael Mendes: `/incident resolve` (pending final checks)

incident-bot: Incident marked *Resolved* by @Rafael Mendes.
Please add a postmortem placeholder for Sev1 within 1 business day.
Template (with taxonomy fields): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/POSTMORTEMS/Postmortem+Template

Kira Thompson: I’ll open the PM placeholder + link it here. also adding note to update the capacity runbook with the maint-window gotcha.

Rafael Mendes: thanks. please tag me + Vanessa on PM.

text:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: *INC-48219* created by @Rafael Mendes
Severity: *Sev1*
Tier: *Hosted*
Region: *us-east-1*
Customer impact: elevated 5xx + timeouts on /v1/chat/completions

Quick links:
• Grafana (API overview): https://grafana.redwoodinference.internal/d/ri-api-overview?var=region=us-east-1
• Grafana (Router): https://grafana.redwoodinference.internal/d/ri-router?var=region=us-east-1
• Grafana (Fleet capacity): https://grafana.redwoodinference.internal/d/ri-fleet-capacity?var=region=us-east-1
• Runbook (Overload/5xx): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGSRE/Runbook+-+Overload+and+5xx
• Runbook (Region capacity/failover): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGINFRA/Runbook+-+Region+Failover+and+Capacity

*Incident taxonomy (required):* incident_type + subtype
• Decision tree: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGSRE/Incident+taxonomy+v1+definitions

Signals detected (last 10m):
• `request_error_rate{code=5xx}` up (baseline 0.2% -> 6.8%)
• `router_fallback_rate` up (baseline 1% -> 22%)
• `gpu_utilization` > 95% in 3 pools

Suggested classification (auto): `capacity.fleet_shortage` / `regional_saturation` (confidence: 0.74)
To apply: `/incident set-type capacity.fleet_shortage regional_saturation`

Rafael Mendes: IC here. seeing 5xx spike + queueing. can someone sanity check if this is capacity vs router doing something dumb?

Aisha Bello: Looking at API overview now. p95 latency also climbing hard (1.2s -> 6-8s) and then errors. smell is overload.

Vanessa Ortiz: Fleet dashboard shows us-east A100 pool at 99% util + pending queue. provision backlog increasing. leaning capacity.

Mina Haddad: Router dashboard: fallback_reason mostly `capacity` not `health`. not seeing weird policy flips. agree capacity primary.

incident-bot: Update: 5xx sustained > 5% for 12 minutes.
Top affected routes:
• /v1/chat/completions (82% of errors)
• /v1/embeddings (11% of errors)

Current router fallback reasons (last 5m):
• capacity: 78%
• timeout: 16%
• policy: 4%
• health: 2%

Dylan Brooks: +1. also note the fallback is happening but we’re still hitting timeouts after fallback. might be that both pools are saturated.

Harish Reddy: capacity note: there was a scheduled maintenance on 1 rack in us-east, we’re running tighter than usual. can accelerate bringing us-east spare pool online but ~20-30 min.

Rafael Mendes: ok applying taxonomy as suggested. 

Rafael Mendes: `/incident set-type capacity.fleet_shortage regional_saturation`

incident-bot: :label: Classification set
incident_type: *capacity.fleet_shortage*
subtype: *regional_saturation*
Primary owner (resolved): *@Infra/Fleet* (oncall: @Vanessa Ortiz)
Secondary/consulted: *@Routing* (consult), *@SRE* (IC)

Recommended next steps (runbook excerpts):
1) Enable graceful degradation (lower max_tokens, tighten concurrency) if safe
2) Trigger regional burst capacity (fleet)
3) Temporarily relax routing constraints to allow cross-region failover

Runbook links:
• Fleet capacity event: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGINFRA/Fleet+Capacity+Event
• Router failover controls: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGPLAT/Router+Failover+Controls

Vanessa Ortiz: taking owner. starting burst in us-east + shifting some traffic to us-west. also checking if autoscaler stuck.

Kira Thompson: For quick mitigation, runbook says we can lower default `max_concurrent_requests` on the hottest model pool. do we want to do that or prioritize failover?

Mina Haddad: I’d prioritize failover first. If we clamp concurrency we may just increase queuing elsewhere. Router can widen allowable regions for hosted tier.

Rafael Mendes: Mina can you make the router change? keep it minimal + reversible.

source.messages:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: Incident opened by @Rafael Mendes — "Burst traffic on /v1/chat/completions; best-effort throttling active; verify enterprise SLO stability" (sev=2) runbook: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/x/runbook-overload-protection

Rafael Mendes: seeing spike in req/s on us-east-1 chat. tail latency climbing but 5xx still low. we *are* shedding best-effort pretty hard. need to confirm enterprise routes still meeting SLO.

Logan Wright: pulling dashboards now. looking at Hot Route Protection overview (us-east) + tier breakdown.

Aisha Bello: any customer-facing impact? Support pinged me about increased 429s in self-serve.

Rafael Mendes: yes expected for best-effort. need to make sure 429 reasons are consistent + we’re not accidentally throttling enterprise.

Peter Holtz: on routing side, admission control kicked in at ~18% scheduler lag threshold (config default). best-effort should be gated first, enterprise budget preserved.

Mina Haddad: confirm which route group? is it `route_group=chat_completions_v1`? we had a config migration last week.

Elena Popov: checking routing logs. seeing policy decisions: `priority=enterprise` admitted, `priority=best_effort` rejected w/ reason `OVERLOAD_ADMISSION_CONTROL`. looks correct so far.

Amara Diallo: gateway 429 payloads should include `x-redwood-rate-limit-reason`. can someone paste a sample from a client report?

Owen Phillips: from support ticket snippet (self-serve):
```
HTTP/2 429
x-redwood-request-id: req_9c3...
x-redwood-rate-limit-reason: overload_admission_control
retry-after: 2
{"error":{"type":"throttled","code":"overload_admission_control","message":"Service is under load. Please retry with backoff."}}
```

Amara Diallo: looks right (code matches). retry-after present. good.

Logan Wright: metrics snapshot (us-east-1, last 10m):
- /v1/chat/completions p95 latency enterprise: 820ms (SLO 1200ms)
- p99 enterprise: 1.9s (SLO 2.5s)
- 5xx enterprise: 0.12%
- 429 enterprise: 0.03% (mostly quota)
- 429 best_effort: 12.4% (overload)
- shed_rate_best_effort sustained ~10-15%

Rafael Mendes: perfect. keep watching p99 + 5xx for enterprise.

Irene Choi: infra view: GPU util in us-east pool is ~94-97% and queue depth oscillating. autoscaler already adding 6 nodes but lead time ~8-10m.

Vanessa Ortiz: fleet capacity signal says queue depth > threshold in `gpu-pool-hosted-a100-ue1` for 14m. not a single node issue; it’s aggregate pressure.

Mina Haddad: do we know source of burst? single tenant / integration?

Elena Popov: top talkers by tenant (routing sampled logs):
- tenant_4b2… (self-serve) ~3.1k rps, long prompts
- tenant_a1f… (self-serve) ~1.4k rps
enterprise tenants stable at normal baseline.

Aisha Bello: if it’s long prompts, could be driving kv pressure + scheduler lag. do we want to tighten best-effort admission earlier (lower threshold) to protect enterprise even more?

Peter Holtz: careful: lowering threshold increases 429s (expected) but might reduce tail latency further. we can do a temporary config change: `admission_control.best_effort.shed_at_queue_depth=Q` down by ~10%.

Rafael Mendes: ok let’s do minimal change. goal is keep enterprise stable, not necessarily reduce best-effort 429s.

Logan Wright: FYI burn-rate alert not firing for enterprise SLO; best-effort has no paging. we’re good on alert hygiene.

Amara Diallo: gateway is healthy; policy eval latency p95 0.7ms. no sign of policy cache misses causing overhead.

Rafael Mendes: action plan:
1) keep current shed behavior, but add one mitigation: cap self-serve burst for chat route group in us-east.
2) ensure incident-bot posts to #support if customer asks.

Owen Phillips: i can drop a note in support thread w/ reason code + retry guidance and that it’s expected during load.

Rafael Mendes: do it.

Peter Holtz: i’ll apply routing config override for us-east only, best-effort burst down 15%, TTL 60m. @Mina Haddad can you sanity check in config repo?

Mina Haddad: yep. paste diff here when ready.

Peter Holtz: proposed override:
```
route_group: chat_completions_v1
region: us-east-1
priority_class: best_effort
limits:
  rps_burst: 800   # was 950
  queue_depth_shed_threshold: 0.85  # was 0.95
expires_at: 1762399100
reason: "incident mitigation - protect enterprise SLO during burst"
```

Mina Haddad: looks fine. make sure it’s behind the incident allowlist key so it doesn’t persist.

Elena Popov: once applied, we should watch for unintended spillover to embeddings/rerank pools. routing should keep it scoped but burst could be multi-route.

Irene Choi: autoscaler additional nodes ETA 6m. if demand stays, we’ll stabilize; if it ramps further, we might need to enable regional overflow for best-effort (eu-west fallback) — enterprise is strict no-fallback.

Rafael Mendes: agree. do *not* enable fallback for enterprise.

incident-bot: Change recorded: routing config override requested by @Peter Holtz (pending) — details: https://deploy.redwoodinference.com/changes/ue1-override-17623961

Logan Wright: after override apply (2m window):
- 429 best_effort up to 16% (expected)
- enterprise p99 improved to 1.6s
- queue depth down ~8%

Aisha Bello: cool. any enterprise complaints in the last 30m?

Owen Phillips: none so far. one self-serve customer asked “why 429 now” — we replied w/ retry/backoff + reason code mapping.

Amara Diallo: reminder: quota 429 uses `quota_exceeded` and overload uses `overload_admission_control` / `overload_shed`. if anyone sees mixed casing or missing header, ping me.

Vanessa Ortiz: fleet signal is recovering. scheduler lag now below trigger threshold for 5 consecutive mins.

Rafael Mendes: keep incident open until autoscaler fully catches up + enterprise p99 stable for 15m.

Logan Wright: enterprise SLO panel green, error budget burn < 0.2x. no sustained 5xx.

Rafael Mendes: closing out in ~10m if holds. @Peter Holtz please set reminder to revert override when TTL hits.

Peter Holtz: ack, TTL set + I’ll verify rollback applied.

incident-bot: Update: mitigation in effect (best-effort tightened) — next check in 15m. Owner: @Rafael Mendes. Linked dashboard: https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/hot-route-protection?var-region=us-east-1&var-route=/v1/chat/completions

Rafael Mendes: status: enterprise stable, best-effort throttled as designed. we’ll follow up w/ traffic source analysis + whether to adjust defaults for long-prompt self-serve patterns.

incident-bot: Change recorded: routing config override requested by @Peter Holtz (pending) — details: https://deploy.redwoodinference.com/changes/ue1-override-17623961

Logan Wright: after override apply (2m window):
- 429 best_effort up to 16% (expected)
- enterprise p99 improved to 1.6s
- queue depth down ~8%

Aisha Bello: cool. any enterprise complaints in the last 30m?

Owen Phillips: none so far. one self-serve customer asked “why 429 now” — we replied w/ retry/backoff + reason code mapping.

Amara Diallo: reminder: quota 429 uses `quota_exceeded` and overload uses `overload_admission_control` / `overload_shed`. if anyone sees mixed casing or missing header, ping me.

Vanessa Ortiz: fleet signal is recovering. scheduler lag now below trigger threshold for 5 consecutive mins.

Rafael Mendes: keep incident open until autoscaler fully catches up + enterprise p99 stable for 15m.

Logan Wright: enterprise SLO panel green, error budget burn < 0.2x. no sustained 5xx.

Rafael Mendes: closing out in ~10m if holds. @Peter Holtz please set reminder to revert override when TTL hits.

Peter Holtz: ack, TTL set + I’ll verify rollback applied.

incident-bot: Update: mitigation in effect (best-effort tightened) — next check in 15m. Owner: @Rafael Mendes. Linked dashboard: https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/hot-route-protection?var-region=us-east-1&var-route=/v1/chat/completions

Rafael Mendes: status: enterprise stable, best-effort throttled as designed. we’ll follow up w/ traffic source analysis + whether to adjust defaults for long-prompt self-serve patterns.

source.messages:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: Incident opened: INC-3197
Severity: Sev1
Impacted: Hosted (us-east-1, us-west-2) + some Dedicated spillover
Symptoms: 5xx spike + elevated p95 latency. Streaming errors elevated.
Primary service(s): router-gateway, serving
Dashboards: https://console.redwood.dev/ops/incidents/INC-3197 | https://grafana.redwood/routers/us-east-1

PagerDuty: @Rafael Mendes you have been paged (SRE Oncall). Ack required.

Rafael Mendes: ack. I’m IC. Can someone grab comms + someone grab routing triage?

Dev Patel: I can do comms and keep Support looped. Starting internal blurb.

Mina Haddad: I’m on routing triage. Looking at fallback + policy stats now.

Vanessa Ortiz: Fleet here. I’m looking at capacity signals (GPU sat, queue depth, provisioning lag) for us-east-1.

Aisha Bello: Pulling incident taxonomy decision tree + SLO impact. Will post suggestions for incident_type once we have root symptom.

Rafael Mendes: Thanks. Quick question for taxonomy: is this shaping up as Routing/Fallback Misbehavior vs Capacity/Fleet Shortage? Need to set incident_type in bot before we get too far.

incident-bot: Reminder: incident_type + subtype is required. Use `/incident set-type`.

Rafael Mendes: yep, but don’t want to misclassify. Let’s get 5 min of signals.

Mina Haddad: Router dashboard: fallback_rate jumped from 2% -> 28% in us-east-1 at ~T0. fallback_reason breakdown looks like mostly `capacity` and some `health_timeout`.

Mina Haddad: Also seeing elevated `route_selection_time_ms` p95. Not sure if cause or effect (queueing).

Vanessa Ortiz: Fleet: GPU utilization is pinned (98-100%) on the primary pool `hosted-a100-east` and queue depth spiked. Provisioning is behind by ~15-20m (spot reclamations earlier).

Harish Reddy: Confirm. Autoscaler is requesting +120 GPUs, cloud provider is returning partial fulfill. We also hit a per-AZ quota wall in us-east-1c.

Rafael Mendes: So primary is likely capacity shortage.

Mina Haddad: Agree capacity is real, but I’m worried router is thrashing between pools. We’re seeing repeated retries across regions; effective request amplification.

Ravi Menon: Jumping in. The router’s fallback policy for Hosted is supposed to degrade to smaller model in-region before cross-region. Are we skipping that?

Mina Haddad: Looks like it’s going cross-region more than expected. For requests pinned to `rw-chat-xxl`, it’s failing the “compatible variants” check and jumping straight to us-west-2.

Sofia Ivanova: If it’s pinned to model version that has no smaller compatible variant, cross-region might be intended. Do we know if the traffic mix changed?

Aisha Bello: From a classification standpoint: if fleet saturation is the initiating event, taxonomy should be `Capacity/Fleet Shortage` with routing issues as contributing factor. If router incorrectly bypassed intended degrade path (policy bug), then `Routing/Fallback Misbehavior`.

Rafael Mendes: Mina, can you confirm whether router behavior deviates from configured policy? Or is it doing the best it can given no capacity?

Mina Haddad: Checking control plane config snapshot…

Mina Haddad: Found something: Hosted policy updated yesterday to prefer `region=us-east-1` but allow immediate cross-region when `queue_depth > 200` (new guardrail). That’s firing hard. So cross-region surge is expected.

Ravi Menon: That would be “policy does what it says” not misbehavior. Still, the `queue_depth` threshold might be too low and creates oscillation.

Vanessa Ortiz: Underlying shortage is the driver. If we had capacity, none of this would trigger.

Harish Reddy: Also seeing connection-level timeouts to west when spillover happens. That’s causing retries and more load.

Rafael Mendes: Ok. I’m going to set primary incident_type = Capacity/Fleet Shortage (Hosted) and add routing fallback as contributing system in notes.

incident-bot: Tip: If multiple causes, select the *initiating* category as incident_type and add others under contributing_factors.

Rafael Mendes: running command.

Rafael Mendes: `/incident set-type capacity_fleet_shortage subtype=gpu_pool_saturation`

incident-bot: Updated INC-3197
incident_type: Capacity/Fleet Shortage
subtype: GPU pool saturation / provisioning lag
Primary owner (auto): Infra/Fleet
Secondary/consulted: Smart Routing
Runbooks: https://runbooks.redwood/infra/fleet-capacity-event | https://runbooks.redwood/routing/fallback-and-spillover

Rafael Mendes: @Vanessa Ortiz you’re primary owner per mapping—can you lead mitigation on capacity side?

Vanessa Ortiz: yes. Actions: (1) reduce max tokens for hosted in us-east temporarily, (2) raise autoscaler cap and request quota bump, (3) shift some baseline capacity from staging pool.

Harish Reddy: I can do quota escalation w/ cloud provider. Starting ticket + paging TAM.

Mina Haddad: For routing: we can temporarily increase the `queue_depth` threshold from 200 -> 600 to reduce oscillation + cross-region retry storms.

Ravi Menon: +1, but careful: if threshold too high, we just pile up latency. Maybe add a cap on retry attempts and prefer degrade-to-smaller-model when available.

Sofia Ivanova: Runtime side: not seeing a regression. Error signature looks like upstream timeouts + overload. No action from runtime right now.

Aisha Bello: For SLA timers: Sev1 Hosted mitigation target is 30m. Dedicated spillover might trigger stricter comms depending on contract, but primary tier is Hosted.

Dev Patel: Support already has 3 tickets. Drafting message: “Elevated error rates and latency due to capacity constraints in us-east-1; traffic being routed to alternate capacity; mitigations underway.” Will avoid mentioning internal thresholds.

Dylan Brooks: FYI incident-bot taxonomy suggestion is being logged; if we end up deciding routing policy was wrong, we can update incident_type later. Just make sure we capture the rationale.

Rafael Mendes: Good call. We’ll reassess after mitigation. For now: capacity primary.

Vanessa Ortiz: Implementing token cap now via config. ETA 3-5m.

Mina Haddad: I’m pushing a temporary routing override: limit cross-region retries to 1 and disable “immediate spillover” for pinned versions that lack compatible variants. Will post diff.

Mina Haddad: ```
change_id: cp-override-7712
router.policy.hosted.queue_depth_spillover_threshold: 600
router.retries.max_cross_region: 1
router.fallback.allow_cross_region_if_no_variant: true (unchanged)
router.fallback.prefer_variant_degrade: true (unchanged)
```

Ravi Menon: Looks sane. Ensure rollback plan in case west gets hammered.

Vanessa Ortiz: Metrics moving: us-east 5xx down from 7.8% -> 3.1% after token cap. Queue depth still high but stabilizing.

Aisha Bello: Confirm SLO burn rate improving. Still violating latency SLO but trending down.

Rafael Mendes: Great. Keep posting. We’ll update incident notes: primary driver fleet saturation; routing guardrail contributed to blast radius but behaved per config.

Dev Patel: Posted customer-facing update draft in #support-escalations for review.

incident-bot: Status check: please update timeline notes + mitigation actions. Use `/incident note`.

Rafael Mendes: `/incident note Capacity saturation in us-east-1 hosted pool; provisioning lag + AZ quota. Routing spillover increased cross-region traffic per policy; temporary threshold adjustment and retry caps applied.`

incident-bot: Note added to INC-3197.

Vanessa Ortiz: Quota request submitted. Also shifted 40 GPUs from low-priority pool; should show up in ~10m.

Mina Haddad: Spillover rate down to 12% from 28%. West latency stable.

Rafael Mendes: Given this, taxonomy stands as capacity shortage. If postmortem finds router policy bug, we’ll reclassify and tag capacity as contributing.

Aisha Bello: +1. Please remember: if we change incident_type later, add rationale in postmortem so reporting doesn’t look like churn.

Rafael Mendes: Will do. Next check-in in 10m unless things regress.

source.messages:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: SEV1 opened by Rafael Mendes
Summary: Elevated 5xx on embeddings requests (reports mainly us-east). Statuspage currently shows "Hosted API - US East" degraded.
Runbook: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ONCALL/Status+page+update+runbook

Rafael Mendes: seeing a mismatch between what’s failing and what statuspage is showing. Customers + internal dashboards say embeddings only, but component map is tagging it as Hosted API (chat). can someone sanity check component mapping?

Christine Wong: looking. gateway metrics show `route=/v1/embeddings` but status classifier is setting capability=chat. that would explain why the public component flips the wrong one.

Logan Wright: yep, I can reproduce in the component-map service. classifier rule is:
`if model_family in {"text","chat"} -> capability=chat` before route-based override. embeddings requests to text models get caught by that.

Aisha Bello: dashboard confirms: chat latency is fine, embeddings error rate is spiking (us-east). component dashboards are clean for chat.

Ravi Menon: this is in the mapping layer config, not statuspage itself. hotfix should be safe: route should win over model family. do we have tests that cover `/v1/embeddings` + text models?

Logan Wright: not currently. I can add a small unit test + patch. will open PR against redwood repo.

Devon Price: from incident-bot side: current incident intake field shows component=hosted_api.us-east, capability=chat. if we fix mapping, we should also correct the incident tags so the draft updates don’t keep using the wrong component.

Rafael Mendes: +1. immediate need is to correct public component state. Support is getting “status says API is degraded but chat is fine; embeddings is broken”.

Dev Patel: confirming. 6 tickets in last ~15 min saying embeddings failing while status page implies everything (Hosted API) is impacted. would like us to update to the embeddings component ASAP.

Christine Wong: question: should we manually set "Embeddings - US East" to degraded now, even before hotfix lands? or do we worry about anti-flap / automation overriding?

Allison Grant: automation is in “suggest” mode right now for sev1 (per the rollout flag), but the component classifier still feeds the draft text. manual statuspage update is fine; just make sure the incident update text matches and we don’t mention internal misclassification.

Rafael Mendes: ok. Christine can you update statuspage components manually: set "Embeddings (Hosted) - US East" to Degraded Performance and revert "Hosted API - US East" back to Operational unless we see broader impact.

Christine Wong: on it. using statuspage UI now.

Christine Wong: update posted. Components:
- Embeddings (Hosted) - US East: Degraded Performance
- Hosted API - US East: Operational
Update text: “We’re investigating elevated error rates affecting embeddings requests in US East. Other API capabilities appear operational. Next update in 30 min.”

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: thank you.

Logan Wright: PR up: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/19563 (fix misclassified embeddings errors in component map) — route-based capability override happens before model_family mapping + adds tests.

Ravi Menon: reviewing. quick nit: ensure reranking route `/v1/rerank` doesn’t get lumped into embeddings. looks fine.

Aisha Bello: can we also add a guard for streaming? we’ve seen similar "text model" confusion there.

Logan Wright: I scoped to embeddings because that’s the incident, but the rule ordering change is generic. streaming route mapping stays explicit. tests now cover `/v1/embeddings` + text model -> capability=embeddings.

Allison Grant: once merged, please ping me. I want to verify the new component tags land in metrics so the status component dashboards line up again.

Ethan Park: heads up: control-plane signals still show minor scheduler retries but no throughput drop; shouldn’t affect component selection.
