- Docs: add “NetworkPolicy allowlist for Prometheus scrapes” section under Private observability day-2
- Installer: detect default-deny in Redwood namespaces + prompt with example policies
- Smoke tests: include default-deny NP scenario

Sahana Iyer: I’ll take smoke-test scenario. Will add a harness step that applies default-deny in runtime/control-plane namespaces and asserts our “allow-prom scrape” policy restores targets.

Rafael Mendes: thanks. closing incident.

Incident Bot: :white_check_mark: Incident resolved. Please add retro notes + action items to tracking ticket.

source.content:
Incident Bot: :rotating_light: INCIDENT DECLARED: Elevated 5xx in us-east for inference API
Impact: Increased request failures + latency
Suspected component: KMS decrypt path
IC: Tomáš Novák
Scribe: Nadia Rahman
Zoom: https://meet.redwoodinference.com/inc-1746508855

Tomáš Novák: seeing spike starting ~10:58 UTC. `api-gw` 5xx up + p95 latency up. Correlates w/ kms decrypt timeouts in `control-plane` + `dataplane-router`.

Nadia Rahman: pulling dashboards. KMS client span `kms.decrypt` p95 went from ~80ms -> 3-5s in us-east only. Error counters mostly `Timeout` + some `Throttling`.

Sean Gallagher: oncall paged. confirming from pager: API 5xx hitting 2.8% in us-east, other regions fine.

Allison Grant: do we have recent deploy? anything in us-east in last hour?

Connor O'Brien: checking deploy feed. No prod deploys in last 2h to api/control plane. Last was docs + unrelated.

Rafael Mendes: if it’s KMS dependency jitter, need to avoid retry amplification. What are current retry settings on wrapper in us-east?

Ethan Park: wrapper config is `timeout=2s`, retries=3 (exp backoff) for decrypt, total budget *should* cap at 6-7s but looks like callers are also retrying upstream.

Benji Okafor: yep, router has its own retry-on-5xx. That plus KMS retries may be stacking. :grimacing:

Tomáš Novák: immediate mitigation options:
1) lower concurrency / shed load in us-east
2) disable upstream retries for kms errors
3) fail open/closed? (we should fail closed for decrypt, but can short-circuit quickly)

Marcus Lin: for decrypt failures we cannot fail open. But we can fail fast + circuit break to prevent storms. Prefer quick error + fallback region if available.

Sean Gallagher: can we route us-east traffic to us-west for hosted? for dedicated/private depends, but this incident is hosted API mostly.

Ethan Park: we can temporarily increase cross-region routing weight to us-west for tier=hosted. Will increase latency but avoids KMS us-east issues.

Allison Grant: +1. Also clamp retry budgets. We had a near-miss earlier with retry storm; let’s not repeat.

Tomáš Novák: executing: bump routing policy to shift 40% from us-east -> us-west for hosted. Connor can you own change + rollback plan?

Connor O'Brien: on it. making config change in control-plane. will announce when applied.

Nadia Rahman: sharing graphs:
- `redwood.kms.latency_ms{region=us-east,op=decrypt}` p99 6.2s
- `redwood.kms.errors_total{code=Timeout}` spiking 10x
- request rate increased 18% but not crazy.

Rafael Mendes: suggests KMS service degradation or network path. Any AWS Health for KMS us-east?

Sean Gallagher: checking AWS Health Dashboard now.

Support Bot: :information_source: 6 new Zendesk/Jira signals tagged "kms" in last 20m. Example: SUP-19377 mentions intermittent 5xx correlated w/ KMS throttling (previous). No new customer ticket yet for this event.

Benji Okafor: can we feature-flag reduce decrypt retries to 0 for a bit? (still one attempt, fail fast)

Ethan Park: there’s a runtime knob `KMS_MAX_RETRIES`. We can set to 1 in prod via config, but it’s global. For us-east only we can override per-region.

Tomáš Novák: do it for us-east. Set retries=1, timeout maybe 1s? Avoid long hangs.

Marcus Lin: careful w/ too short timeout; could increase failures if KMS is just slow. But current 2s * 3 is too much. I’m ok w/ timeout=1.5s retries=1 for us-east as temporary.

Allison Grant: agree. Also make sure we label this as incident mitigation + follow up with guardrails (this is exactly why we wanted retry budget caps).

Connor O'Brien: routing change applied (hosted us-east weight down, us-west up). Tracking in release thread. ETA effects 2-3 min.

Nadia Rahman: seeing immediate dip in us-east request rate. 5xx still high but starting to trend down.

Sean Gallagher: AWS Health shows "KMS - Increased API Latency" in us-east-1 starting 10:55 UTC. ok external.

Rafael Mendes: good. Next mitigation: retry reduction.

Ethan Park: pushing config override:
`KMS_TIMEOUT_MS=1500`
`KMS_MAX_RETRIES=1`
Region: us-east
Applying now.

Tomáš Novák: comms: I’ll post internal status + customer status page? we usually avoid public unless sustained >15m + customer visible.

Sean Gallagher: we’re at ~12m elevated 5xx. If it persists another 5-10m, we should update status page as degraded performance in us-east.

Hanae Suzuki: any sign of key mismatch / invalid ciphertext? want to ensure not our rollout.

Nadia Rahman: no. errors are almost entirely timeout/throttle. No `InvalidCiphertext`.

Benji Okafor: upstream retries in router: can we disable retry-on-5xx for KMS error category only? currently generic.

Ethan Park: not instantly; needs code change. But we can lower retry count globally for router in us-east. Risk: reduces resilience for other transient errors.

Allison Grant: let’s hold that unless needed. We already shifted traffic + reduced KMS retries.

Connor O'Brien: config override applied. Confirmed in control plane: us-east runtime now timeout 1500ms, retries 1.

Nadia Rahman: metrics 2-3 min later:
- `kms.decrypt` p95 down to ~1.1s (still high)
- timeout errors down ~60%
- API 5xx down from 2.8% -> 0.9% in us-east

Tomáš Novák: nice. keep watching.

Sean Gallagher: we should add status note internally now: AWS KMS latency in us-east; mitigation in place (traffic shift + retry clamp). No evidence of data loss.

Rafael Mendes: :+1: and capture follow-up items: (1) prevent double retry stacking, (2) add throttle budget/backpressure, (3) enforce config guardrails.

Allison Grant: follow-ups should map to:
- PR-18563 hotfix reduce retry storm on timeout (if not already merged)
- PR-18570 throttle budget/backpressure (in progress)
- PR-18502 config guardrails
Also create incident ticket for retro.

Tomáš Novák: agreed. I’ll link as action items at end.

Support Bot: FYI support is drafting macro response if customers ask: "AWS KMS latency in us-east, we shifted traffic and reduced retry amplification; services stabilizing".

Ethan Park: longer-term, router should short-circuit on `KMSDependencyUnavailable` category and trigger region failover quicker.

Marcus Lin: and update runbook to explicitly call out AWS Health check + recommended mitigations (shift region + clamp retries) with thresholds.

Nadia Rahman: capturing logs sample for retro:
```
level=warn msg="kms decrypt failed" code=Timeout op=Decrypt region=us-east latency_ms=1543 attempt=2 request_id=...
level=warn msg="upstream retry" component=router reason="dependency_timeout" retry=1
```

Sean Gallagher: confirms retry stacking. We should ensure wrapper exposes "already retried" marker or caller policy to avoid re-retrying.

Connor O'Brien: are we safe to start rolling back routing weight when AWS recovers? keep some shift until KMS health normal.

Tomáš Novák: keep shift for 30m after stability. We can gradually restore.

Nadia Rahman: current us-east 5xx 0.3% and falling. KMS latency also trending down (p95 420ms).

Sean Gallagher: ok, no status page needed if we’re back under SLO. Let’s hold and monitor.

Incident Bot: :bar_chart: Reminder: post incident updates every 15 minutes.

Tomáš Novák: 15m update: AWS KMS us-east latency event. Mitigation applied (traffic shift + retry clamp). API 5xx down to 0.3% and improving. Monitoring.

Rafael Mendes: any sign of backlog / queue growth due to earlier timeouts?

Nadia Rahman: router queue depth peaked but now normal. No sustained backlog.

Allison Grant: once stable, revert config override to normal? or keep for a day as safety?

Sean Gallagher: I’d keep retries=1 for us-east for rest of day. We’ve seen KMS wobble before. But we should confirm no negative impact.

Marcus Lin: ok as temporary, but document exception + ensure decrypt correctness unaffected (it is). For SOC2 narrative, this is availability tuning, not crypto change.

Benji Okafor: I’ll open a Linear follow-up: disable router retries on KMS error category specifically.

Tomáš Novák: creating incident follow-up tasks:
- Hotfix: reduce retry amplification + add jitter (PR-18563) — owner Ethan/Benji
- Add throttle budget/backpressure (PR-18570) — owner Ethan
- Guardrails for timeout/retry budget (PR-18502) — owner Allison/Nadia sign-off
- Update runbook `kms-decrypt-failures` w/ AWS health + mitigation steps — owner Sean/Tomáš
- Ticket link to SUP-19377 to cross-reference customer history

Connor O'Brien: FYI I’ll note in release log: regional override applied. Will schedule revert checklist for tomorrow.

Nadia Rahman: last datapoint: KMS p95 120ms now. API 5xx 0.08% (baseline-ish). Looks resolved.

Tomáš Novák: :white_check_mark: marking incident as mitigated. Keeping monitoring for 30m and leaving us-east retry clamp in place.

Incident Bot: INCIDENT STATUS -> Monitoring

Sean Gallagher: thanks all. Please drop any notes/screenshots into the retro doc. Also link AWS Health incident id in the writeup.

Marcus Lin: and add note: no evidence of key material mismatch; purely dependency latency.

Incident Bot: :white_check_mark: INCIDENT RESOLVED
Duration: 42m
Primary cause: External dependency degradation (AWS KMS latency) + retry amplification
Next step: schedule retro within 3 business days

Tomáš Novák: closing thread. Retro items already captured above; I’ll file the formal postmortem + link PRs/tickets once merged.

source.messages:
IncidentBot: \u26a0\ufe0f ALERT: Elevated 504/5xx rates observed for Crescent Bank (account_id: cb-402) in us-west-2 on /v1/chat.generate. Triggered 2024-08-01T14:12:02Z
Mira Li: oncall SRE picking up. seeing sustained queue backlog in the serving frontend; p95 latency spiked to 2.1s. Routing shows traffic stuck on pool redwood-dedicated-usw2-2.
Tom Anders: I can see autoscaler failed to add nodes; `autoscaler-controller` logs show `rate limit exceeded` talking to cloud provider quota API. Also some workers stuck in STATUS=RESTARTING.
Priya Natarajan: taking pager. First action: confirm if dedicated pool quota or control-plane throttling. `rdwctl pools describe redwood-dedicated-usw2-2` and `kubectl get pods -n inference -o wide --selector=pool=redwood-dedicated-usw2-2`
Carlos Ruiz: running those now. Output: `pool desired=12 running=8 pending=4`, pending stuck due to provider quota error: `APILimitExceeded: Request rate too high`. Two pods with crashloop backoff referencing `failed to mount nvme-shared-cache`.
Mira Li: that explains sticky scatters — some requests are timing out waiting for cached shard mounts. Can we enable fallback to shared cached images and route new sessions to redwood-dedicated-usw2-1?
Tom Anders: yes, route change doable: `rdwctl route set --pool redwood-dedicated-usw2-1 --weight 1.0 --region us-west-2`. But need to ensure it has capacity for enterprise in-flight sessions.
Priya Natarajan: did the route change. traffic now shifting. 5xx down from 12% -> 3.2% in last 3m. Still seeing intermittent 504s for longer sessions.
Lydia (AM): cc: Account team. Crescent Bank contacts: primary ops@crescentbank.com (Megan Torres), secondary infra@crescentbank.com (Devon Lee), legal@crescentbank.com. Their contractual SLA: 99.9% monthly for dedicated pools with 4-hour response SLA for P1s.
Mira Li: drafting customer-specific impact note. Key points: which endpoints affected, recommended immediate mitigation (use lighter model or retries), ETA for next update. Avoid guessing RCA.
Priya Natarajan: root cause likely twofold: provider rate-limits prevented autoscaler from provisioning replacement nodes + stale cache mounts causing worker crashloops. Both together caused capacity shortfall for the dedicated pool.
Carlos Ruiz: repro steps for nodes: failed to mount because host kernel timed out on NVMe hotplug after orchestration patch last night. I'll open a separate issue with eng-infra. For immediate fix, I reprovisioned one node from golden image which has stable NVMe mounting behavior.
Mira Li: customer note draft (internal):
```
Crescent Bank Ops team,
We're investigating elevated 5xx/504 responses impacting your /v1/chat.generate traffic in us-west-2. Symptoms: higher latency and some requests returning 504. Mitigation in progress: we've shifted traffic to an alternate dedicated pool and reprovisioned affected nodes. Recommended client mitigations: enable short retries with jitter (max 3 attempts) and consider lowering request concurrency for long sessions. We will provide updates every 10 minutes until resolved. No customer data was accessed.
Regards,
Redwood Inference Ops
```
Mira Li: adding tailored impact statement: "Impact limited to dedicated pool redwood-dedicated-usw2-2; if you have failover to our other dedicated pool or hosted region enabled, traffic should succeed there. If you rely solely on redwood-dedicated-usw2-2, you may experience service degradation until reprovisioning completes."
Lydia (AM): please include explicit contact routing: sending to Megan Torres (ops), cc Devon Lee (infra); escalation to legal only if outage exceeds 60m. I will send the note and loop in AM shadow.
PagerDuty: Incident acknowledged by Priya Natarajan. Priority P1 for impacted enterprise customer.
Tom Anders: reprovisioning notes: kicked off node reprovision for 3 nodes, and temporarily increased provider API backoff window to avoid further quota hits. Also toggled `mount_retry=5` on agent to help NVMe binds.
Carlos Ruiz: after reprovision one node returned healthy, 504s dropped. But there are still two pods in CrashLoopBackOff due to corrupted cache metadata; I'm forcing a cache rehydrate from S3 for those workers: `rdwctl cache rehydrate --worker worker-23`
Priya Natarajan: sending internal status update template for comms channel (ops-team/incidents):
```
[INCIDENT] P1 - Crescent Bank - us-west-2 - ongoing
Impact: Elevated 5xx/504 for /v1/chat.generate for single enterprise dedicated pool
Mitigation: traffic routed to redwood-dedicated-usw2-1; reprovisioning nodes; cache rehydrate in progress
Customer contacts: Megan Torres (ops@crescentbank.com), Devon Lee (infra@crescentbank.com)
Next update: +10m
Owner: Mira Li (SRE)
```
Mira Li: Nora, please post private status page entry and schedule a public advisory if other customers are impacted.
Nora (status): drafting private post: "Crescent Bank - partial outage in us-west-2 resolved? pending confirmation" and will publish enterprise-visible update.
Lydia (AM): sent the tailored customer note and AMs report back that Crescent Bank has acknowledged and switched a subset of traffic to their backup region. They've asked for ETA on full restoration. Do not promise less than 20 minutes.
Priya Natarajan: verifying workers: `rdwctl workers list --pool redwood-dedicated-usw2-2` now shows running=11 desired=12 pending=1. Error rate at customer endpoint back to 0.9% and latency p95 420ms.
Carlos Ruiz: cache rehydrate completed for worker-23 and 24. Crash loops cleared. We've also added a transient guard to the autoscaler to slow provisioning attempts when provider returns 429s. Patch queued: autoscaler/backoff-throttle-20240801.
Tom Anders: filing follow-up tickets: INFRA-987: audit kernel hotplug patch; AUTOSCALE-112: provider quota handling; CACHE-561: rehydrate safeties.
Mira Li: OK. Drafting resolution/update for the customer: include timeline, RCA (provider rate limit + NVMe mount timeouts after orchestration change), mitigation steps we took, recommended client-side recommendations, and action items we will track with estimated timelines.
Mira Li: customer resolution draft:
```
Crescent Bank team,
Update: We have restored normal service for your us-west-2 traffic. Timeline: incident started ~14:11 UTC; traffic shifted to alternate pool and reprovisioning/cache rehydrate completed by 14:42 UTC. Root cause: a combination of provider API rate limiting which prevented timely autoscaler provisioning, and NVMe host mount timeouts caused by a recent orchestration hotplug change. Mitigations: routed traffic to redwood-dedicated-usw2-1, reprovisioned affected nodes, rehydrated local cache stores, and added autoscaler backoff throttling to avoid provider quota exhaustion. Recommended client actions: enable retries with jitter (max 3), consider multi-region failover for critical flows. We will follow up with a full postmortem and action item timeline.
Apologies for any disruption.
4cc Redwood Inference Ops
```
Lydia (AM): sent. Customer reply: "Thanks for the rapid response. Please share postmortem and let us know if we should change our failover config."
PagerDuty: Incident resolved by Priya Natarajan. Severity lowered to P2; auto-close pending postmortem.
Mira Li: creating incident ticket RDW-INC-2024-0801-cb402. Action items: 1) infra to assess orchestration hotplug patch impact (ETA 2 days); 2) autoscaler provider-backoff improvements (ETA 1 week); 3) add customer-specific autoscaler canary flag for dedicated pools (ETA 3 weeks).
Carlos Ruiz: noted. Uploading forensic logs and cache snapshots to s3://redwood-forensics/cb-402/20240801/ for RCA.
Nora (status): private post to Crescent Bank account page published; public status page shows brief advisory: "Transient service degradation in us-west-2 affecting a small group of dedicated-pool customers; resolved."
Mira Li: handing off postmortem ownership to Tom Anders for RCA write-up. Tom, please include timeline, root cause, and the tailored customer communications we used.
Tom Anders: acknowledged. I'll take the RCA and the Jira ticket. Closing live incident thread. :white_check_mark:

Lydia (AM): please include explicit contact routing: sending to Megan Torres (ops), cc Devon Lee (infra); escalation to legal only if outage exceeds 60m. I will send the note and loop in AM shadow.
PagerDuty: Incident acknowledged by Priya Natarajan. Priority P1 for impacted enterprise customer.
Tom Anders: reprovisioning notes: kicked off node reprovision for 3 nodes, and temporarily increased provider API backoff window to avoid further quota hits. Also toggled `mount_retry=5` on agent to help NVMe binds.
Carlos Ruiz: after reprovision one node returned healthy, 504s dropped. But there are still two pods in CrashLoopBackOff due to corrupted cache metadata; I'm forcing a cache rehydrate from S3 for those workers: `rdwctl cache rehydrate --worker worker-23`
Priya Natarajan: sending internal status update template for comms channel (ops-team/incidents):
```
[INCIDENT] P1 - Crescent Bank - us-west-2 - ongoing
Impact: Elevated 5xx/504 for /v1/chat.generate for single enterprise dedicated pool
Mitigation: traffic routed to redwood-dedicated-usw2-1; reprovisioning nodes; cache rehydrate in progress
Customer contacts: Megan Torres (ops@crescentbank.com), Devon Lee (infra@crescentbank.com)
Next update: +10m
Owner: Mira Li (SRE)
```
Mira Li: Nora, please post private status page entry and schedule a public advisory if other customers are impacted.
Nora (status): drafting private post: "Crescent Bank - partial outage in us-west-2 resolved? pending confirmation" and will publish enterprise-visible update.
Lydia (AM): sent the tailored customer note and AMs report back that Crescent Bank has acknowledged and switched a subset of traffic to their backup region. They've asked for ETA on full restoration. Do not promise less than 20 minutes.
Priya Natarajan: verifying workers: `rdwctl workers list --pool redwood-dedicated-usw2-2` now shows running=11 desired=12 pending=1. Error rate at customer endpoint back to 0.9% and latency p95 420ms.
Carlos Ruiz: cache rehydrate completed for worker-23 and 24. Crash loops cleared. We've also added a transient guard to the autoscaler to slow provisioning attempts when provider returns 429s. Patch queued: autoscaler/backoff-throttle-20240801.
Tom Anders: filing follow-up tickets: INFRA-987: audit kernel hotplug patch; AUTOSCALE-112: provider quota handling; CACHE-561: rehydrate safeties.
Mira Li: OK. Drafting resolution/update for the customer: include timeline, RCA (provider rate limit + NVMe mount timeouts after orchestration change), mitigation steps we took, recommended client-side recommendations, and action items we will track with estimated timelines.
Mira Li: customer resolution draft:
```
Crescent Bank team,
Update: We have restored normal service for your us-west-2 traffic. Timeline: incident started ~14:11 UTC; traffic shifted to alternate pool and reprovisioning/cache rehydrate completed by 14:42 UTC. Root cause: a combination of provider API rate limiting which prevented timely autoscaler provisioning, and NVMe host mount timeouts caused by a recent orchestration hotplug change. Mitigations: routed traffic to redwood-dedicated-usw2-1, reprovisioned affected nodes, rehydrated local cache stores, and added autoscaler backoff throttling to avoid provider quota exhaustion. Recommended client actions: enable retries with jitter (max 3), consider multi-region failover for critical flows. We will follow up with a full postmortem and action item timeline.
Apologies for any disruption.
4cc Redwood Inference Ops
```
Lydia (AM): sent. Customer reply: "Thanks for the rapid response. Please share postmortem and let us know if we should change our failover config."
PagerDuty: Incident resolved by Priya Natarajan. Severity lowered to P2; auto-close pending postmortem.
Mira Li: creating incident ticket RDW-INC-2024-0801-cb402. Action items: 1) infra to assess orchestration hotplug patch impact (ETA 2 days); 2) autoscaler provider-backoff improvements (ETA 1 week); 3) add customer-specific autoscaler canary flag for dedicated pools (ETA 3 weeks).
Carlos Ruiz: noted. Uploading forensic logs and cache snapshots to s3://redwood-forensics/cb-402/20240801/ for RCA.
Nora (status): private post to Crescent Bank account page published; public status page shows brief advisory: "Transient service degradation in us-west-2 affecting a small group of dedicated-pool customers; resolved."
Mira Li: handing off postmortem ownership to Tom Anders for RCA write-up. Tom, please include timeline, root cause, and the tailored customer communications we used.
Tom Anders: acknowledged. I'll take the RCA and the Jira ticket. Closing live incident thread. :white_check_mark:

source.content:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: Triggered alert: Hosted API p99 latency burn (us-east) is above threshold for 10m
Signals: chat.completions p99=18.7s (baseline 4.2s), timeout_rate=3.8%, 5xx_rate=1.1%
Dashboards: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/hosted-api-us-east-overview?from=now-30m
Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/ONCALL/Hosted+Overload+Circuit+Breaker+Runbook

Rafael Mendes: seeing this too. oncall says mostly chat.completions, model=rw-llama-70b in us-east. Anyone already digging in runtime signals?

Aisha Bello: looking at overload dash (new one). shed_rate is basically 0 (not enabled), but runtime queue depth is spiking hard. prefill_q_depth p95 ~ 140 (normally <20). decode tokens/sec saturated ~ 95-98%.

Christine Wong: pulling request samples from gateway logs. a bunch of client timeouts, retries overlapping. seeing long prompts too (preamble ~12-16k tokens). looks like burst + long-context.

Ethan Park: gateway side: concurrency is unbounded for that route right now besides tenant quota. we can put a temporary cap per-route in us-east if needed. question is do we want to flip breaker canary early?

Peter Holtz: +1 consider canary. concern: if we start returning 503/overloaded without Retry-After in all paths we could trigger retry storm. do we have the hotfix everywhere in us-east?

Christine Wong: Retry-After consistency should be good post pr-18472 but I’m not 100% if prod us-east has it on all handlers. I can verify quickly.

Mina Haddad: routing perspective: we can also enable fallback to us-west for a subset if policy allows. but careful: cross-region latency and west is already ~70% utilization.

Sofia Ivanova: runtime: sched backlog is real. batching is already at cap. we’re also seeing kv cache pressure on a subset of GPUs (fragmentation proxy up). if we keep admitting at this rate, p99 will keep walking.

Rafael Mendes: ok, goal is stop cascade. options:
1) temporary gateway cap (concurrency + token bucket)
2) enable breaker canary (us-east only, small tenant slice)
3) routing fallback (only for tenants configured)

Aisha Bello: error budget burn is accelerating. for the last 15m, availability SLI for hosted in us-east is trending <99.0 if this continues.

Amara Diallo: I can apply a per-route concurrency cap via the gateway config toggle (no deploy). We have a “soft reject” mode that returns 429 w/ Retry-After when at cap. But do we want 429 or 503 here?

Nikhil Sharma: from PM side: prefer stable semantics. overload should be 503 with explicit code if it’s platform saturation. 429 is more “client throttled”. but if we only have 429 available quickly, do it + clear messaging.

Peter Holtz: +1 semantics, but priority is stop tail-latency + timeouts. timeouts are worst UX.

Christine Wong: checked a few live responses in us-east via replay. overload handler is not active so no direct proof. but the common 503s we see are upstream timeouts (no Retry-After). :grimacing:

Ethan Park: the breaker framework is behind flag `overload_protection.breaker_enabled`. canary mode exists: `breaker_mode=canary` + `canary_tenant_hash=5%`. It would return 503 with `error.code=overloaded` and set Retry-After.

Rafael Mendes: that sounds like what we want, but we’re mid-incident. are we confident it won’t false-positive and take out healthy traffic?

Aisha Bello: thresholds from tuning sheet for us-east: open when queue_depth>120 for 30s OR token_sat>0.95 for 60s. We’re currently above both for ~8-10m. So it would definitely open.

Sofia Ivanova: from runtime side, opening would help immediately. we need to reduce admission. we can also temporarily reduce max_tokens cap under duress, but that’s a broader behavior change.

Mina Haddad: if breaker opens, routing can attempt fallback only when configured. do we have default fallback configured for self-serve? I think no (opt-in).

Ethan Park: correct, no default fallback. breaker open would primarily shed/queue at gateway.

Connor O'Brien: FYI deploy status: us-east control plane updated yesterday, runtime patch is current. no deploys in progress. if we flip flags, it’s immediate.

Peter Holtz: can we do a two-step? apply a small gateway cap now to reduce blast radius, then flip breaker canary to get correct error semantics + correlation IDs.

Amara Diallo: I can set cap to 80% of current observed throughput and add token bucket on chat.completions only. That will start returning 429 w/ Retry-After=2 for excess.

Nikhil Sharma: if we do 429, please make sure Support gets a heads up. customers will ask.

Rafael Mendes: ok do the cap now. then we enable breaker canary 5% tenants in us-east, monitor for 5-10m, then decide expand.

Ethan Park: ack. I’ll flip breaker canary flag for us-east only after Amara’s cap is in. Christine can you monitor error payloads to ensure Retry-After present + correlation id?

Christine Wong: yep. I’ll tail gateway responses and spot-check.

Amara Diallo: applying cap now…

deploy-bot: Config change applied: gateway/us-east route=chat.completions
- concurrency_cap: 2200 -> 1600
- token_bucket_rps: 0 -> 900 (burst 1200)
- reject_status: 429
- retry_after_seconds: 2
Requested by: Amara Diallo

Aisha Bello: immediate impact: queue depth is starting to flatten (not climbing). timeout_rate down from 3.8% -> 2.6% last 5m but still high.

Rafael Mendes: good. Ethan go ahead with breaker canary.

Ethan Park: flipping `breaker_enabled` to canary (us-east, 5%).

deploy-bot: Feature flag updated: overload_protection.breaker_enabled
- region: us-east
- mode: canary
- canary_tenant_hash: 5%
- error_status: 503
- error_code: overloaded
- include_retry_after: true
Requested by: Ethan Park

Christine Wong: seeing 503 responses now with payload:
`{"error":{"code":"overloaded","message":"Service is temporarily overloaded. Please retry.","request_id":"req_7f2..."}}`
Headers include `Retry-After: 3` and `x-request-id` present. :thumbsup:

Peter Holtz: nice. any sign of clients ignoring Retry-After and hammering?

Aisha Bello: retry traffic is elevated but not spiking further. overall RPS stabilized. breaker_open_rate for canary slice ~0.9 (expected given saturation). also seeing 503s replacing timeouts for that slice.

Sofia Ivanova: runtime now recovering: decode backlog down ~20%. token/sec sat still high but not pegged. should see p99 improve in ~10-15m.

Rafael Mendes: decision point: expand breaker beyond canary? or keep canary + cap until load subsides.

Mina Haddad: keep canary only for now. expanding means more hard 503s and we haven’t notified Support / status page. if p99 improves w/ cap, that’s enough.

Nikhil Sharma: agreed. for now keep impact minimal.

Rafael Mendes: ok. action items:
- Keep gateway cap for 60m, reassess.
- Keep breaker at 5% canary in us-east.
- Aisha to watch SLO burn + p99.
- Christine to confirm Retry-After coverage on remaining overload paths.

Aisha Bello: update: p99 chat.completions us-east is down to 11.2s (from 18.7s). timeout_rate now 1.4% and trending down.

Peter Holtz: :thumbsup: can we also add a note in #support that some tenants may see 503 overloaded w/ Retry-After, expected during incident mitigation.

Rafael Mendes: I’ll ping Support liaison.

Rafael Mendes: @Dev Patel heads up: us-east had burst traffic causing timeouts. We applied temporary caps and enabled 5% breaker canary. Some tenants may see 503 `error.code=overloaded` with Retry-After; guidance is exponential backoff + respect header.

Connor O'Brien: FYI for postmortem: capture current thresholds, cap values, and canary config. I’ll open an incident doc.

Christine Wong: one more thing: some 429s from cap don’t include `error.code` right now (just message). not ideal but ok short-term. I’ll file a bug / link to overload taxonomy task.

Aisha Bello: metrics stable for last 10m. p99 now 8.6s. still above baseline but improving.

Rafael Mendes: holding steady. if it continues improving, we can remove cap gradually and keep canary running for another day to collect data.

incident-bot: :information_source: Incident status updated by Rafael Mendes: MITIGATING
Summary: us-east saturation due to burst + long prompts. Applied gateway admission caps + enabled 5% breaker canary to convert timeouts into explicit overload errors.
Next update in 30m.

source.text:
Incident Bot: :rotating_light: *Quality Guardrail Alert* :rotating_light:
Signal: `quant_profile_quality_regression`
Environment: `beta-hosted` / `perf-canary`
Model: `llama-3.1-70b-instruct` variant `x1`
Profile: `qp_llama31_70b_x1_int8_v3` (library)
Route(s): `dp-acme/chat-prod` (design partner allowlist)
Metric: `eval.delta` exceeded threshold for suite `redwood-pset-summ-v2`
Observed: -1.9pp (threshold -1.0pp)
Fallback rate: 0.2% (normal)
Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/SRE/rollback-procedure-quantization-profiles
Dashboard: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/quant-profiles/rollout?var_model=llama-3.1-70b-instruct&var_profile=qp_llama31_70b_x1_int8_v3

Selene Huang: seeing this fire in both perf-canary and the beta route. looks like *real* quality delta, not a harness flake. :eyes:

Jonas Weber: pulled the latest eval harness run. the delta is concentrated in summarization + a couple of instruction-following items.
- suite: `redwood-pset-summ-v2` -> -1.9pp (p<0.01)
- suite: `redwood-pset-chat-safety-mini` -> within noise
decode latency looks fine. this is quality-only.

Arjun Desai: confirm profile `...int8_v3` was published yesterday w/ “beta” tag for x1. calibration set might be under-representing the summarization prompts.
we should *block* this profile version for `x1` ASAP.

Connor O'Brien: agree. do we want to do route-level disable for acme immediately, or kill-switch? (kill-switch feels too heavy if isolated to one variant/profile)

Selene Huang: route-level disable first. criteria says any statistically sig regression beyond threshold => disable + investigate.
kill-switch only if we see blast radius or can't roll back quickly.

Ravi Menon: I can flip the control-plane policy to resolve `quantization_profile_ref=default` for that route. also can add a temporary denylist entry for `qp_llama31_70b_x1_int8_v3` + variant `x1` so people can't re-enable.

Noah Patel: runtime side is behaving as expected (profile applied, quant kernels selected). no correctness errors, just output drift. logs show `quant_profile_id=qp_llama31_70b_x1_int8_v3` with `resolution=applied`.

Mateo Alvarez: +1. no crash/NaNs. this looks like genuine quant quality tradeoff, not a runtime bug.

Paula Mendes: do we know if any other beta routes enabled this profile? want to scope impact for comms.

Ravi Menon: checking now via route settings query. so far only `dp-acme/chat-prod` has the profile explicitly set. perf-canary has it via scenario config.

Dev Patel: support side: acme had asked for “opt-in only” w/ fast rollback. I can ping Markus/Miguel w/ heads up once we flip it.

Connor O'Brien: ok let's execute:
1) disable on `dp-acme/chat-prod` (route setting)
2) block profile for x1 in allowed list (so UI/API shows incompatible)
3) rerun evals with a recalibrated candidate if/when Applied ML has one

Selene Huang: +1. also please capture artifacts + link in follow-ups. and open a post-incident note even if low severity.

Ravi Menon: I’m applying route change now.

Ravi Menon: change submitted: `route dp-acme/chat-prod` -> `quantization_profile_ref=default`
Audit event should show up in ~1 min.

Incident Bot: :white_check_mark: Audit Log Event
actor: `ravi.menon`
action: `route.quantization_profile_ref.update`
route: `dp-acme/chat-prod`
before: `qp_llama31_70b_x1_int8_v3`
after: `default`

Olivia Grant: perf-canary: I can disable the scenario for tonight’s run so we stop re-triggering alerts while we investigate.

Jonas Weber: please do, but keep a baseline run so we can confirm recovery / rule out harness issues.

Arjun Desai: I’ll mark `qp_llama31_70b_x1_int8_v3` as blocked for `x1` and open a hotfix PR to update the registry constraints. likely same as what we discussed in the “block publication pending recalibration” policy.

Connor O'Brien: Arjun pls link PR here. we should get it into next patch train.

Arjun Desai: will do. plan:
- update profile metadata: `status=deprecated_for_variant` (x1 only)
- add note: “fails redwood-pset-summ-v2 regression threshold”
- ensure Console shows warning + prevents enable

Selene Huang: also add a guardrail test so we catch this earlier: if a profile is newly published, require it to pass `summ-v2` specifically for 70B x1.

Paula Mendes: can we add a dashboard annotation for the disable event + include in beta weekly update?

Connor O'Brien: yes. Paula can you draft a short note for release/beta update? keep it factual: “quality regression detected, rolled back within X minutes, no SLA impact.”

Dev Patel: if acme asks what happened, we can say:
- regression alert fired
- profile disabled on their route
- they’re back to baseline precision
- we’ll share a new candidate after recalibration + eval report

Noah Patel: monitoring right now: after disable, `quant_profile_applied_rate` on that route is dropping to 0. fallback rate steady. p95 latency unchanged.

Jonas Weber: kicking a quick rerun on eval harness comparing baseline vs old profile to get a per-category breakdown for Applied ML.

Ravi Menon: I’ll also add a policy rule so if someone tries to set `qp_llama31_70b_x1_int8_v3` on x1 it resolves as incompatible + returns a clear error (vs silently applying).

Selene Huang: good. “no silent changes” + “no silent incompatible enablement” were explicit beta conditions.

Arjun Desai: PR incoming shortly. also note: the same int8_v3 profile is *passing* on variant `x2` (different kernel path). so we should scope the block narrowly to x1.

Connor O'Brien: perfect. keep scope tight.

Incident Bot: :information_source: Alert Recovery
Signal: `quant_profile_quality_regression`
Status: recovered (no new firing for 20m)

Connor O'Brien: ok marking incident as mitigated. follow-ups:
- [Arjun] hotfix PR to block/deprecate profile for x1 + publish notes
- [Jonas/Selene] attach eval breakdown + confirm against regression thresholds doc
- [Ravi] ensure control plane returns explicit incompat error + Console warning
- [Olivia] adjust perf-canary scenario (disable this profile on x1) + keep baseline
- [Paula] beta update note + dashboard annotation
- [Dev] inform design partner contact + offer to review their own prompt-set eval plan

Selene Huang: :thumbsup: thanks all. please also link the audit event + dashboard snapshot in the post-incident doc.

Arjun Desai: PR link: https://github.com/redwood-inference/redwood/pull/18592 (hotfix: disable profile on model variant x1 due to quality regression)

Connor O'Brien: :thumbsup: will queue for patch train. please add a short rollback note in the PR description.

Arjun Desai: done. adding “how to re-enable once recalibrated” as well.

Jonas Weber: eval breakdown ETA ~30m. prelim: the biggest hits are long-context summarization prompts (>4k tokens). might need a max-context constraint in the profile metadata for x1.

Noah Patel: that tracks w/ activation quant + kv-cache interactions at long seq. might be worth adding `max_context_tokens` guardrail to compatibility checks.

Ravi Menon: yep we can enforce `max_context_tokens` in policy engine if we have it in the profile schema/metadata. will open a follow-up ENG ticket once we confirm.

Selene Huang: cool. closing loop once eval breakdown is posted.

Observed: -1.9pp (threshold -1.0pp)
Fallback rate: 0.2% (normal)
Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/SRE/rollback-procedure-quantization-profiles
Dashboard: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/quant-profiles/rollout?var_model=llama-3.1-70b-instruct&var_profile=qp_llama31_70b_x1_int8_v3

Selene Huang: seeing this fire in both perf-canary and the beta route. looks like *real* quality delta, not a harness flake. :eyes:

Jonas Weber: pulled the latest eval harness run. the delta is concentrated in summarization + a couple of instruction-following items.
- suite: `redwood-pset-summ-v2` -> -1.9pp (p<0.01)
- suite: `redwood-pset-chat-safety-mini` -> within noise
decode latency looks fine. this is quality-only.

Arjun Desai: confirm profile `...int8_v3` was published yesterday w/ “beta” tag for x1. calibration set might be under-representing the summarization prompts.
we should *block* this profile version for `x1` ASAP.

Connor O'Brien: agree. do we want to do route-level disable for acme immediately, or kill-switch? (kill-switch feels too heavy if isolated to one variant/profile)

Selene Huang: route-level disable first. criteria says any statistically sig regression beyond threshold => disable + investigate.
kill-switch only if we see blast radius or can't roll back quickly.

Ravi Menon: I can flip the control-plane policy to resolve `quantization_profile_ref=default` for that route. also can add a temporary denylist entry for `qp_llama31_70b_x1_int8_v3` + variant `x1` so people can't re-enable.

Noah Patel: runtime side is behaving as expected (profile applied, quant kernels selected). no correctness errors, just output drift. logs show `quant_profile_id=qp_llama31_70b_x1_int8_v3` with `resolution=applied`.

Mateo Alvarez: +1. no crash/NaNs. this looks like genuine quant quality tradeoff, not a runtime bug.

Paula Mendes: do we know if any other beta routes enabled this profile? want to scope impact for comms.

Ravi Menon: checking now via route settings query. so far only `dp-acme/chat-prod` has the profile explicitly set. perf-canary has it via scenario config.

Dev Patel: support side: acme had asked for “opt-in only” w/ fast rollback. I can ping Markus/Miguel w/ heads up once we flip it.

Connor O'Brien: ok let's execute:
1) disable on `dp-acme/chat-prod` (route setting)
2) block profile for x1 in allowed list (so UI/API shows incompatible)
3) rerun evals with a recalibrated candidate if/when Applied ML has one

Selene Huang: +1. also please capture artifacts + link in follow-ups. and open a post-incident note even if low severity.

Ravi Menon: I’m applying route change now.

Ravi Menon: change submitted: `route dp-acme/chat-prod` -> `quantization_profile_ref=default`
Audit event should show up in ~1 min.

Incident Bot: :white_check_mark: Audit Log Event
actor: `ravi.menon`
action: `route.quantization_profile_ref.update`
route: `dp-acme/chat-prod`
before: `qp_llama31_70b_x1_int8_v3`
after: `default`

Olivia Grant: perf-canary: I can disable the scenario for tonight’s run so we stop re-triggering alerts while we investigate.

Jonas Weber: please do, but keep a baseline run so we can confirm recovery / rule out harness issues.

Arjun Desai: I’ll mark `qp_llama31_70b_x1_int8_v3` as blocked for `x1` and open a hotfix PR to update the registry constraints. likely same as what we discussed in the “block publication pending recalibration” policy.

Connor O'Brien: Arjun pls link PR here. we should get it into next patch train.

Arjun Desai: will do. plan:
- update profile metadata: `status=deprecated_for_variant` (x1 only)
- add note: “fails redwood-pset-summ-v2 regression threshold”
- ensure Console shows warning + prevents enable

Selene Huang: also add a guardrail test so we catch this earlier: if a profile is newly published, require it to pass `summ-v2` specifically for 70B x1.

Paula Mendes: can we add a dashboard annotation for the disable event + include in beta weekly update?

Connor O'Brien: yes. Paula can you draft a short note for release/beta update? keep it factual: “quality regression detected, rolled back within X minutes, no SLA impact.”

Dev Patel: if acme asks what happened, we can say:
- regression alert fired
- profile disabled on their route
- they’re back to baseline precision
- we’ll share a new candidate after recalibration + eval report

Noah Patel: monitoring right now: after disable, `quant_profile_applied_rate` on that route is dropping to 0. fallback rate steady. p95 latency unchanged.

Jonas Weber: kicking a quick rerun on eval harness comparing baseline vs old profile to get a per-category breakdown for Applied ML.

Ravi Menon: I’ll also add a policy rule so if someone tries to set `qp_llama31_70b_x1_int8_v3` on x1 it resolves as incompatible + returns a clear error (vs silently applying).

Selene Huang: good. “no silent changes” + “no silent incompatible enablement” were explicit beta conditions.

Arjun Desai: PR incoming shortly. also note: the same int8_v3 profile is *passing* on variant `x2` (different kernel path). so we should scope the block narrowly to x1.

Connor O'Brien: perfect. keep scope tight.

Incident Bot: :information_source: Alert Recovery
Signal: `quant_profile_quality_regression`
Status: recovered (no new firing for 20m)

Connor O'Brien: ok marking incident as mitigated. follow-ups:
- [Arjun] hotfix PR to block/deprecate profile for x1 + publish notes
- [Jonas/Selene] attach eval breakdown + confirm against regression thresholds doc
- [Ravi] ensure control plane returns explicit incompat error + Console warning
- [Olivia] adjust perf-canary scenario (disable this profile on x1) + keep baseline
- [Paula] beta update note + dashboard annotation
- [Dev] inform design partner contact + offer to review their own prompt-set eval plan

Selene Huang: :thumbsup: thanks all. please also link the audit event + dashboard snapshot in the post-incident doc.

Arjun Desai: PR link: https://github.com/redwood-inference/redwood/pull/18592 (hotfix: disable profile on model variant x1 due to quality regression)

Connor O'Brien: :thumbsup: will queue for patch train. please add a short rollback note in the PR description.

Arjun Desai: done. adding “how to re-enable once recalibrated” as well.

Jonas Weber: eval breakdown ETA ~30m. prelim: the biggest hits are long-context summarization prompts (>4k tokens). might need a max-context constraint in the profile metadata for x1.

Noah Patel: that tracks w/ activation quant + kv-cache interactions at long seq. might be worth adding `max_context_tokens` guardrail to compatibility checks.

Ravi Menon: yep we can enforce `max_context_tokens` in policy engine if we have it in the profile schema/metadata. will open a follow-up ENG ticket once we confirm.

Selene Huang: cool. closing loop once eval breakdown is posted.

source.messages:
Incident Bot: :rotating_light: *New incident reported* 
Source: Pilot customer (LTS candidate) via Support escalation
Summary: Runtime crash (SIGSEGV) in serving runtime on LTS candidate build
Env: Dedicated pilot pool, us-east-1, A100-80GB
First seen: ~15 min ago
Impact: requests failing, pods restarting
Links: SLO dashboard (LTS): https://slo.redwood.ai/dash/lts (internal)

Sean Gallagher: grabbing. anyone from runtime on? @Samir Qureshi @Sofia Ivanova @Hiro Tanaka

Owen Phillips: Support context: customer says it started right after we rolled the RC image to their dedicated pool. They’re seeing 500s + reconnect loops. They can reproduce with a long context + streaming.

Rafael Mendes: I’m on as escalation. Sean, want me to start incident doc + comms?

Sean Gallagher: yes please. make this Sev1 until we know blast radius. keep scope to pilot but it’s a release candidate so treat as blocker.

Allison Grant: looking at metrics. LTS pool error rate jumped to ~38% for that customer namespace. restarts correlate w/ `runtime-server` pods. p95 latency irrelevant since they’re hard failing.

Connor O'Brien: fyi we were planning to publish 1.18.1-lts notes tomorrow AM. if this is RC-only, we should hold.

Samir Qureshi: on. can someone paste the crash log? need stack.

Sean Gallagher: from kube events + container logs:
```
Mar 06 19:12:07 runtime-server[1]: FATAL: signal 11 (SIGSEGV) at address 0x0000000000000000
Mar 06 19:12:07 runtime-server[1]: tid=1187 name=worker-3
Mar 06 19:12:07 runtime-server[1]: stacktrace:
  #0 0x0000000002c7a91d kv_cache::Compactor::compact_in_place(KvBlock*)
  #1 0x0000000002c78f42 kv_cache::Compactor::maybe_compact(...)
  #2 0x0000000001a02c11 scheduler::TokenScheduler::on_decode_step(...)
  #3 0x0000000001a11d7b scheduler::TokenScheduler::run_loop()
  #4 0x0000000000f1b812 std::thread::_State_impl<...>::_M_run()
```
RC image tag: `runtime:1.18.1-lts-rc3` (per deploy annotation)

Hiro Tanaka: kv_cache compactor… that’s the same area as the compaction crash we fixed on main last week. did we cherry-pick that into lts yet?

Connor O'Brien: not yet. we had it in the backport candidate tracker but it was pending perf validation. (it’s the one that became PR-23341 on the lts branch draft, i think)

Samir Qureshi: yep this looks identical. If it’s null deref in compaction path, it triggers with mixed seq lengths + eviction pressure.

Marcus Reed: can confirm the perf suite includes a mixed-length workload but it doesn’t always hit compaction under the same pressure. we can add a targeted stress test, but for now this is release-blocking.

Sofia Ivanova: scheduler frame shows decode step calling compactor, so scheduler not root cause. but if they’re using bursty traffic it may increase evictions.

Allison Grant: customer traffic pattern shows spikes + long contexts (p99 prompt tokens ~7k). KV cache eviction rate is ~4x baseline.

Rafael Mendes: incident doc created: https://internal.incidents.redwood.ai/INC-291 (internal)
Current status: investigating / mitigation pending

Sean Gallagher: immediate mitigation: roll them back to previous stable runtime (not RC). @Connor O'Brien do we have an LTS-ish baseline deployed for pilot before rc3?

Connor O'Brien: they were on `1.18.0` mainline before we moved them to the candidate. For pilot we can roll back to `runtime:1.18.0` (non-lts) or `1.17.6` (older). If they’re ok with losing some features, 1.17.6 is safer.

Rachel Kim: from CS side, customer’s priority is stability. they explicitly asked not to churn weekly. rolling back is fine but we need a clear plan + timeline.

Liam O'Rourke: +1. they said "we can’t keep taking runtime upgrades during our internal change freeze". If we tell them "RC had a crash, we’re holding release" that’s acceptable if we’re crisp.

Sean Gallagher: let’s roll back to 1.18.0 for now to restore service quickly, then cut new rc w/ the backport fix and rerun gates. agree?

Rafael Mendes: agree. from SRE side, minimizing delta first. any known regressions on 1.18.0 for them?

Allison Grant: they had a latency regression ticket on 1.18.0 earlier (p95 +12%) but it wasn’t crashing. this incident is worse.

Hiro Tanaka: I’ll pull the fix commit hash from main + prep backport PR to lts branch immediately. @Samir Qureshi can you sanity check? It’s small but touches compactor.

Samir Qureshi: yes. also want to add a reproducer. customer can probably share a prompt shape but we can simulate. I’ll work with Marcus on a minimal test.

Connor O'Brien: action items:
1) HOLD release publish for 1.18.1-lts until resolved
2) Backport compaction crash fix to `runtime-1.18-lts`
3) Re-run CI + perf gates (esp long-context + eviction)
4) New RC tag (rc4) + redeploy pilot
I’ll update eng-releases once we confirm rollback.

Sean Gallagher: I’ll coordinate rollback with oncall for Dedicated. @Allison Grant can you watch dashboards for recovery.

Allison Grant: on it.

Owen Phillips: Support will message customer that we’re rolling back to restore stability, then we’ll share an updated RC ETA once we’ve validated.

Rafael Mendes: added note to incident doc: "release-blocker" + "LTS" label. also pinged @Dr. Aisha Rahman FYI since it’s crash but not security.

Sean Gallagher: rollout command from Dedicated operator:
```
rdw-dedicated rollout set-image --customer <pilot-a> --service runtime-server --image runtime:1.18.0
rdw-dedicated rollout status --customer <pilot-a> --service runtime-server
```

Allison Grant: seeing restarts drop. error rate trending down. current 5xx at ~6% and falling.

Sean Gallagher: good. keep watching until back to baseline.

Hiro Tanaka: found the mainline fix: "Guard compaction when block ptr is null during eviction edge case". creating backport PR now.

Samir Qureshi: make sure we include the follow-up that adds an assert + log (helps if it happens again). might be slightly noisy but better than segfault.

Hiro Tanaka: ack. I’ll include the log at WARN, not assert, in lts.

Marcus Reed: I can run the compaction stress workload on A100 and H100 tonight. will post perf gate summary. if it’s under noise, good.

Connor O'Brien: once backport PR is up, please tag `lts-approved?` after review so our tooling picks it up for notes. also need to update known issues doc to mention rc3 crash (for internal tracking).

Sofia Ivanova: if we’re holding publish, please also update the release notes generator output to remove rc3 artifacts from any external links.

Connor O'Brien: yep. we won’t publish notes until rc4 passes.

Allison Grant: recovery update: 5xx now <0.5%, pods stable. p95 back to their pre-incident level.

Sean Gallagher: marking mitigation successful (rollback). incident stays open until fix lands + validated.

Hiro Tanaka: backport PR opened: https://github.com/redwood/redwood/pull/23341 (internal) 
Adds null guard + adds targeted unit test for compaction path.
Reviewers requested: @Samir Qureshi @Connor O'Brien

Samir Qureshi: reviewing now.

Rafael Mendes: updated incident doc w/ PR link + changed status to "mitigated; fix in progress".

Connor O'Brien: decision recorded: *Hold 1.18.1-lts release* pending backport + revalidation. We’ll cut rc4 after merge and gates.

Sean Gallagher: thanks all. keep thread updated when PR merges + when pilot redeploy is scheduled.

Connor O'Brien: yep. we won’t publish notes until rc4 passes.

Allison Grant: recovery update: 5xx now <0.5%, pods stable. p95 back to their pre-incident level.

Sean Gallagher: marking mitigation successful (rollback). incident stays open until fix lands + validated.

Hiro Tanaka: backport PR opened: https://github.com/redwood/redwood/pull/23341 (internal) 
Adds null guard + adds targeted unit test for compaction path.
Reviewers requested: @Samir Qureshi @Connor O'Brien

Samir Qureshi: reviewing now.

Rafael Mendes: updated incident doc w/ PR link + changed status to "mitigated; fix in progress".

Connor O'Brien: decision recorded: *Hold 1.18.1-lts release* pending backport + revalidation. We’ll cut rc4 after merge and gates.

Sean Gallagher: thanks all. keep thread updated when PR merges + when pilot redeploy is scheduled.

source.content:
Incident Bot: :rotating_light: Incident opened by @Sean Gallagher: Purge job backlog after DB maintenance (retention enforcement). Suspected impact: TTL lag increasing in us-east. Owners: Eng Platform + SRE. Tracking in #incidents thread.

Sean Gallagher: seeing retention purge backlog spike right after last night’s DB maintenance window. Dash: ttl_lag_seconds p95 for request-logs jumped from ~2h -> ~19h. purge_failures also up.

Allison Grant: confirm. retention-controls-verification dashboard: 
- request_logs: backlog_size ~8.4M rows, purge_rate collapsed
- audit_logs: mild increase but not as bad
Also seeing DB CPU pegged in us-east primary.

Logan Wright: this lines up w/ vacuum/reindex that ran 02:00–03:10 UTC. If purge jobs resumed at full throttle right after, they could be competing w/ catch-up replication / cache warm.

Kevin Osei: looking at purge worker logs. a bunch of retries + timeouts:
```
ERROR purge_job request_logs job_id=purge-request-logs-us-east-20250301T0315Z err="pq: canceling statement due to statement timeout"
WARN  purge_job request_logs batch_size=10000 elapsed_ms=30004 retries=3
```
Batch size is 10k right now.

Sean Gallagher: let’s mitigate by throttling purge jobs (lower concurrency + smaller batch) until DB settles. Anyone opposed?

Allison Grant: +1. Also make sure we don’t violate retention windows; we can tolerate some TTL lag but need to keep it observable.

Nadia Rahman: I can patch the purge-job framework config. Current in us-east:
- concurrency=24
- batch_size=10000
- max_runtime=55m
Propose: concurrency=6, batch_size=2000, add jitter between batches.

Sean Gallagher: do it. Also cap max deletions per minute to avoid surprise load (even if concurrency creeps). We can re-open after we see DB load normalize.

Jordan Lee: FYI policy evaluator cache looks fine; this is execution-side. No spike in policy change traffic.

Kevin Osei: for audit logs store, it’s using same framework but different query. It’s not timing out as much, but if we drop global concurrency it’ll slow too. Maybe keep audit_logs concurrency higher?

Sean Gallagher: keep audit_logs at 8, request_logs at 6. Goal is stabilize DB first.

Nadia Rahman: rolling config change now. ETA 5 min.

Incident Bot: Deploy Bot: Change requested by @Nadia Rahman to retention-purge config (us-east). Status: IN_PROGRESS.

Allison Grant: while that rolls: can we validate DB pressure? I’m checking pgbouncer queue depth + locks.

Allison Grant: seeing lock waits on request_logs partitions. Not crazy, but enough. Also statement timeout was at 30s — with big batch deletes that’s expected.

Marcus Lin: any security/compliance risk here? only risk is retention enforcement delayed. Need to document exception window + mitigation steps for evidence.

Sean Gallagher: ack. We’ll record TTL lag and remediation in postmortem and in retention enforcement evidence notes. No data exposure.

Nadia Rahman: config applied. request_logs purge workers restarting w/ new settings.

Incident Bot: Deploy Bot: Change completed. Status: SUCCESS.

Kevin Osei: logs now show smaller batches completing:
```
INFO purge_job request_logs batch_size=2000 rows_deleted=2000 elapsed_ms=4120
INFO purge_job request_logs batch_size=2000 rows_deleted=2000 elapsed_ms=3988
```
No timeouts so far.

Allison Grant: DB CPU down from 92% -> 63% over last 10m. pgbouncer queues trending down too.

Sean Gallagher: good. what’s backlog doing?

Allison Grant: backlog still rising slightly but rate is slowing. ttl_lag_seconds plateauing ~19–20h. Need ~1-2h to see it reverse.

Logan Wright: note: after maintenance, autovacuum has to catch up; delete-heavy workloads can make it worse. Might want to pause purge for 30m entirely if DB gets hot again.

Sean Gallagher: let’s keep reduced throttle, not pause yet. Set an alert threshold if DB CPU >85% for 5m to page.

Allison Grant: already have db_cpu_high. I’ll add a temporary annotation + tighten for this incident window.

Kevin Osei: should we also temporarily raise statement_timeout to 60s for purge queries? With smaller batches, 30s is fine, but if we later increase batch size it might help.

Sean Gallagher: prefer not to touch DB-level timeouts during recovery. We can revisit after.

Nadia Rahman: agreed. also there’s a known issue: the request_logs purge query does not use the new covering index on (expires_at, tenant_id) in one partition. Might be why deletes were slow.

Jordan Lee: can we confirm? If planner is missing index due to stale stats after maintenance, ANALYZE could help.

Allison Grant: I can ask DBA on-call to run analyze on request_logs partitions. But careful w/ load.

Sean Gallagher: yes, request DBA to ANALYZE targeted partitions. Keep it scoped.

Allison Grant: pinged infra-db-oncall.

Incident Bot: Status update: Mitigation in progress (throttling purge jobs). Next checkpoint in 30 minutes.

Kevin Osei: quick customer impact check: any customers in us-east complaining? This is internal retention enforcement; request logs are internal store. No API latency impact seen.

Sean Gallagher: good. Still treat as P1 for compliance posture.

Allison Grant: 30m update: ttl_lag p95 down slightly (20h -> 18.7h). backlog_size stopped growing; now slowly decreasing.

Logan Wright: nice. Once DB stable, we can step concurrency up gradually (6->10) with guardrails.

Sean Gallagher: plan:
1) hold current throttle for 2h
2) if db cpu <70% and no timeouts, increase request_logs concurrency to 10, batch 2000
3) monitor purge_failures + ttl_lag slope

Nadia Rahman: I’ll prep a staged config change template so we can bump safely.

Marcus Lin: for follow-up actions, please include:
- evidence note: retention enforcement delayed from X to Y due to infra maintenance
- control: monitoring/alerting worked, remediation applied
- preventive: post-maintenance purge ramp-up

Sean Gallagher: :thumbsup:

Allison Grant: DBA oncall responded: ran ANALYZE on the hottest partitions, no issues. Also noted maintenance reset some bloat metrics; autovacuum should settle.

Kevin Osei: action item: add post-maintenance “purge ramp” mode to purge framework (start at low concurrency for N minutes after DB maintenance window), configurable per region.

Jordan Lee: +1. Could key off maintenance event from infra scheduler or a manual toggle.

Sean Gallagher: capturing AIs:
- @Nadia Rahman: add purge ramp mode / config knob (and doc) (ENG follow-up)
- @Kevin Osei: investigate index usage / planner regression on request_logs delete query; add EXPLAIN plans to runbook
- @Allison Grant: update runbook-retention-purge-jobs w/ mitigation steps + when to throttle/pause; add dashboard annotation guidance
- @Marcus Lin: provide wording for compliance evidence note for incident

Incident Bot: Incident status changed by @Sean Gallagher: MONITORING. Mitigation applied; backlog decreasing; continuing to monitor TTL lag until within SLO.

source.messages:
IncidentBot: :rotating_light: Heads up: possible incident-adjacent issue reported via Support — some customers seeing 4xx when using quickstart templates. Suspected cause: new template telemetry header. Thread for triage + mitigation.

Christine Wong: I’m on. What’s the current symptom + blast radius?

Priya Shah: From SUP intake: “works in Postman / local, fails in corp network.” Getting 400/403 from their proxy before request reaches us. Started after they updated to the newest template. I’ll paste the snippet they shared:
```
> POST https://api.redwoodinference.com/v1/chat/completions
< 403 Forbidden
< Server: CorporateProxy
< X-Proxy-Error: disallowed_header

Blocked header: X-Redwood-Template
```

Monica Patel: That matches what we just added in quickstarts PR-115 (template-origin tagging). Header name in code: `X-Redwood-Template: rag-agent` and `X-Redwood-Template-Version: 0.4.0`.

Logan Wright: Ugh. Some enterprises run “allowlist headers only” proxies. Even if our gateway accepts it, their proxy can hard-fail.

Brian Murphy: Confirming: we allowlist those headers at our edge (PR-18421). But that only helps once traffic reaches us. If their proxy rejects unknown `X-*`, we can’t fix server-side.

Christine Wong: Do we have a quick repro? Anyone able to simulate with a strict proxy locally?

Ibrahim Khan: I can spin up a quick nginx in “underscores_in_headers off” mode but that’s different. The customer error is explicit “disallowed_header”. Some Zscaler configs do this.

Allison Grant: Also note: if we try to move it to baggage/trace headers, some networks block those too. `traceparent` is usually allowed but not guaranteed.

Monica Patel: Immediate mitigation: make telemetry headers opt-in in templates (default off). CLI can ask during init or a config flag/env.

Dev Patel: +1. Support needs a simple workaround we can hand to users today.

Priya Shah: What should we tell them right now? “Remove the header”? In templates it’s automatic.

Monica Patel: For current users: set env var to disable tagging and redeploy. We already have a config module; we can add something like:
`REDWOOD_TEMPLATE_TELEMETRY=0`
and skip adding `X-Redwood-Template*` headers.

Brian Murphy: Alternative: move identifier to `User-Agent` (most proxies permit). Example: `User-Agent: redwood-quickstart/rag-agent-python/0.4.0` appended to whatever the HTTP library sets.

Logan Wright: For KPI measurement, `User-Agent` is acceptable for a first pass (lower risk). We’d need the gateway to pass it through unchanged (it does). Analytics can parse it.

Christine Wong: Any downside? Some SDKs set UA; we’d be concatenating. But that’s fine.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: Security/privacy note: whatever we send must not include user identifiers, org names, repo names, etc. Template name + version only.

Monica Patel: Agree. We’ll keep it to fixed strings.

Owen Phillips: FYI I’m seeing the same pattern in a Solutions Eng Slack DM from a prospect. They said “proxy strips all non-standard headers; returns 4xx”. So likely not isolated.

Christine Wong: Let’s make this a mitigation plan:
1) Hotfix templates to disable custom telemetry headers by default.
2) Provide workaround: disable flag/env var; upgrade templates.
3) Follow-up: move template tagging to `User-Agent` (opt-in) + update docs.

Logan Wright: I’ll update telemetry requirements: treat custom headers as “best effort” and not required for success metrics. We can still track “template install/init” events separately.

Priya Shah: I’ll draft a troubleshooting blurb for docs + support macro:
- Symptom: 403/400 from corporate proxy
- Fix: disable template telemetry headers (`REDWOOD_TEMPLATE_TELEMETRY=0`) or upgrade to next patch
- Explanation: some proxies block unknown headers

Dev Patel: Please include a copy/paste snippet in the macro.

Monica Patel: Here’s what it’ll look like in Python template client init:
```py
if config.template_telemetry_enabled:
    headers["X-Redwood-Template"] = TEMPLATE_NAME
    headers["X-Redwood-Template-Version"] = TEMPLATE_VERSION
```
Default `template_telemetry_enabled = False`.

Brian Murphy: From platform side: nothing urgent to deploy. But we should add a note in the gateway docs that template telemetry headers are optional and may break behind strict proxies.

Allison Grant: If we do `User-Agent` tagging, please don’t put it into span attributes as-is (cardinality). Use normalized values only.

Christine Wong: Assigning owners:
- Monica: patch templates + CLI docs, PR today
- Priya: support macro + docs troubleshooting update
- Logan: adjust KPI spec / analytics expectations
- Brian: sanity-check UA pass-through + confirm no downstream sanitization

Alex Martinez: Jumping in — let’s prioritize the “default off” change. We can’t have templates fail in enterprise networks.

Monica Patel: Acknowledged. I’ll cut a patch release (0.4.1) for both Python + TS templates. We’ll add a release note: “Telemetry headers now opt-in due to proxy compatibility.”

Priya Shah: For the existing customer ticket, I’ll reply now with the workaround and note a patch is coming.

Brian Murphy: Quick test result: our edge forwards `User-Agent` as received; no rewriting. So UA tagging is viable.

Christine Wong: Great. Any indication of impact to users not behind proxies? None — only those strict networks.

IncidentBot: :memo: Status update requested. Current state: mitigation in progress (template change). No service degradation observed.

Monica Patel: PR up internally; should merge within the hour. I’ll ping Dana for TS template parity.

Christine Wong: Once merged, please post the exact env var and the release version so Support can point to it.

Monica Patel: Final names:
- Env: `REDWOOD_TEMPLATE_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false` (more explicit)
- Default: false
- Docs: “If you’re behind a corporate proxy, keep this disabled.”

Dev Patel: Works for Support.

Priya Shah: +1, clearer than `=0`.

Logan Wright: I’ll align dashboards to use UA tag if present, otherwise rely on init events + repo/CLI download telemetry.

Christine Wong: Cool. Marking as contained once patch is out + workaround communicated.

IncidentBot: :white_check_mark: Triage complete. Next check-in after template patch release is published + Support macro updated.

source.body:
PagerDuty: 🔴 SEV0 triggered: Elevated 503s + timeouts (streaming + non-streaming) in eu-west. Impact: hosted-api + dedicated shared pool. Alerts: `gateway_5xx` + `p99_latency` + `stream_disconnect_rate`.

Sean Gallagher: on it. declaring Sev0. @Rafael Mendes can you grab IC? I’ll take comms for now.

Rafael Mendes: yep IC. Starting thread checklist.

Miles Thornton: joining. also FYI incident-bot v2 beta is on for #incidents, should post context cards if we use the button.

Rafael Mendes: /incident start eu-west partial outage

Incident Bot: Incident created: INC-4821 (SEV0)
• Commander: Rafael Mendes
• Channel: #incidents
• Time: 2025-02-28 10:20 PT
Actions: [Post incident context] [Assign roles] [Open timeline]

Rafael Mendes: clicking context.

Incident Bot: Incident Context (v2 beta) for `hosted-api` • region=`eu-west` • window=`last 30m`

[Card: Saturation]
• GPU utilization (p95): 98–100% (threshold 92%)
• Scheduler queue depth: 4.2k (threshold 1.0k)
• Backpressure/admission control: ON (dropping/deferring requests)
• KV cache pressure: 0.93 (threshold 0.85)
• Batch fill: high (continuous batching at max)
Deep links:
• Saturation Overview: https://console.redwoodinference.com/obs/saturation?region=eu-west&service=hosted-api&from=-30m
• Scheduler/Queue: https://console.redwoodinference.com/obs/scheduler?region=eu-west&service=hosted-api&from=-30m

[Card: Recent deploys]
• `scheduler` deployed 18m ago: image `sha: 7c1f2d9` rollout=100% eu-west, canary=PASS (eu-central)
  – PR: https://github.com/redwood/redwood/pull/29120 (add scheduler saturation metrics)
• `router` deployed 24m ago: image `sha: 3a84b11` rollout=50% eu-west (canary still RUNNING)
  – PR: https://github.com/redwood/redwood/pull/29104 (export deploy metadata)
Deep links:
• Deploy timeline: https://console.redwoodinference.com/deploys?region=eu-west&service=router&from=-2h

[Card: Config changes (tenant-safe)]
• Routing policy diff detected (redacted): 22m ago
  – `fallback_policy`: changed (details redacted)
  – `capacity_tier`: changed (details redacted)
  – `max_concurrency`: changed (details redacted)
Actions:
• [View redacted diff] • [Request elevated diff access]

• Queue depth: 2.1k -> 540
• Admission denies: 6% -> 1%
• 5xx rate: 0.9% -> 0.2%

Aisha Bello: looks like we’re basically back. burn rate should recover soon.

Rafael Mendes: moving to monitoring. if stable 10m, we can resolve Sev0 and keep an eye on spillover rollback.

Mina Haddad: reducing spillover cap from 30% -> 10% now.

Jacob Stein: no new errors in logs. queue stable sub-600.

Logan Wright: telemetry looks healthy again. also confirming no alert floods.

Rafael Mendes: ok calling incident mitigated. keep spillover 10% for 10m, then off.

Sean Gallagher: status page updated.

Rafael Mendes: resolved. Next steps:
1) Postmortem: why router canary didn’t block rollout + why eu-west headroom so tight.
2) Add automated “deploy correlation” callout to incident template (bot already does it, but we should standardize).
3) Ensure spillover + token cap playbook is documented.

Miles Thornton: I’ll clip the bot v2 context output into the retro doc. also: the auto-refresh summaries were super useful.

Aisha Bello: +1. also alert tuning: we paged after 5xx already high; maybe add earlier saturation alert on queue depth.

Rafael Mendes: assigning owners in follow-up. thanks all.

Incident Bot: Incident INC-4821 marked Resolved by @Rafael Mendes. Timeline export: https://console.redwoodinference.com/incidents/INC-4821/timeline

source.body:
Incident Bot: :rotating_light: *INCIDENT* (beta) - "rollout split mismatch" reported by Northstar in us-east. Expected canary=10%, observed ~35-45% over last 10m. Route: `hosted/chat/northstar-support-prod`. Rollout: `rlt_7f2c`.

Sean Gallagher: seeing the same in our cohort dashboard. us-west looks ~10% +/- noise, eu-central ~9-11%. us-east is way off.

Aisha Bello: pulling quick numbers from metrics.
`sum(rate(rw_infer_requests_total{route="hosted/chat/northstar-support-prod",region="us-east",cohort="canary"}[5m])) / sum(rate(rw_infer_requests_total{route="hosted/chat/northstar-support-prod",region="us-east"}[5m]))`
= 0.39 right now.

Rafael Mendes: ack. is this impacting latency/error? customer-facing impact?

Sean Gallagher: error rate stable, but this is a safety issue if the new model is bad. We should treat as sev2-ish for beta. Let's contain.

Mina Haddad: on it. This is traffic-splitting enforcement in data plane. If it’s only us-east, could be hash input mismatch on that cluster or a config propagation issue.

Ravi Menon: +1. First check: are we using the same `cohort_key` in us-east? It’s supposed to be `tenant_id + route_id + stable_user_key` with fallback to request_id if missing.

Emily Zhao: I can check the us-east router build / config digest. Might be running slightly different commit?

Vikram Singh: grabbing router logs for rlt_7f2c in us-east. any recent deploy?

Aisha Bello: latency p95 is slightly up on canary vs baseline (not huge): baseline 820ms, canary 910ms in us-east over 15m. errors flat.

Rafael Mendes: mitigation: can we pause rollout / force canary=0 in us-east only?

Hannah Rivera: orchestrator supports pause globally; region-scoped override is behind flag but I think Smart Routing has region weights per rollout? Mina confirm.

Mina Haddad: yes, routing primitive has per-region enforcement weights. Quickest is set canary weight to 0 in us-east (keep 10% elsewhere). Alternatively pause the rollout which sets all regions to baseline.

Sean Gallagher: do region 0% if safe, to minimize disruption. But only if we’re confident it won’t wedge state.

Elena Popov: I can push a config patch to set `regions.us-east.canary_pct=0` for rlt_7f2c. Will take ~2-3 mins to propagate.

Dylan Brooks: FYI Console shows rollout at step 10% with soak timer running. If we do region override, Console won’t reflect it yet (known gap). Marissa: heads up.

Marissa Cole: yep, understood. For beta customer safety > UI accuracy. We’ll message Northstar through support channel.

Rafael Mendes: +1. Let’s do it.

Elena Popov: applying patch now.

Incident Bot: Change requested by @Elena Popov: "Set rlt_7f2c canary_pct=0 in us-east" (config pipeline: `routing-prod`).

Vikram Singh: while that propagates, here’s log sample from us-east router:
```
rollout_id=rlt_7f2c route=hosted/chat/northstar-support-prod region=us-east
cohort_key_source=header:x-rw-user-key
cohort_key_present=false
fallback_source=ip
hash_seed=route_id_only
assigned=canary
```
That looks suspicious: `hash_seed=route_id_only`??? should include tenant_id at least.

Ravi Menon: whoa. That `hash_seed` field indicates the "v0" cohorting code path. That would skew based on NAT IP distribution too.

Emily Zhao: checking build stamp in us-east: it’s on `router@1.38.2` but others are `1.38.3`. us-east missed the rollout during last deploy window.

Mina Haddad: ok so we have version skew. 1.38.2 uses legacy cohorting fallback behavior (route-only seed when user key missing). That can absolutely produce mismatch.

Sean Gallagher: root cause candidate: us-east routers running old version, using IP fallback and route-only seed => canary assignment biased.

Aisha Bello: config patch has landed. Observed canary pct in us-east is dropping: 0.22 -> 0.11 over last 2m.

Elena Popov: confirming propagation complete on all us-east POPs. should converge to ~0 within a few mins.

Rafael Mendes: good containment. Next action: get us-east routers to 1.38.3 ASAP.

Emily Zhao: I can kick a redeploy for us-east only. Need release channel sign-off since we’re mid-beta.

Sean Gallagher: do it. This is safety-related.

Incident Bot: :information_source: Deployment requested: `router` to `us-east` version `1.38.3` (requested by @Emily Zhao).

Hannah Rivera: meanwhile I’ll pause the orchestrator step timer? If Console is still counting soak at 10% but us-east is effectively 0, it may confuse auto-stop logic.

Aisha Bello: auto-stop checks are per-region AND global? Right now stop-condition evaluator is using global aggregates with region tag optional. For this rollout we set `regions=["us-east","us-west","eu-central"]` but evaluator is looking at all regions combined.

Sean Gallagher: so with us-east at 0 canary, global canary traffic decreases, which could extend time to significance. That’s ok short-term.

Mina Haddad: agreed; don’t pause orchestrator unless necessary. Just note in activity log / internal timeline.

Marissa Cole: I’ll DM support to tell Northstar “we temporarily disabled canary in us-east while we fix routing layer, no action needed.”

Dylan Brooks: can we add a banner in Console? probably not in time, but I’ll log a follow-up issue.

Vikram Singh: more logs from us-east show `cohort_key_present=false` a lot. Northstar doesn’t send x-rw-user-key consistently; they rely on our default. Our default should use `x-request-id` but it looks like 1.38.2 uses IP first. That’s the skew.

Ravi Menon: yep 1.38.3 changed fallback precedence: `x-rw-user-key` > `x-rw-session-id` > `request_id` (stable for retries) > last resort IP. Also fixes hash seed to include tenant+route.

Emily Zhao: redeploy started. ETA 8-10 mins.

Incident Bot: DeployBot: `router us-east` rolling update in progress (10% -> 50% -> 100%).

Aisha Bello: metrics update: us-east canary pct now 0.01 (effectively off). us-west and eu-central remain ~0.10.

Rafael Mendes: ok. once redeploy done, we can remove region override and go back to intended 10% globally.

Sean Gallagher: careful: remove override after confirming cohorting logs show expected hash inputs and observed split matches.

Mina Haddad: +1. Let’s verify on one POP first.

Incident Bot: DeployBot: `router us-east` update complete. version=1.38.3

Emily Zhao: us-east now on 1.38.3 across the fleet.

Vikram Singh: checking new logs:
```
rollout_id=rlt_7f2c region=us-east
cohort_key_source=header:x-rw-user-key
cohort_key_present=false
fallback_source=request_id
hash_seed=tenant+route
assigned=baseline
```
Looks good.

Aisha Bello: if we keep us-east override at 0, split stays 0 there. If we revert override, should stabilize at ~10%.

Elena Popov: I can revert the override now.

Sean Gallagher: do it, but watch for a few minutes. also note we might still see variance if they don’t send stable user key and request_ids are random per request (not per user). That’s ok though; expected distribution still correct.

Elena Popov: reverting us-east override back to configured canary_pct=10.

Incident Bot: Change requested by @Elena Popov: "Revert rlt_7f2c us-east canary_pct to 10".

Aisha Bello: watching.

Rafael Mendes: for follow-up: we need an alert for region version skew on router + a guard in control plane to block rollouts if region not on min version.

Mina Haddad: yes. Also add a metric: expected_pct vs observed_pct with threshold alert, per region.

Dylan Brooks: and Console should display a warning when region override / kill switch applied, even if internal-only.

Marissa Cole: +1. also for beta, we should recommend customers send `x-rw-user-key` or `x-rw-session-id` for more stable cohorting. Docs note?

Sean Gallagher: Aisha, what’s split now?

Aisha Bello: us-east canary pct is climbing to ~0.08 over 5m, trending to 0.10. Looks within noise now.

Sean Gallagher: cool. marking contained / resolved. keeping thread open for action items.

Rafael Mendes: Action items:
1) Prevent region version skew (release process / deploy guard) - @Emily Zhao
2) Control plane check: require router_min_version for rollouts + fail closed if region stale - @Mina Haddad @Ravi Menon
3) Add observed vs expected split alert per route/region/rollout - @Aisha Bello
4) Update runbook "bad split" with version-skew diagnostic and emergency region override steps - @Rafael Mendes
5) Docs: recommend stable cohort keys headers - @Marissa Cole @Wei Chen

Mina Haddad: ack on #2. Will file ENG issue and link to the routing PRs.

Ravi Menon: ack. also we should backport the fallback precedence fix into a patch release and enforce it as minimum.

Emily Zhao: ack #1. I’ll work with releases to ensure us-east can’t drift (and add a monitor on deploy parity).

Aisha Bello: ack #3. I can start with a dashboard panel + simple alert: |observed-expected| > 0.15 for 10m.

Sean Gallagher: closing as resolved once Northstar confirms. :thumbsup:

Marissa Cole: support says Northstar sees expected behavior again and is unblocked.

Incident Bot: Status updated: *RESOLVED* (root cause: router version skew in us-east using legacy cohorting fallback; mitigation: region canary disabled + router redeploy).
