Mina Haddad: yes. Also add a metric: expected_pct vs observed_pct with threshold alert, per region.

Dylan Brooks: and Console should display a warning when region override / kill switch applied, even if internal-only.

Marissa Cole: +1. also for beta, we should recommend customers send `x-rw-user-key` or `x-rw-session-id` for more stable cohorting. Docs note?

Sean Gallagher: Aisha, what’s split now?

Aisha Bello: us-east canary pct is climbing to ~0.08 over 5m, trending to 0.10. Looks within noise now.

Sean Gallagher: cool. marking contained / resolved. keeping thread open for action items.

Rafael Mendes: Action items:
1) Prevent region version skew (release process / deploy guard) - @Emily Zhao
2) Control plane check: require router_min_version for rollouts + fail closed if region stale - @Mina Haddad @Ravi Menon
3) Add observed vs expected split alert per route/region/rollout - @Aisha Bello
4) Update runbook "bad split" with version-skew diagnostic and emergency region override steps - @Rafael Mendes
5) Docs: recommend stable cohort keys headers - @Marissa Cole @Wei Chen

Mina Haddad: ack on #2. Will file ENG issue and link to the routing PRs.

Ravi Menon: ack. also we should backport the fallback precedence fix into a patch release and enforce it as minimum.

Emily Zhao: ack #1. I’ll work with releases to ensure us-east can’t drift (and add a monitor on deploy parity).

Aisha Bello: ack #3. I can start with a dashboard panel + simple alert: |observed-expected| > 0.15 for 10m.

Sean Gallagher: closing as resolved once Northstar confirms. :thumbsup:

Marissa Cole: support says Northstar sees expected behavior again and is unblocked.

Incident Bot: Status updated: *RESOLVED* (root cause: router version skew in us-east using legacy cohorting fallback; mitigation: region canary disabled + router redeploy).

source.text:
Incident Bot: :rotating_light: SEV1 declared by @Rafael Mendes
Service: hosted-api
Symptom: elevated 503s (5xx) in prod
Regions: us-east-1, eu-west-1
Start: now
Runbook: https://console.redwoodinference.com/runbooks/hosted-api-5xx

Rafael Mendes: seeing 503 spike on hosted-api /v1/chat completions. Started ~2-3 mins ago. Anyone aware of changes?

Sean Gallagher: I’m on. Confirm impact? user reports / pager?

Owen Phillips: Support is getting pings from 2 customers (Acme + smaller). “all requests failing intermittently”

Aisha Bello: paging says 5xx SLO burn is fast. looks like not just one tenant.

Jacob Stein: I’m pulling dashboards. 503s mostly from router layer? errors show “upstream unavailable” and “admission control reject”.

Miles Thornton: incident-bot context is thin here. no deploy card + no config card yet :/ anyone know last deploy?

Deploy Bot: Recent deploys (prod)
- hosted-api: no deploy in last 60m
- router: no deploy in last 60m
- control-plane: no deploy in last 60m

Rafael Mendes: ok so not a deploy. smells like config push.

Ethan Park: there was a control-plane config push earlier today but not sure timing. what’s the exact start time?

Aisha Bello: graph shows jump at ~17:18 UTC. pretty sharp.

Mina Haddad: 503 + admission control could be routing policy change. any recent route weights / fallback toggles?

Jacob Stein: checking router logs… seeing more “policy denied: quota guardrail” and “route unavailable: pinned model” errors.

Rafael Mendes: can someone diff the config around 17:18? what changed?

Benji Okafor: I can try, but config-service diff endpoint isn’t in prod for bot yet. I can do manual compare via config-service UI if I can find the revision ids.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: reminder: prod config diff requires break-glass / audited access for tenant-specific. For global flags / routing policy it should be ok.

Miles Thornton: this is exactly what the bot v2 “config diff w/ redaction” card is supposed to solve. right now we’re guessing.

Rafael Mendes: +1 but we need mitigation now. can we identify last global config revision?

Benji Okafor: grabbing config-service audit log… last write around 17:16 UTC by automation user `rollout-controller`. looks like feature flag update.

Sean Gallagher: do we know which flag? this is the painful part.

Benji Okafor: I can query the config snapshot API for “routing.*” keys and compare, but it’s slow + lots of fields.

Logan Wright: careful with heavy queries right now; telemetry already stressed. If you need a targeted check, tell me which keyspace.

Jacob Stein: I’m seeing increased rejects specifically for model “rwqwen-32b-v3” pinned routes. Did something flip default pinning?

Mina Haddad: there’s a flag `routing.enforce_model_pins_strict` that can cause hard-fail when pinned variant unavailable. if that turned on globally, could cause 503s.

Rafael Mendes: can we confirm if that flag changed?

Benji Okafor: looking… found it. `routing.enforce_model_pins_strict` appears to be ON in current snapshot. I don’t know what it was 10 mins ago.

Sean Gallagher: ugh.

Miles Thornton: any place we can see the last value without full diff?

Benji Okafor: config-service UI has “history” per key but it’s clunky. trying now.

Nadia Rahman: GPU/queue looks normal-ish. doesn’t look like capacity saturation causing the 503s. more like policy/routing.

Aisha Bello: agree. latency isn’t climbing first; error rate jumps instantly. config regression pattern.

Benji Okafor: ok, per-key history says `routing.enforce_model_pins_strict` flipped from false -> true at 17:16:42 UTC.

Rafael Mendes: that matches. roll it back.

Ethan Park: I can revert that flag via control-plane if we’re confident. any risk?

Mina Haddad: strict pins should be for small cohort only. global enable will hard-fail when pinned model isn’t in region or pool. revert to false.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: please use the standard change path (audited). If you need break-glass, call it out in the incident log.

Ethan Park: proceeding with revert (audited). setting `routing.enforce_model_pins_strict=false` globally. ETA 2-3 mins propagate.

Deploy Bot: Control-plane change requested by @Ethan Park (request id cpchg_8f13a)

Jacob Stein: watching metrics… 503s starting to drop in us-east.

Aisha Bello: eu-west still elevated but trending down.

Rafael Mendes: good. keep an eye on partial propagation.

Benji Okafor: propagation is staged; some router pods cache config for ~60s. should settle soon.

Miles Thornton: :facepalm: We lost ~15 mins to “what changed?” We need config diff surfaced in #incidents automatically (with safe redaction).

Sean Gallagher: +1. capture in follow-ups.

Owen Phillips: Support asking what to tell customers. any summary?

Rafael Mendes: tell them: “misconfigured routing flag caused increased 503s, reverted; recovery in progress.” no data loss.

Jacob Stein: 5xx back to baseline now. last few mins look clean.

Incident Bot: Update requested: please provide current status + next update ETA.

Rafael Mendes: Status: mitigated. 503s back to baseline after reverting `routing.enforce_model_pins_strict` (accidentally enabled globally). Monitoring for full propagation. Next update: 10 mins.

Aisha Bello: marking SLO burn stopped at ~17:34 UTC.

Logan Wright: telemetry stable. no signs of saturation. agree config-only.

Ethan Park: root cause likely rollout-controller pushing wrong cohort rules. I’ll pull the change request details.

Mina Haddad: also check if any other routing flags changed in same push. might be more than one key.

Benji Okafor: yeah, the UI shows a bundle update but I can’t easily see the bundle diff without exporting. another reason we need the diff endpoint.

Miles Thornton: action item: bot v2 config diff card (redacted) + per-key history shortcuts + deep link to config revision. I’ll file against ENG-5422 / permission scopes.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: please include: least privilege scopes + audit event for reads. and a “degraded mode” when config-service is down (link + manual steps).

Sean Gallagher: +1. also add runbook snippet: “if sudden 5xx w/o deploy, check config changes first.”

Rafael Mendes: closing the loop: incident mitigated, monitoring for 15 mins then resolve.

Incident Bot: :white_check_mark: SEV1 resolved by @Rafael Mendes
Duration: ~33m
Summary: 503s after global routing feature flag flip; reverted.
Follow-ups: please create tickets + link here.

Miles Thornton: ticket created: https://linear.app/redwood/issue/ENG-5422 (Config diff fetcher + redaction). also adding note: “this incident took 15m to confirm single flag change due to lack of diff.”

Ethan Park: I’ll open a separate follow-up for rollout-controller guardrails (prevent global enable of strict pins) and add to postmortem.

Rafael Mendes: thanks all. :thumbsup:

source.content:
Incident Bot: 🚨 SEV1 declared: Dedicated customer timeouts (suspected TP hang). Commander: Rafael Mendes. Channel: #incidents. Impact: customer-facing request timeouts / hangs. Start: 1740165551

PagerDuty: Triggered: "Dedicated p99 latency > 20s" for account ACME-AI (dedicated cluster: ded-acme-a100-usw2). Assigned to Rafael Mendes.

Rafael Mendes: Taking IC. Can someone grab customer impact + comms? @Lauren Bishop @Rachel Kim

Lauren Bishop: On it. I’ll ping CS + update status page language for Dedicated (no public post yet). Do we have confirmation it’s isolated to Acme?

Aisha Bello: Seeing elevated timeouts in ded-acme-a100-usw2 only. Hosted looks normal. Graph: request_timeout_rate spiking to 18% last 10m, p99 ~42s.

Sean Gallagher: Looping in runtime. @Noah Patel @Hiro Tanaka @Sofia Ivanova — suspected TP stall again. We need mitigation now + confirm signature.

Noah Patel: Here. Do we have stall signals / GPU metrics? Any NCCL errors in logs?

Tomáš Novák: Pulling logs from affected replicas. Initial glance: requests stuck in decode, no explicit CUDA error. Threads show long gaps between token emits.

Owen Phillips: Support has two tickets opened from Acme (timeouts + hanging streams). They’re asking for ETA + mitigation. What can we safely recommend?

Rafael Mendes: Immediate objective: stop the bleeding. Proposed mitigations:
1) Drain/replace suspicious replicas
2) Reduce concurrency cap on TP deployments
3) If needed, disable CUDA graphs / enable extra NCCL debug during canary window

Aisha Bello: Correlation: timeouts cluster around replicas tp=8, model=rwi-llama-70b, runtime=1.18.3. Replica IDs with highest timeout count: rr-31, rr-34.

Jacob Stein: Trace sampling shows spans missing “collective_end” event; last tag is `tp.step=decode.iter` then nothing. Smells like collective wait / scheduler starvation.

Rafael Mendes: @Tomáš Novák can you drain rr-31/rr-34 and confirm if timeouts fall?

Tomáš Novák: Yep. Draining now via control plane.

Tomáš Novák: Command:
`rdwctl replicas drain --cluster ded-acme-a100-usw2 --replicas rr-31,rr-34 --reason "suspected tp hang"`

PagerDuty: Note added by Tomáš Novák: "Draining two high-timeout TP replicas rr-31 rr-34"

Grace Kim: If this is the same stall as last week, we saw it more when prefill mix was heavy + streaming cancels. Any recent traffic shape change?

Rachel Kim: Acme says they just rolled out a new feature that increases max context (some requests up to 24k), plus more streaming.

Noah Patel: That lines up with our mixed prefill/decode stall signature. Can we clamp max concurrent requests per replica as a short-term fix?

Aisha Bello: Current per-replica concurrency limit is 48 (Acme asked for higher throughput last month). We can drop to 24 and see.

Rafael Mendes: Do it. Let’s reduce concurrency cap for their deployment. @Aisha Bello can you apply the runtime config override?

Aisha Bello: Applying config override in Dedicated policy now.
`dedicated_override: max_inflight_requests_per_replica=24 (was 48)`

Lauren Bishop: I’ll message Acme: "We’re mitigating by draining affected replicas + temporarily reducing concurrency to stabilize while we investigate." Need an internal ETA for next update.

Sean Gallagher: +1. Also please ask them to provide example request IDs + whether client is cancelling streams aggressively.

Owen Phillips: Will do. Also they asked whether they should retry on 5xx/timeouts. What’s guidance?

Noah Patel: For now yes: retry idempotent calls with jitter/backoff. If it’s a hang, we’ll surface timeouts; retries should route to healthy replicas after drains.

Tomáš Novák: Drain complete. New replicas rr-51/rr-52 coming up.

Aisha Bello: Seeing timeout rate down already: from ~18% to ~6% over last 5m. p99 improving to ~19s (still high).

Hiro Tanaka: Any GPU-side evidence of wedges? nvidia-smi shows persistent 100% util but no progress? If we can grab a `nsys` or at least NCCL debug snippet from one hung replica before drain next time.

Tomáš Novák: We didn’t capture this time. We can enable extra logging briefly if we expect recurrence.

Rafael Mendes: Let’s prep a controlled debug window. @Caleb Johnson can we safely bump NCCL logging for this cluster for ~30m without exploding log volume?

Caleb Johnson: Yes, but recommend `NCCL_DEBUG=WARN` + selective `NCCL_DEBUG_SUBSYS=COLL` only. Also route to dedicated log sink w/ sampling.

Sean Gallagher: If we do that, we need security sign-off? Probably ok for Dedicated internal logs; keep it short.

Rafael Mendes: Do it with guardrails. @Caleb Johnson coordinate with @Tomáš Novák.

Sofia Ivanova: If scheduler starvation is involved, we should check queue depth + fairness counters. Do we have scheduler metrics by rank?

Jacob Stein: Not per-rank in prod yet. We have coarse `scheduler.queue_depth` and `prefill_to_decode_ratio`. For this incident, queue depth spiked to ~2k, ratio 4.8:1 prefill-heavy.

Grace Kim: That’s the pattern where decode collectives can be starved if prefill keeps landing in front.

Noah Patel: Acknowledge. This aligns with ENG-4824 / scheduler guardrail. For now, mitigation is concurrency cap + possibly reduce max batch tokens.

Aisha Bello: We can also reduce `max_batch_prefill_tokens` from 1.2M -> 700k for this deployment. Would trade throughput for latency/stability.

Rafael Mendes: OK, make that change (temporary) if Acme can tolerate. @Lauren Bishop please set expectation.

Lauren Bishop: Will frame as “temporary throttles to maintain availability.”

Rachel Kim: Acme prioritizes uptime over throughput today. They’ll accept some rate limiting.

Owen Phillips: Support script: recommend client-side max concurrency reduction too? They’re pushing ~900 rps spikes.

Sean Gallagher: Yes, ask them to cap burst concurrency until we finish canary.

Aisha Bello: Applied prefill token cap override:
`max_batch_prefill_tokens=700000` (was 1200000)

Aisha Bello: Metrics: timeout rate now ~1.5%, p99 9.8s and falling. GPU util normalized.

Rafael Mendes: Good. Keep monitoring 30m. Next: confirm root cause and handoff to runtime owners with artifacts.

Noah Patel: For handoff: we need request IDs + any patterns (client disconnects, streaming cancels). Also capture one hang with debug enabled.

Tomáš Novák: Debug window set up (30m):

Incident Bot: :rotating_light: INC-2197 opened by Amara Diallo
Severity: SEV-2
Service: api-gateway
Summary: Possible authz regression / missing permission check on streaming route
Primary suspect: `POST /v1/chat/completions:stream` (SSE)

Amara Diallo: flagging asap. during routine check + a customer report, looks like our streaming chat endpoint might be bypassing authz middleware in gateway. non-streaming path returns expected 403 when missing permission; streaming returns 200 + begins streaming tokens.

Marcus Lin: :eyes: confirm whether this is actual bypass (no authz evaluation) vs authz evaluated but decision not enforced due to streaming flush?

Amara Diallo: looks like bypass. i can reproduce in staging and prod.
Steps:
1) Create API key scoped to org but w/ no `inference.generate` (or equivalent) permission.
2) Call non-streaming `POST /v1/chat/completions` -> 403 `permission_denied` (expected)
3) Call streaming `POST /v1/chat/completions?stream=true` OR `POST /v1/chat/completions:stream` (depending on client) -> 200 and event stream starts.

Amara Diallo: quick curl (sanitized):
```bash
curl -N https://api.redwood.example/v1/chat/completions:stream \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer rw_test_***" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model":"redwood/llama-3.1-70b","stream":true,"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"hi"}]}'
```
Non-streaming equivalent returns 403.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: this sounds like the route is hitting the legacy streaming handler that predates the new deny-by-default middleware. do we have two mux paths?

Jordan Lee: if it bypasses gateway, do we still have service-side checks? control plane vs inference runtime? (inference service generally trusts gateway today for hosted)

Ethan Park: inference runtime path for hosted is gateway -> inference-router -> model worker. iirc permission check is in gateway only for inference requests. (control plane endpoints have service interceptors, but not here.)

Marcus Lin: ok treat as real authz bypass until proven otherwise.
Immediate questions:
- scope: hosted only? dedicated/private too?
- can an unprivileged *user* in org call streaming and get inference output? (seems yes)
- does it allow cross-org? likely no if org routing still uses API key org, but still violates least-privilege.

Amara Diallo: scope seems hosted gateway path. dedicated/private might be different ingress but we share the same gateway code in dedicated. private installer has different config; unsure.

Priya Natarajan: SEV-2 makes sense. we need to stop the bleeding.
Options:
1) quick disable streaming route behind feature flag (return 503/403)
2) patch middleware chain to include streaming routes
3) add service-side fallback check (longer)

Connor O'Brien: release-wise we can hotfix gateway today if we keep it minimal. if we disable streaming, we need comms + watch support volume.

Brian Murphy: customer impact: streaming is heavily used by SDK defaults. disabling would be loud. better to patch quickly.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: i’m digging in code. there’s a `StreamProxyHandler` registered under `/v1/chat/completions:stream` using the old router, not wrapped with `AuthzMiddleware`. the non-streaming handler is on the new router stack. :facepalm:

Marcus Lin: do we have any telemetry showing authz eval count mismatch between streaming vs non-streaming? (requests without `authz.decision` fields?)

Amara Diallo: checking logs now. for non-streaming we emit `authz.decision=deny` with `reason_code=missing_permission`.
for streaming requests, no `authz.*` fields at all in gateway logs. so yep.

Leila Farouk: for repro: do you want me to add an integration test once fix is in? we should gate on “no streaming route without permission annotation” in CI.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: current registry maps non-streaming chat completions to `inference.chat.generate`. streaming should match.

- Amara + Kaitlyn patch gateway route registration to ensure streaming handler is wrapped by same authz middleware.

Fiona McCarthy: (popped in via @mention) do we need a SecOps alert for potential abuse? if this is a bypass, we should add a temporary detection query (streaming inference requests without authz context) + watch.
